Original_Search_Title: Prices of Pepper, Onions, Tomatoes Stable in Lagos Markets
Headline: Nigeria: Prices of Pepper, Onions, Tomatoes Stable in Lagos Markets
Body_Text:
The prices of pepper, onions and tomatoes have remained stable with just a few days to Christmas in most markets in Lagos, the News Agency of Nigeria (NAN) reports.
A survey by NAN, however, revealed that the prices of live stock, some food items and imported used clothes had increased by at least 35 per cent.

Original_Search_Title: Imo - Anxiety Over 2015 Polls, Austerity
Headline: Nigeria: Imo - Anxiety Over 2015 Polls, Austerity
Body_Text:
Owerri — In Imo State, the burden of the 2015 polls have not only become a common phenomenon, but also a visible nightmare to the masses, who have become apprehensive over what may become the end of the exercise. Already, the governorship race has become the centre of attraction by the political class and keen observers of the unfolding political development in the state.
This is because, the political atmosphere has been saturated with election campaigns by politicians seeking one elective position or the other with ceaseless radio and television jingles, slogans and promos, while imposing bill boards, posters and leaflets are conspicuously visible in all strategic points all bearing messages of liberation and salvation for the poor, the needy and the downtrodden.

Original_Search_Title: Off to Mabamba for the Shoebill Stork, We Go
Headline: Uganda: Off to Mabamba for the Shoebill Stork, We Go
Body_Text:
No sooner had the car engine started at the Uganda museum on November 29 than Ronald Sekiziyivu's voice came through: "If you see a bird, shout it out so we can record it."
Two first-time birders in the minibus exchanged puzzled looks; beyond the ordinary chicken and duck - oh, and the kalooli (marabou stork; although the first two do not count as birds in birding) - they were not sure they could identify any bird even if it hit them smack in the head, one of them noted smugly.

Original_Search_Title: 30th Convocation - Ibadan Polytechnic Graduates 4,977
Headline: Nigeria: 30th Convocation - Ibadan Polytechnic Graduates 4,977
Body_Text:
The Polytechnic, Ibadan on Friday graduated 4,977 students for its 2013/2014 academic session
3,675 graduated with National Diploma(ND) while 1,302 graduated with Higher National Diploma(HND).

Original_Search_Title: Ibadan Polytechnic Graduates 4,977
Headline: Nigeria: Ibadan Polytechnic Graduates 4,977
Body_Text:
THE Polytechnic, Ibadan on Friday graduated 4,977 students for its 2013/2014 academic session at its 30th convocation ceremony.
The News Agency of Nigeria (NAN) reports that 3,675 graduated with National Diploma(ND) while 1,302 graduated with Higher National Diploma(HND).

Original_Search_Title: In Benin, Ibadan, Port Harcourt, Ebola Still Haunts Bush Meat Sellers
Headline: Nigeria: In Benin, Ibadan, Port Harcourt, Ebola Still Haunts Bush Meat Sellers
Body_Text:
Benin — Despite the certification of Nigeria by the World Health Organisation (WHO) as Ebola Virus Disease (EVD) free country, consumers of bush meat have not yet returned to the market to buy their delicacy, bush meat.
Following the lack of patronage of the bush-meat market, those engaged in hunting for the animals and the marketers are lamenting the drop in their economic fortunes.

Original_Search_Title: Minority Want Cocaine Saga Probed
Headline: Ghana: Minority Want Cocaine Saga Probed
Body_Text:
On the 16th November, 2014, many Ghanaians across the world got wind of a major arrest and scandal at the Heathrow Airport in the United Kingdom regarding the carrying of narcotics weighing 12 kilograms, worth about £3.5million or $5million through the Kotoka International Airport (K.I.A).
The Narcotics Control Board (NACOB) has confirmed that one young lady was arrested on the 10th November, 2014. Many days after the arrest, the Government of Ghana was forced to admit the scandal. The lady has many aliases, many passports and presumably used the VVIP lounge at the KIA to board her fateful flight out of this country.

Original_Search_Title: Port Harcourt - 300 Benefit From NDE Special Empowerment Scheme
Headline: Nigeria: Port Harcourt - 300 Benefit From NDE Special Empowerment Scheme
Body_Text:
Three hundred people have benefited from the Special Empowerment Scheme of the National Directorate of Employment (NDE).
The scheme is designed to alleviate poverty among the populace.

Original_Search_Title: Rivers - What Shell Can't Do in Dubai, Texas, It's Doing in Rumuekpe
Headline: Nigeria: Rivers - What Shell Can't Do in Dubai, Texas, It's Doing in Rumuekpe
Body_Text:
Like the gory tale of other oil producing communities in the Niger Delta coastal belt, the pathetic story of Rumuekpe community in the Emohua Local Government area of Rivers State is rather mind rending. Located deep down the mangrove settlement off the busy East-West road, Rumuekpe is comfortably seated amid well-orchestrated green vegetation.
With about eight villages and a population of more than 5,000 (verifiable) people, in that community one can dare say, without sounding immodest, that Rumuekpe is one of the richest communities in Rivers State.

Original_Search_Title: Dickson Submits N320.6 Billion 2015 Budget to Assembly
Headline: Nigeria: Dickson Submits N320.6 Billion 2015 Budget to Assembly
Body_Text:
Governor Seriake Dickson of Bayelsa State on Monday presented the state 2015 budget estimate of N320,621,216.641 to the state House of Assembly for consideration.
Presenting the budget tagged, "Budget of Completion," Governor Dickson explained that, the amount comprises N175, 776, 828, 076 or 54 per cent for recurrent expenditure, while N144, 844, 388, 565 or 45.2 per cent representing capital expenditure.

Original_Search_Title: Ebola Shutdown Brings New Fears of Rape and Teenage Pregnancy
Headline: Sierra Leone: Ebola Shutdown Brings New Fears of Rape and Teenage Pregnancy
Body_Text:
Mabel, 15, was always the kind of girl who did well in class. She enjoyed maths, was good with numbers and aspired to be an accountant.
But when the Ebola virus ripped through her community of Moyamba in southern Sierra Leone, killing family, friends and other children, Mabel's school was closed due to fears that large gatherings would exacerbate the spread of the virus.

Original_Search_Title: 'Big Sister' Xolile Tshabalala - Nigeria in Her Future Plans
Headline: Nigeria: 'Big Sister' Xolile Tshabalala - Nigeria in Her Future Plans
Body_Text:
LEADING South African born stage and screen actress Xolile Tshabalala, is in love with Nigeria. The pretty, amiable and down-to-earth screen personality of vast credits and an Ambassador of the 2014 edition of Africa International Film Festival (AFRIFF), says she is so in love with Nigeria that she is already considering a proposal to settle in Nigeria when she is "much older". The star of some of South Africa's critically acclaimed productions including the soapie Generations, Soul City, Isidingo and Fallen, Xolile who has received a number of acting awards including the 2004 People Magazine Crystal Award for best soapie, disclosed in this interview conducted at the lobby of the Tinapa Lakeside Hotel in Calabar, Cross Rivers State, that the 2014 AFRIFF has confirmed that filmmakers in Africa must team up to find a voice for African people in the film industry. Excerpts:
You have been here so many times. What is your impression about Nigeria?

Original_Search_Title: We Have Governance Without Ideas, Culture Without Reading, Says Amuta
Headline: Nigeria: We Have Governance Without Ideas, Culture Without Reading, Says Amuta
Body_Text:
MANY serious minded watchers of Nigeria's political space have long suspected that governance in the country has been on auto-pilot since 1999 when the current democratic dispensation came into being. Perhaps, one individual who has put Nigeria's governance anomaly into proper perspective is erudite scholar, journalist, communication expert, author and businessman, Dr. Chidi Amuta. He also has a new book titled, Writing the Wrong, which is due for launch next week Tuesday, November 18, 2014 at Oduduwa Hall, Southern Sun Hotel, Alfred Rewane Road, Ikoyi, Lagos. It's a product of his journalistic experience, his professional memoir, which he hopes would help the advancement of ideas in the country's lack-lustre public space.
Like many like-minded Nigerians who continue to bemoan poor governance system that has left the country drifting on uncertain waters, Amuta is appalled at the paucity of ideas among those in political offices across the country. A small indication of this is the poor attitude of leaders to the ever-deteriorating performance of students in national examinations, a parlous testimony to the premium the country places on education and development.

Original_Search_Title: Seychelles' Own Mcdonald's Style Fast Food Joint 'Butcher's Grill' to Expand
Headline: Seychelles' Own Mcdonald's Style Fast Food Joint 'Butcher's Grill' to Expand
Body_Text:
Victoria — The Indian Ocean archipelago of the Seychelles is free of any global fast food outlets like McDonalds, Burger King or KFC, but that does means its islanders aren't big fans of a juicy burger and chips.
What started out as a little outlet to sell quick bites, such as chips, sausages, burgers and sandwiches at Mont Fleuri, a southern suburb of Victoria, the capital of the Seychelles, proved so popular that a new chain was established.

Original_Search_Title: Where Everyone Sounds Filthy Rich
Headline: Tanzania: Where Everyone Sounds Filthy Rich
Body_Text:
CASTO, my youngest son, was playing ragball with friends outside the gate when I arrived.
He came bounding towards me and jumped into my hands chuckling, apparently enjoying a sense of belonging.

Original_Search_Title: Invisible Borders... Road Trip Arrives Sarajevo
Headline: Nigeria: Invisible Borders... Road Trip Arrives Sarajevo
Body_Text:
Abuja — THE Invisible Borders' 'Road Trip: Lagos To Sarajevo', which began on June 2, 2014, has finally reached Sarajevo after over 140 days on the road. Traveling across 20 countries with over 40 stops between Lagos, Nigeria to Sarajevo, Bosnia-Herzegovina, they finally reached their European destination.
Through Facebook and the Invisible Borders 'Tripp App', the artists have been streaming and communicating the journey and their experiences to fans and followers all through the road trip.

Original_Search_Title: The Gods Protect Traders' Wares in Mfum, Spirit Arrests Gari Thieves
Headline: Nigeria: The Gods Protect Traders' Wares in Mfum, Spirit Arrests Gari Thieves
Body_Text:
When a traveller hinted that there was a community in Niger Delta where traders do not pack their wares home after the day's business, but just cover them with waterproofs and other materials, it sounded too real to be true in this evil era.
So this reporter went to Mfum community in Ogoja Local Government Area, Cross River State, located at the intersection that leads to Abakaliki and Igoli, which is 30 and 15 kilometres away, respectively, from Ogoja metropolis, to authenticate the claim.

Original_Search_Title: Kayonza Residents Await Compensation Six Years On
Headline: Rwanda: Kayonza Residents Await Compensation Six Years On
Body_Text:
Residents of Kayonza have complained over delays to compensate them for property and land lost in 2008 to pave way for construction of feeder roads in the town.
The affected residents are from Bwiza, Nyagatovu and Kayonza cells in Mukarange Sector.

Original_Search_Title: Deconstructing Fayose's Populism
Headline: Nigeria: Deconstructing Fayose's Populism
Body_Text:
While delivering his inaugural speech Thursday October 16, Governor Ayodele Fayose of Ekiti State clearly depicted the stature of a populist. But it did not quite come out as clearly if Fayose realised that populism, as a weapon of political domination, more or less compels moral imperative that comes with a huge burden.
By now, no one needs to be convinced or impressed any further about the mission Fasoye has set for himself. Even if the burden of such mission is unenviable, he has left no one in doubt about his desire to consolidate on a populist template that has endeared him to the hearts of overwhelming majority of Ekiti electorate.

Original_Search_Title: We 'Ll Change the One-City Status of Rivers - Maeba
Headline: Nigeria: We 'Ll Change the One-City Status of Rivers - Maeba
Body_Text:
Senator Lee Maeba represented Rivers South-East in the Senate on the banner of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP). During his stint in the Senate, he was chairman, Senate Committee on Petroleum and one of the strong voices of Ogoniland. Now aspiring to succeed Governor Rotimi Amaechi, Maeba, in this interview, speaks on his vision and mission for Rivers State, his chances and what the state would gain from his administration, if elected.
You aspire to be the governor of Rivers State, how prepared are you?

Original_Search_Title: Doctor Outlines Food to Build Immune System
Headline: Liberia: Doctor Outlines Food to Build Immune System
Body_Text:
As the fight against the deadly Ebola Virus Disease (EVD) continues Dr. Billy C. Johnson, one of Liberia's foremost medical practitioners has been giving an outline of food specimen that can help boost ones' Immune system. The Ebola Virus Disease (EVD) takes over the DNA and damages the immune system leaving the body without any protection.
Dr. Johnson told journalists yesterday at the regular Ebola News Conference held at the Ministry of Information that as Liberians continue to fight the deadly virus, it is prudent that they eat food that will help boost their immune system and strengthen their body tissues.

Original_Search_Title: Alobam Designer, Myro Steps Out Solo
Headline: Nigeria: Alobam Designer, Myro Steps Out Solo
Body_Text:
HE first came to limelight after he designed Phyno's Alobam merchandise shirts, but today, Ozah Oghenemairo otherwise known as Myro has resolved to start a solo career in music. His intention is to conquer the industry within the next few years and to establish his design and merchandise outfit, which he will run alongside his music career.
Born in Ajegunle and grew up in Surulere, Lagos, Myro attended Abbey Junior Mixed and Infants School where he had his first came in contact with performing arts, being taught by Joke Silva and Olu Jacobs, before proceeding to Abbey College on a full scholarship. He eventually finished his secondary education at Avi-Cenna International School on another art scholarship. He later enrolled at the Caleb University.

Original_Search_Title: And He Laughs!
Headline: Nigeria: And He Laughs!
Body_Text:
Comedy is great fun, I especially love to laugh heartily and I can make jokes of most sad situations. If you can find some mirth in adversity, you can find enough hope to positively spin things around.
That said, there is nothing funny about tragedy, plagues and terror, I haven't found the jokes in those situations just yet! I am amazed to be writing this column this week as it wasn't a possibility I even gave a thought to.

Original_Search_Title: Bold N' Daring Cynthia Agholor
Headline: Nigeria: Bold N' Daring Cynthia Agholor
Body_Text:
AS a child, Cynthia Agholor loved to act and express herself a lot. Unknown to her, this passion will later pave way for her in life. Ever since the marketing graduate of Federal Polytechnic, Oko, Anambra State, started acting in Nollywood, she has not looked back. Some of the movies she has featured include, My sister, My child; Forced love; Sincerely yours; The year 2000; Village toaster; Lake of fire; World behind; Tears for tomorrow and Gina and the fish. Her awards include, African Youth Society Award on Outstanding Actresses of the Year, NEGA Awards in Germany to name a few. In this chat with GERALDINE AKUTU, she shares her experience in the film industry.
What childhood experience led you into acting?

Original_Search_Title: Big Brother Unveils Nigerian Housemate
Headline: Nigeria: Big Brother Unveils Nigerian Housemate
Body_Text:
Another twist has been introduced to this season's Big Brother Africa, which will hit the screens on 5 October, 2014.
It is appropriately termed Hotshots, and Big Brother has commenced teasing its teeming fansby revealing three Hotshots from the various participating countries, daily.

Original_Search_Title: Danwaki - Where Dogs Go for Palm Oil
Headline: Nigeria: Danwaki - Where Dogs Go for Palm Oil
Body_Text:
Seriki Adinoyi writes on an ancient trade by barter in a market where traders exchange dogs for palm oil in Plateau State
Thursday mornings are usually eventful at Danwaki, a famous international dog market in Kanke Local Government Area of Plateau State, where there has never been a dull moment among the traders. It is one thing: A market of convenience, where culture of barter thrives.

Original_Search_Title: Mobile Firm Pledges to Fund Local SMEs
Headline: Nigeria: Mobile Firm Pledges to Fund Local SMEs
Body_Text:
Lagos — LOCAL small and medium organisations have welcomed a proposal by a mobile network operator to issue grants to such firms.
A cross section of the up-and-coming companies, under the aegis of Small and Medium Enterprises Development Agency of Nigeria commended the move by Globacom saying it would empower members.

Original_Search_Title: 25 Hours Under Church Guesthouse Rubble
Headline: Nigeria: How I Survived "Demonic Attack" On Synagogue Church After 28 Hours in Rubble - - Survivor
Body_Text:
One of the survivors of the building collapse at The Synagogue Church of All Nations, Friday, told journalists that the incident was a demonic attack on the church.
Taiwo Temitayo, who studied Business Studies at the University of West London, said that he doesn't believe the incident was "ordinary."

Original_Search_Title: When NGO, Onicha-Ugbo People United Against Diabetes
Headline: Nigeria: When NGO, Onicha-Ugbo People United Against Diabetes
Body_Text:
Lagos — In the bid to enhance the health status of people in their environs, the people of Onicha-Ugbo, living at the Alimosho area of Lagos on September 7 held a health education programme on healthy lifestyle.
Onicha-Ugbo is in Aniocha North Local Government Area of Delta State.

Original_Search_Title: Bolanle Olukanni - Voyage Through Dreams to Reality Show
Headline: Nigeria: Bolanle Olukanni - Voyage Through Dreams to Reality Show
Body_Text:
Yinka Olatunbosun takes the veil off Bolanle Olukanni, the new co-host of Project Fame West Africa, who is on a roll, living her dreams which are coming true faster than she can blink.
When the on-going MTN Project Fame West Africa Season 7.0 kicked off, many viewers were surprised to see Joseph Benjamin on stage with a new co-host, Bolanle Olukanni, who had replaced Adaora as presenter on the popular television reality show.

Original_Search_Title: Child Hawkers On the Ascendency On Monrovia-Ganta Highway
Headline: Liberia: Child Hawkers On the Ascendency On Monrovia-Ganta Highway
Body_Text:
Monrovia — Hawking is an age long tradition among Liberians. It has become a child of necessity owing to the inability of most traders to afford the high cost of shops and the limited space in the market places, especially in the cities. To some hawkers, low sales in the shops and markets make them hawk in the streets, while to others, the need to bring their wares closer to passersby, motorists and passengers make them embrace hawking in the streets and highways in Liberia.
A cross section of hawkers on the Monrovia-Ganta highway who spoke to FrontPageAfrica said that the high volume of sales they make on the streets, and the fact that they don't have to pay rent for shops, have made them dare the laws against hawking on the highway.

Original_Search_Title: '404' Medicinal, Chases Away Evil Spirits - Cross River, Akwa-Ibom Consumers
Headline: Nigeria: '404' Medicinal, Chases Away Evil Spirits - Cross River, Akwa-Ibom Consumers
Body_Text:
Calabar — In some communities in the Niger-Delta, eating dog meat is a taboo, but among the Efiks in Calabar and other tribes in Cross-River and Akwa-Ibom States, eating '404', 'J5', 'pepperoni', among others, as dog meat is prevalently known, is a way of life.
The guzzlers tell you bluntly that it is a delicacy that goes smoothly with beer besides the medicinal and healing power.

Original_Search_Title: Mixed Fortunes for Shoppers
Headline: Rwanda: Mixed Fortunes for Shoppers
Body_Text:
Food prices in most city and regional markets are stable compared to three weeks ago. A kilo of Irish potatoes was unchanged at between Rwf150 and Rwf170 in major towns of Base, Gakenke, Musanze, Kinigi, Byangabo, Butaro and Cyanika markets. However, it is at between Rwf190 and Rwf200 in most city markets, also stable compared to a week ago.
Anserine Nirere, a trader in Kicukiro market, projected prices to reduce even further because of the rainy season.

Original_Search_Title: Soboum-Bilongue - Community Grapples With Insecurity, Pollution
Headline: Cameroon: Soboum-Bilongue - Community Grapples With Insecurity, Pollution
Body_Text:
The myriad of insecurities in the neighbourhoods are linked to dirty environment.
Life is quite different in the neighbourhoods of Soboum and Bilongue, Douala III Subdivision, from the rest of the economic capital. The nicety and calmness of daily activities is quickly interrupted by the sight of a greatly polluted gutter and its bushy length as one edges towards the bridge connecting the two neighbourhoods.

Original_Search_Title: Sempaya Hot Springs Under Climate Threat
Headline: Uganda: Sempaya Hot Springs Under Climate Threat
Body_Text:
Semuliki — Those who visited Sempaya Female Hot Springs in the early 1990s would have seen huge volumes of water at a very high velocity springing up.
Today, that velocity is steadily decreasing. The other hot springs have also toned down on their speed. This may be as a result of climate change.

Original_Search_Title: Inside Riverine Communities of Rivers
Headline: Nigeria: Inside Riverine Communities of Rivers
Body_Text:
Marcus Yemiete writes about the rich heritage of the Ijaw people in Rivers State and their agitation for political relevance Morning and night are inseparable wherever you find them. They are industrious and quite creative. True, it's very hard to find an ijaw man begging for alms. They are providers. Not only that, there's some quite genius about the ijaws.
Unsurprisingly, one of their progenitors, Prince Jaja of Opobo made an impressive record of himself till today. In his days, he lived in a society where sheer prowess and ability made men whatever they were. And that was why he was able to rise to the pinnacle of his career as an administrator of no mean standing. At that time, leadership was not a function of mere royal descent among the Delta people. Jaja of Opobo was a merchant prince who founded the Opobo city-state through his extraordinary dexterity.

Original_Search_Title: Mahama's Lamentations Over Donor Deafness
Headline: Ghana: Mahama's Lamentations Over Donor Deafness
Body_Text:
President John Dramani Mahama has taken his disappointment with the donor community or our development partners to the National House of Chiefs.
Addressing Nananom in Kumasi last Wednesday, President Mahama conceded that the non-availability of the previously free-flowing donor funds was a wake-up call for dependence on ingenuity.

Original_Search_Title: And Omisore Wins the 'Award'
Headline: Nigeria: And Omisore Wins the 'Award'
Body_Text:
THE ridiculous level politicians will reduce themselves to; to win the people's heart is becoming very alarming. And last week, the People's
Democratic Party (PDP) gubernatorial candidate in Osun State, Senator Iyiola Omisore, truly took the macabre drama to a rather all time ludicrous height.

Original_Search_Title: Floods Cut Off Parts of Douala
Headline: Cameroon: Floods Cut Off Parts of Douala
Body_Text:
Most houses, wooden residential structures and marketplaces ceased to function until late afternoon yesterday, July 8, 2014, following floods that covered most parts of Douala. Public and private activities did not resume as squatters in Mambanda, "Bois des Singes" and "Petit Paris" in Bonapriso, "Village" as well as city dwellers in Akwa Nord and New Bell continued to flush water from homes and around business centres.
The economic capital has been experiencing episodes of heavy rains that last about two to four hours daily since last week and intensifying yesterday as from 5 am. The floods destroyed household properties like parlour chairs, mattresses, and electrical gadgets in Mambanda and some areas of New Bell. No death was registered.

Original_Search_Title: Enduring Standoff in Edo House
Headline: Nigeria: Enduring Standoff in Edo House
Body_Text:
Benin — There has been palpable apprehension that what happened in the Edo State House of Assembly on Monday, June 9, 2014, could eventually lead to anarchy. On that day, the House took a decision to suspend four of its members over what was described as 'misconduct'. Those suspended were the Deputy Speaker of the House, Mr. Festus Ebea, representing Esan South East Constituency; Hon Friday Ogieirhiaikhi, representing Orhionmwon South; Jude Ise-Idehen, representing Ikpoba-Okha, and Patrick Osayimwen, representing Oredo East Constituency. A total of 11 members were present at the sitting where the lawmakers were suspended, and they based the suspension on Section 38 Rule 3 of the House Rules.
The suspension order

Original_Search_Title: Striking Doctors to Treat Boko Haram Victims
Headline: Nigeria: Striking Doctors to Treat Boko Haram Victims
Body_Text:
The federal government has promised that striking members of the Nigerian Medical Association (NMA) would continue the treatment of bomb blast victims despite the strike.
The Coordinator of the National Briefing Centre on Terrorism and Director General of National Orientation Agency (NOA), Mr. Mike Omieri, who disclosed this yesterday in Abuja, said the leadership of NMA had expressed its desire to treat bomb blast victims during the period of the strike.

Original_Search_Title: Military Denies Allegations of Rights Abuse
Headline: Nigeria: Military Denies Allegations of Rights Abuse
Body_Text:
The Nigerian military on Wednesday denied allegations of human rights abuses levied against troops operating in the North Eastern part of Nigeria.
The military has been accused of human rights abuses and extra-judicial killings by witnesses, local and international observer groups, including the Human Rights Watch.

Original_Search_Title: U.S. Deputy Secretary of State Visits USAID Liberia-Ibex
Headline: Liberia: U.S. Deputy Secretary of State Visits USAID Liberia-Ibex
Body_Text:
Madam Higginbottom and delegation met with local IBEX/IESC staff and 10 Liberian women entrepreneurs
Monrovia - The United States Deputy Secretary of State for Management and Resources, Heather Higginbottom along with a U.S. Department of State and USAID Liberia delegation Thursday, 19 June visited USAID Liberia's Investing for Business Expansion Program (IBEX).

Original_Search_Title: Police Nab Four Human Parts Dealers in Lagos
Headline: Nigeria: Police Nab Four Human Parts Dealers in Lagos
Body_Text:
Lagos — Special Anti-Robbery Squad, SARS, at the Lagos State Police Command, weekend, arrested two herbalists and two Islamic clerics (aka Alfas), who specialised in buying and selling of human body parts.
Vanguard gathered that the suspects, identified as Wasiu Oyebamiji, Ogunshola Olakunle Afolabi, Akorede Wasiu and Rasheed Opeyemi, were apprehended in various parts of Ogun and Lagos states.

Original_Search_Title: Ekiti Poll As Template for 2015 Elections
Headline: Nigeria: Ekiti Poll As Template for 2015 Elections
Body_Text:
Lagos — When he was impeached in October 2006, former Governor of Ekiti State (and now governor-elect), Ayo Fayose, was reported to have made several calls to Ekiti residents in Ado Ekiti and Afao-Ekiti, his hometown, to assure them that he would "bounce back". Many then had dismissed the message as wishful thinking because a state of emergency was declared in Ekiti and he was not returned to power till the expiration of his tenure which was completed by a sole administrator, Tunji Olurin. But like the proverbial cat with nine lives, or more like a prophet, Fayose did bounce back exactly 8 years later as he was unanimously voted as the governor-elect of Ekiti State on Saturday, June 21. Not only did he win the election by a landslide, he also made history by being the first candidate to defeat two incumbent governors, an achievement that is a no mean feat, given the type of politics practised in Nigeria.
Against expectations:

Original_Search_Title: Police Affairs Minister Says He Regrets Not Beating a Former Governor
Headline: Nigeria: Police Affairs Minister Says He Regrets Not Beating a Former Governor
Body_Text:
Minister of Police Affairs Alhaji Jelili Adesiyan has expressed regret for not flogging the first civilian governor of Osun State, Senator Isiaka Adetunji Adeleke, during the People's Democratic Party's (PDP) stakeholders meeting with the officials of the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) in Osogbo.
Adeleke alleged that the Osun State PDP governorship candidate, Senator Christopher Iyiola Omisore, Adesiyan and a retired Customs officer, Mr. Sogo Agboola, beat him at a function a day before the state PDP governorship primaries, in which he planned to run.

Original_Search_Title: Over 1.2million Stems Uprooted to Curb Banana Disease Spread
Headline: Tanzania: Over 1.2million Stems Uprooted to Curb Banana Disease Spread
Body_Text:
Bukoba — OVER 1,296,000 banana stems were recently uprooted and destroyed by burning in Bukoba Rural District in effort to combat the deadly Banana Xanthomonas Wilt (BXW).
Bukoba district executive director, Ms Gladys Dyamvunye, said 80 out of 92 villages in the district had been affected by BXW, adding that the district faced shortage of 51,879 tonnes of food.

Original_Search_Title: More to June 3 Than Meets the Eye
Headline: Uganda: More to June 3 Than Meets the Eye
Body_Text:
Unveiling unsung places related to Uganda martyrs
Every June 3, hundreds of pilgrims throng Namugongo to pay tribute to the Uganda martyrs who died for religion between 1862 and 1887.

Original_Search_Title: There Was No Money in Edo to Loot - - Igbinedion
Headline: Nigeria: There Was No Money in Edo to Loot - - Igbinedion
Body_Text:
Former Governor of Edo State, Chief Lucky Igbinedion, has maintained a low profile since leaving office seven years ago. Ahead of his 57th birthday today, he broke his long silence in the public space during an interview session with journalists in Abuja last weekend.
Igbinendion affirmed that he had been wrongly perceived by the general public, asserted that he exceeded the performance of all governors before him and also claimed that he left office a poorer man than when he entered. Excerpts:

Original_Search_Title: WEFA - 'The Road to Economic Prosperity'
Headline: Africa: WEFA - 'The Road to Economic Prosperity'
Body_Text:
Comrade Folorunso Oginni is the Lagos Zonal Chairman, Petroleum and Natural Gas Senior Staff Association of Nigeria (PENGASSAN). He spoke on the World Economic Forum hosted by Nigeria, the activities of the organised labour, after 100 years of existence, the mono-cultural economic practice in Nigeria, sharp labour practices going on in some companies and the need for the Federal Government to harness un-tapped resources.
As a stakeholder in a major sector like oil and gas, how do you evaluate the World Economic Forum on forging inclusive growth, creating jobs, hosted by Nigeria?

Original_Search_Title: My Plans for Aniocha South - - Osammor
Headline: Nigeria: My Plans for Aniocha South - - Osammor
Body_Text:
Chief Fidelis Uchedike Osammor recently retired as a director in the Federal Civil Service and is now set to enter the jostling for political ascendancy in his native Aniocha South Local Government of Delta State. He spoke in an interview on his plans and prospects. Excerpts:
NOW that you have retired from the civil service, what would be your next step?

Original_Search_Title: DStv Launches Movie Rental Boxoffice for Subscribers
Headline: Nigeria: DStv Launches Movie Rental Boxoffice for Subscribers
Body_Text:
Lagos — Cable network provider, DSTV, has launched a new entertainment package called BoxOffice, which already has 15 Hollywood and five Nollywood movies in its kitty.
At a press launch in Lagos, management of the service provider said what they have done is to devise one of the easiest way of watching recent movies for its teeming subscribers.

Original_Search_Title: The Day Workers Get Paid
Headline: Rwanda: The Day Workers Get Paid
Body_Text:
Very strange things happen when people get paid for their sweat.
People in possession of cheques from debtors now rush to cash them before said debtor empties the account to deal with more pressing needs. It's called stealing from Claudine to pay Sandrine. But that's not all.

Original_Search_Title: Multichoice's BoxOffice Launches Locally
Headline: Nigeria: Multichoice's BoxOffice Launches Locally
Body_Text:
Lagos — MULTICHOICE has officially launched its BoxOffice offering in the country.
This would provide digital satellite television (DSTV) subscribers with the ultimate in home television entertainment, according to the company.

Original_Search_Title: For the Good of All
Headline: Cameroon: For the Good of All
Body_Text:
The government decision announced several months ago to ban the use of plastic materials in commercial transactions and other usages goes into force across the national territory from Thursday.
In the build-up to D-day today, there have been many reactions, many negative, as to the timeliness of the initiative with many predicting untold harm to the economy and even social cohesion, given the widespread manner in the use of these plastics. All this acrimony surrounding the measure has, primarily, to do with habits that have been practiced for a very long time and going by the proverbial habits that die had, one can understand these feelings of resentment. But all is not about managing sentiments for now.

Original_Search_Title: Cross River Blurs the Rural-Urban Divide
Headline: Nigeria: Cross River Blurs the Rural-Urban Divide
Body_Text:
In Cross River State, rural dwellers have been given a new vista, as government continues to revamp access roads to connect them to urban areas.
Already well-known for its tourist's attraction, the Cross River State Government is changing the way people live in its rural areas. With access roads and other social amenities, the hinterlands of a state that prides itself in culture are having a semblance of city centres, at least, if not for anything else, the newly constructed roads that are now proving connection between the communities are giving citizens a sense of pride.

Original_Search_Title: Pregnant Woman Was 'Sacrificed'
Headline: Liberia: Pregnant Woman Was 'Sacrificed'
Body_Text:
The second state witness in the ongoing mercenary case involving 18 persons currently on trial at Criminal Court "D" at the Temple of Justice last Friday testified that General Nyezee Barway allegedly killed a bright pregnant woman as sacrifice for the attack in Ivory Coast in 2012.
Neatly dressed in a blue jean and T-shirt, with a pair of brown slippers, Baryee Gaye told the court after crossing with their weapons on the Ivorian side of the border, Barway reportedly informed the fighters that the only way their mission would be accomplished, except a pregnant woman is sacrificed.

Original_Search_Title: Living By the Wheelbarrow
Headline: Liberia: Living By the Wheelbarrow
Body_Text:
In Liberia, wheelbarrows are humble but robust technology. They provide employment and help keep local economies moving. Critics say they are a reminder of underdevelopment.
In Red Light Market, Paynesville, a suburb east of the Liberian capital Monrovia, a wheelbarrow is more than just a tool or an implement. 34-year-old Layee Kromah earns his living with a wheelbarrow, transporting goods or belongings for a small fee. "It's a difficult job," said the former high school French teacher, who quit teaching because the salary was too low.

Original_Search_Title: Recruitment Agencies Worsen Our Plight - Job Seekers
Headline: Nigeria: Recruitment Agencies Worsen Our Plight - Job Seekers
Body_Text:
Unemployment, insecurity, harsh economic conditions among others indicate that young graduates in the country are losing out. While unconfirmed statistics indicate that three out of ten graduates in the country are unemployed, some of them have become commercial motorcycle operators and petrol station attendants.
To add to their frustration, some smart but dubious recruitment agencies have capitalised on their woes to exploit them under the guise of securing profitable jobs for them. Some job seekers shared their experiences in the labour market with Saturday Vanguard.

Original_Search_Title: Living By the Wheelbarrow in Liberia
Headline: Liberia: Living By the Wheelbarrow in Liberia
Body_Text:
In Liberia, wheelbarrows are humble but robust technology. They provide employment and help keep local economies moving. Critics say they are a reminder of underdevelopment.
In Red Light Market, Paynesville, a suburb east of the Liberian capital Monrovia, a wheelbarrow is more than just a tool or an implement. 34-year-old Layee Kromah earns his living with a wheelbarrow, transporting goods or belongings for a small fee. "It's a difficult job," said the former high school French teacher, who quit teaching because the salary was too low.

Original_Search_Title: Ekiti 2014 - Why Fayemi Is Not Building Stomach Infrastructure - - Daramola
Headline: Nigeria: Ekiti 2014 - Why Fayemi Is Not Building Stomach Infrastructure - - Daramola
Body_Text:
Congressman Bimbo Daramola, a member of the House of Representatives, is the Director-General of the Kayode Fayemi Campaign Organisation. In this interview he gives reasons the people of Ekiti State would choose Dr. Fayemi to become the first governor of the state to win reelection in the state.
WHAT are the issues in your estimation that will shape this election?

Original_Search_Title: Now the Poor Have Hope in Delta - - Ashiedu
Headline: Nigeria: Now the Poor Have Hope in Delta - - Ashiedu
Body_Text:
Ashiedu, who holds a doctorate in political science, was before her appointment as a commissioner, a special adviser to the governor. In this interview she speaks on the impact of the micro-credit programme on the populace. Excerpts:
Delta State Micro-Credit Programme (DMCP) was actually launched in December 14, 2007 but took off effectively in 2008. You would wonder why micro-credit?

Original_Search_Title: 'Why Fayemi Is Going for Second Term'
Headline: Nigeria: 'Why Fayemi Is Going for Second Term'
Body_Text:
Lagos — Ahead of the commencement of the campaign for Ekiti State governorship election the Director-General, Kayode Fayemi Campaign Organisation, Bimbo Daramola, speaks on Governor Fayemi's achievement in office and why the governor deserves second term. Excerpts:
Your party, the All Progressives Congress (APC) has endorsed Governor Kayode Fayemi to run on the forthcoming governorship election in the state. Does this endorsement mean there won't be primaries before?

Original_Search_Title: A Recruitment Tragedy, Moron Moro and a Sordid Blame Game
Headline: Nigeria: A Recruitment Tragedy, Moron Moro and a Sordid Blame Game
Body_Text:
A bad system produces bad products. I am not surprised the system threw up an Abba Moro at the helm of the Interior Ministry.
The Nigerian Immigration Service (NIS) made N7billion from the sale of forms and vests to 7million candidates seeking employment with the agency. The sale of forms is understandable but what about vests? Oh! They are supposed to engage in paramilitary exercise, oh yeah? Where? How? In a crowded stadium of over 60,000 applicants? What an ugly joke this is? Why are we like this? This exercise that ended in premeditated deaths of innocent job seekers is another orchestrated fleecing of hapless Nigerian youths.

Original_Search_Title: A Murder So Bizarre
Headline: Nigeria: A Murder So Bizarre
Body_Text:
Early last month in Kankia, Katsina State, an angry mob descended on Pastor Dike Ocha in the most bizarre fashion and cut him down in his prime.The traumatised widow of the clergyman, Mrs. Favour Ocha recounts to Senator Iroegbu on life after the ugly incident
"I lost my husband and my house, nothing was left, not even a pin. It is just sympathisers that have been helping me and my children with clothes and other items. Frankly speaking, I am not happy because they killed him innocently."

Original_Search_Title: 'The Gobarchop Woman - Liberia's Marketers Power Brokers in Action
Headline: Liberia: 'The Gobarchop Woman - Liberia's Marketers Power Brokers in Action
Body_Text:
Monrovia — It is a sunny Friday afternoon the eve of International Women's Day, Miatta Jackson, 43 and other local business women in Monrovia's commercial district of Duala are busy running behind vehicles filled with goods that has just come from upcountry.
Together they call themselves the by most peculiar name that you can imagine and even though it is in no way related to the former Soviet President Mikhail Gorbachev, it is the name by which they ply their trade with pride.

Original_Search_Title: Ghana At 57 - Politicians Its Bane
Headline: Ghana At 57 - Politicians Its Bane
Body_Text:
Fifty-seven years to the day at the Polo Grounds, Accra Osagyefo Dr. Kwame Nkrumah, a rare breed Ghanaian politician, declared Ghana's independence from British rule, and linked its meaning to the total liberation of the African continent.
True to his word, he assisted freedom fighters across the continent - South Africa, Namibia, Angola, Southern Rhodesia, Mozambique, the Congo, name it, anywhere the African was subjugated in his own country - in their quest for independence.

Original_Search_Title: Dissecting Labor's Partnership With the Media in Preventing Child Labor
Headline: Liberia: Dissecting Labor's Partnership With the Media in Preventing Child Labor
Body_Text:
You don't need to come from the headquarters of the International Labor Organization (ILO) in Geneva, Switzerland, to know it. Child labor and abuse is commonplace in our society. It is prevalent in developing countries of the world--with alarming statistics.
It's worrisome. In Liberia, you witness it from the beautiful beaches of Grand Cape Mount County, through the capital Monrovia, to central Liberia's Gbarnga, and to the adoring coastlines of Cape Palmas in Maryland County.

Original_Search_Title: Marketing Moxie - Creating a Better Blog
Headline: Uganda: Marketing Moxie - Creating a Better Blog
Body_Text:
Kampala — "I would like to market my company products online and increase company sales," a Ugandan businessman recently wrote to me in a LinkedIn message. "I request your advice on how to create a blog."
This reader brings up a very good topic. A blog can help to attract viewers to your website, educate them about your products and services, and ultimately, result in more sales.

Original_Search_Title: Quick Talk - Navio Talks Paani, Gonja
Headline: Uganda: Quick Talk - Navio Talks Paani, Gonja
Body_Text:
When Quick Talk sits down for the interview with Navio at Café Javas, she is conscious of other diners' eyes boring through her back.
It is disconcerting and she wonders how light-skinned, six-foot-three-inches Navio gets through all the staring daily.

Original_Search_Title: eLDee Tha Don: The Problem-solver
Headline: Nigeria: eLDee Tha Don: The Problem-solver
Body_Text:
Not only is Lanre Dabiri a.k.a eLDee Tha Don the founder of one of Nigeria’s most recognised record labels (Trybe Records),he has continually proven that, as a musician, he is a force to reckon with. The man who is a passionately creative music entrepreneur, discusses the importance of social media as a tool to bring about ‘change’ in the Nigerian polity and how he has managed to remain scandal-free.
You are an artiste, producer, music video director, record label-owner, social crusader and, according to Wikipedia, a “cutting edge interactive media consultant who has worked with Fortune 500 companies”.

Original_Search_Title: Marketers, Residents At Mercy of Garbage
Headline: Liberia: Marketers, Residents At Mercy of Garbage
Body_Text:
At Gobachov Market In Paynesville Residents and marketers living and selling in the Gobachov Market in the commercial hub of Paynesville, outside Monrovia have alarmed over the huge pile of garbage in the area with fingers pointed at the management of the Paynesville City Cooperation (PCC) for doing absolutely nothing to remove waste.
The huge pile which is being continuously dumped on a newly constructed road along the Gobachov Market Police Station is said to have been deposited there for more than three months now.

Original_Search_Title: Byabashaija, Commissioner General of Excellence
Headline: Uganda: Byabashaija, Commissioner General of Excellence
Body_Text:
As a boy, he loved the colour maroon and admired a veterinary doctor who drove a Mercedes Benz in 1969.
Where would he end up? In a maroon Benz? Well, he studied veterinary medicine, joined the maroon Prisons service, and is now a poultry breeder. Here is Dr Johnson Christopher Byabasaija, the commissioner general of Uganda Prisons Services.

Original_Search_Title: A Family's Six-Month Drive From Sout Africa to Calabar
Headline: Nigeria: A Family's Six-Month Drive From Sout Africa to Calabar
Body_Text:
Carnivals are one of the top tourism magnets all over the world, no matter how far flung or near their locations are. From long haul flights to stopovers at different airports, carnival enthusiasts remain undaunted in their desire to quench this thirst. Calabar Carnival is no different as one couple (The Sampsons) decided to do it differently this year, bypassing the comfort of a 6-hour flight from South Africa to Nigeria for a 6-month drive in the quest to savour the thrill of Calabar Carnival. The Sampsons talk to Omolola Itayemi while enjoying the carnival
From a modest beginning in 2005, the Calabar Carnival, reputed to be the biggest street party in Africa today, has become an annual fiesta steadily transforming into a continental and global showpiece with hundreds of thousands in attendance every year. Little wonder, it served as the fulcrum of Mr and Mrs Mark Sampson with their children's (Rubi and Zola) 6-month journey by road to Nigeria. Very daring and not exactly an option people will opt for.

Original_Search_Title: Pastor, Church Member Held Over Killing of Girl, 12
Headline: Nigeria: Pastor, Church Member Held Over Killing of Girl, 12
Body_Text:
Lagos — The General Overseer of a Pentecostal church, the Perfect Christian Ministry in Surulere, Lagos, has been arrested by the police over his alleged involvement in the murder of 12 year old girl.
Simply identified as Fireman, the pastor and his 18 year-old member are currently being detained at the State Criminal Investigation Department (SCID) Panti Street, Yaba, Lagos following the confession the member, Ikechukwu Friday, who was nabbed while strangulating 12 year-old Bose.

Original_Search_Title: Healthy Criticisms Are Welcome - Kojo Bonsu
Headline: Ghana: Healthy Criticisms Are Welcome - Kojo Bonsu
Body_Text:
THE MAYOR of Kumasi, Hon. Kojo Bonsu has welcomed constructive criticism of his administration, saying it keeps him on his toes for the good of the development of Kumasi.
He says he bears no grudge against any media organization which criticizes his administration provided it is not done out of malice.

Original_Search_Title: Liberia's Unending Story of Child Labor, Trafficking - Victims Explain Their Ordeal
Headline: Liberia's Unending Story of Child Labor, Trafficking - Victims Explain Their Ordeal
Body_Text:
Despite global, regional and national efforts to fight child labor and trafficking, the situation seems far from ending in Liberia as many describe it as a "normal way of life."
Liberia is a source, transit, and destination country for young women and children subjected to forced labor and sex trafficking, the United States Department of States reported in its 2012 Trafficking in Persons Report.

Original_Search_Title: Hope for SMEs as Banks Moot Affordable Loan Packages
Headline: Rwanda: Hope for SMEs as Banks Moot Affordable Loan Packages
Body_Text:
Startup capital and loan guarantees are some of the main stumbling blocks the business people face daily. Businesses, especially small-and-medium enterprises (SMEs) say they do not have collateral nor can they afford the loan guarantees to secure funding from financial institutions.
"Last year, I applied for a loan of Rwf53 million to buy a bus to start a transport business, but did not get it I could not afford the loan guarantee," recounts Gilbert Mugabo, a resident of Remera Sector, Gasabo District.

Original_Search_Title: Peter Greste, Always Ready to Ask the Hard Questions
Headline: Egypt: Peter Greste, Always Ready to Ask the Hard Questions
Body_Text:
It was the sort of rain that drenched your pants, an unrelenting soaking that moulds skin to cloth.
I was with Al Jazeera's correspondent Peter Greste, cameraman John Kinyua, and our fixer Jacob* in a small town called Walikale, in the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. We were in the middle of an old fashioned stakeout outside of a primary school, and the rain just wouldn't end.

Original_Search_Title: Kiss Daniel, Slyde, Lil Kesh Others to Rule 2015
Headline: Nigeria: Kiss Daniel, Slyde, Lil Kesh Others to Rule 2015
Body_Text:
In ‎2014, a lot of upcoming acts released songs with little or no fan base and to the surprise of many, they became stars with Lil Kesh, Kiss Daniel, Slyde, Runtown and Reekado Banks as the stand out stars. ‎
Lil Kesh

Original_Search_Title: Food Prices Remain Stable in Dar
Headline: Tanzania: Food Prices Remain Stable in Dar
Body_Text:
Scores of Dar es Salaam residents yesterday turned out for final shopping of Christmas holiday preparations, as the price of food items remained stable.
Christians in the country today join fellow believers around the world to celebrate the annual festival commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ in fulfilment of the Old Testament's Messianic prophecy.

Original_Search_Title: Years in the Making, Arms Trade Treaty Enters Into Force
Headline: Africa: Years in the Making, Arms Trade Treaty Enters Into Force
Body_Text:
United Nations — A new Arms Trade Treaty (ATT) beginning on Dec. 24 represents a historic moment in global efforts to keep weapons proliferation in check.
Nounou Booto Meeti, programme director at the Centre for Peace, Security and Armed Violence Prevention, told IPS that in her own home country, the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), the uncontrolled trade of arms has contributed to human rights violations including rape and the recruitment of child soldiers.

Original_Search_Title: Promoting Peace, Unity Through Photography
Headline: Rwanda: Promoting Peace, Unity Through Photography
Body_Text:
Four hands, two white and two black, are enjoined to form a square-like shape in a sign of unity.
And, a short distance away, three young people happily walk on a stony road, also holding up their hands-sending out a message of a nation and a people moving forward to the same destination and with the same vision.

Original_Search_Title: 2015 - Understanding PDP's N21 Billion Sweepstakes!
Headline: Nigeria: 2015 - Understanding PDP's N21 Billion Sweepstakes!
Body_Text:
First, let me put the titling of today's piece in its proper context. What occurred in the Banquet Hall of the Aso Rock Presidential Villa last week, in which a motley crowd of pot-bellied politicians and their cronies contributed 21 billion naira to the campaign fund for the re-election of President Goodluck Jonathan, did not qualify to be referred to as 'sweepstakes' in the strict sense of the word. For it to qualify, the president needed to be the only individual who stood to reap from the infamy.
What occurred was not just another dinner-fundraiser in the true sense of the word. It was the meeting of high profile 'investors' with various stakes in the comic tragedy that is present-day Nigeria. And the same investors have become so accustomed to taking Nigerians for granted that they can no longer conceal the open contempt they have for our collective sensibilities.

Original_Search_Title: Changing Face of Christmas
Headline: Uganda: Changing Face of Christmas
Body_Text:
It's that time of the year again when everything around seems more colourful. The festive season is here. Many people are thinking of their own special way of marking the Christmas break. Some people are trendy while others prefer a more traditional approach; in the same way that we may not like change whileothers among us love to move with the times. For those who believe in moving with the changing modes of celebrations, keeping up-to-date with new trends is a must.
For Christian believers like the head of the Anglican diocese of Namirembe, Bishop Wilberforce Kityo Luwalira, Christmas is the period when Christ should "be born anew in us this Christmastide, his light burn in us, and that we may labour to spread it in the darkness."

Original_Search_Title: Farmer Accuses Church of Harassment
Headline: Kenya: Farmer Accuses Church of Harassment
Body_Text:
EMBU A family has accused the Catholic Diocese of harassment over a land dispute.
Alexander Karioko said on November 26, the church sent two agents to spray his maize with a dangerous chemical.

Original_Search_Title: Court Upholds Life Sentence for Convicted Lindi Rapist
Headline: Tanzania: Court Upholds Life Sentence for Convicted Lindi Rapist
Body_Text:
A NKOWE villager, in Lindi's Ruangwa District, James Sharifu, will remain behind bars for the rest of his life for defiling a fiveyear- old girl.
The Court of Appeal has dismissed an appeal the convict had lodged to fault a High Court judgment that convicted and sentenced him for the offence.

Original_Search_Title: Sambo Vs Osinbajo - How the PDP, APC Running Mates Will Influence Voting in 2015
Headline: Nigeria: Sambo Vs Osinbajo - How the PDP, APC Running Mates Will Influence Voting in 2015
Body_Text:
Abuja/Kaduna/Lagos — As the nation heads into the general elections early next year, the intense focus will be the presidential election which will be holding on February 14, eight weeks from now. For the first time since the return to democratic rule in 1999, the ruling party, the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) is expected to be stretched to the limit as Gen Muhammadu Buhari, the presidential candidate of the All Progressives Congress (APC), enjoying his most popular democratic support ever since he began contesting for election in 2003, confronts PDP's President Goodluck Jonathan.
The two formidable candidates are not unaware of the dynamics in elections in Nigeria and the influence their running mates would need to bring to the polling booth to effect victory. This is moreso imperative in the APC, which was confronted with a delicate balance of certain sentiments as it strove to explore the right running mate appeal.

Original_Search_Title: It's Eleven Years for Bus Driver Tumwijukye On Kigali-Kampala Route
Headline: Rwanda: It's Eleven Years for Bus Driver Tumwijukye On Kigali-Kampala Route
Body_Text:
For many people, the festive season is null and void if they don't leave their urban comforts and head to the countryside or ancestral homes. This annual exodus also includes those who travel from Kigali to Kampala and other parts of Uganda. Others travel from parts of Uganda to come and enjoy the big days here in Rwanda.
On this journey, scores who cannot afford an air ticket rely on the numerous buses that ply the Kigali - Kampala route.

Original_Search_Title: Osinbajo, the Anointed VP?
Headline: Nigeria: Osinbajo, the Anointed VP?
Body_Text:
Lagos — There is no denying the fact that the events leading to the emergence of Professor Yemi Osinbajo as running mate to General Muhammadu Buhari, the presidential flag bearer for the All Progressives Congress (APC) was a very tough one.
Indeed, there were many contenders for the top position, foremost of which were Governor Rotimi Amaechi of Rivers State and his counterpart in Edo State, Adams Oshiomole.

Original_Search_Title: The Man Professor Yemi Osinbajo
Headline: Nigeria: The Man Professor Yemi Osinbajo
Body_Text:
Lagos — Not many people know his face but his name surely rings a bell, especially in the last few days. Prof. Yemi Osinbajo has been mentioned severally in the media as the most favored candidate among those shortlisted for the post of vice president under the All Progressives Congress (APC) presidential ticket.
With his unveiling yesterday as the running mate to the APC presidential candidate General Muhammadu Buhari, people are now asking questions about Osinbajo's personality, pedigree and background.

Original_Search_Title: Meet Buhari's Running Mate, Yemi Osinbajo
Headline: Nigeria: Meet Buhari's Running Mate, Yemi Osinbajo
Body_Text:
Professor Yemi Osinbajo, Wednesday, emerged the running mate to the All Progressives Congress Presidential candidate, Gen. Muhammadu Buhari.
Here is a profile of the former Lagos State Attorney General and a Snr. Pastor in the Redeemed Christian Church of God(RCCG).

Original_Search_Title: Buhari Formally Presents Osinbajo As APC Presidential Running Mate
Headline: Nigeria: Buhari Formally Presents Osinbajo As APC Presidential Running Mate
Body_Text:
The presidential candidate of the All Progressives Congress, APC, Muhammadu Buhari has formally presented Yemi Osinbajo, a professor of law and former attorney general and commissioner for Justice in Lagos state, as his running mate.
Making the presentation at the national secretariat of the APC in Abuja, Mr. Buhari said he sought advice from within and outside the party before making his choice.

Original_Search_Title: Kabbe On Early Flood Alert
Headline: Namibia: Kabbe On Early Flood Alert
Body_Text:
Windhoek — Residents of flood-prone Kabbe are warned to prepare for a possible early flood as the Zambezi River continues to rise above normal levels for this time of the year.
The level of the Zambezi River on Monday stood at 0.59m compared to 0.66m recorded in the same period last year.

Original_Search_Title: Sheila Ndyanabangi - I Would Marry Nathan Again and Again
Headline: Uganda: Sheila Ndyanabangi - I Would Marry Nathan Again and Again
Body_Text:
Dr Sheila Ndyanabangi, is a marriage counsellor who also used to appear on NTV's Let's Talk. Ndyanabangi is also the principal medical officer for Mental Health and Control of Substance Abuse at the ministry of Health. Since Quick Talk found her talking about the proposed anti-tobacco bill...
How is that bill treating you?

Original_Search_Title: Hell Beckons for Ousted Bigwigs
Headline: Zimbabwe: Hell Beckons for Ousted Bigwigs
Body_Text:
IT was Aristotle the ancient Greek philosopher who said, "politicians have no leisure because they are always aiming at something beyond political life itself, power and glory, or happiness".
The quotation aptly sums up the situation of politicians who are seen as unique breed.

Original_Search_Title: NGO Has Gone Rogue and Must to Be Stopped
Headline: Kenya: NGO Has Gone Rogue and Must to Be Stopped
Body_Text:
Today's column is written from a rather cold New York City where I am attending the 13th Session of the Assembly of States Parties to the ICC. I am part of a four-person delegation from the Kenya Citizens Coalition, an NGO-network that is interested in ensuring that the right narrative about Kenya is told, especially around the International Criminal Court and its work in Kenya.
My colleagues are Ronald Osumba, a former deputy presidential candidate and currently the head of the social platform Onward, which conducted an audit of the status of the 2007-08 post-election violence victims. This audit shows that though we do not have a perfect picture, substantial work has been done over the last seven years by the government. Njenga Mwangi was the first legal representatives for victims immediately after the PEV and engaged more than 4,000 direct victims. Lydia Kwamboka is the coalition's legal secretary.

Original_Search_Title: Pastors Are Planning a Major Robbery in the Churches in January
Headline: Nigeria: Pastors Are Planning a Major Robbery in the Churches in January
Body_Text:
Jesus berated the mercenary religious leaders of biblical days. He told them: "'It is written, my house shall be called a house of prayer,' but you have made it a 'den of thieves.'" (Matthew 21:13).
This word remains pertinent to pastors in the churches of today. They come up with all kinds of schemes designed to squeeze money out of their church-members. They search the scriptures, looking for quotable quotes that can be used to make merchandise of men. Some of their favourites are those scriptures dealing with giving first-fruits to priests.

Original_Search_Title: Omo Collect and Win Promotion Ends With Excitement
Headline: Uganda: Omo Collect and Win Promotion Ends With Excitement
Body_Text:
Kampala — As a brand, OMO has evolved over the years and with each step, they have made more effective products. Since 1962, OMO has set a consumer trend built on trust, quality and innovation.
In regards to that, the OMO Fast Action launch in May this year, Unilever Uganda embarked on a countrywide consumer activation campaign dabbed 'OMO Collect and Win' promotion where consumers were given the chance to win 10 new Toyota Premio vehicles, motorbikes, washing machines among other exciting prizes.

Original_Search_Title: Why the Wachagga Travel Home for Christmas Celebration
Headline: Tanzania: Why the Wachagga Travel Home for Christmas Celebration
Body_Text:
IF there is a relative I often remember, even after her death fifteen years ago, then that person is my grandmother Amalia Ngawaia. This grandma was a neat disciplined lady, who was brought up by Dutch missionaries at Kilema Catholic Church in Moshi rural district, Kilimanjaro region in early 1900s.
She understood some words in German. In May 1996, two days before I left for my studies in Rome Italy, I went to bid her farewell at her house in Singa Kibosho.

Original_Search_Title: Celebrating the Power of Really Good Food
Headline: Kenya: Celebrating the Power of Really Good Food
Body_Text:
I watched a movie recently and at the risk of reverting to my days as a film reviewer I'm going to try and tell you a little about it while trying not to give away too much of the plot for those who may not have seen the '100 Foot Journey'.
The film revolves around the love for food and touches on culture, immigration, romance and racism -- all topics that I enjoy discussing and reading about.

Original_Search_Title: There Is Fine Line Between Sexy and Vulgar Music Videos - Adasa Cookey
Headline: Nigeria: There Is Fine Line Between Sexy and Vulgar Music Videos - Adasa Cookey
Body_Text:
Adasa Cookey may sound like a nickname but it is his real name. He is one of the most sought after music video directors in the country and you will be surprised that this 33-year-old young man finds fun in odd things order than partying like his mates. In this interview with Senior Reporter, Ifeoma Ononye, he speaks about his work, his hobbies and why nudity in music videos can be a turn off
Is Adasa Cookey your real name?

Original_Search_Title: Clearing Drainage Needs Concerted Effort
Headline: Zimbabwe: Clearing Drainage Needs Concerted Effort
Body_Text:
MANY storm water drains in Harare are partly or fully blocked with rubbish and many surface drains in the suburbs are silted or have been landscaped and filled by those seeking to extend their gardens to the road. So, each time there is a storm we face floods.In Harare city centre, there are damaged or vandalised covers to catch-pits, those entrances to the underground drains, and these have to be repaired.
But the major problem is that most catch-pits are filled with garbage, as are some near surface drains under pavements and kerbs that were designed to take water to a catch-pit.

Original_Search_Title: Nkayi Mortuary Not Working for the Past Six Years
Headline: Zimbabwe: Nkayi Mortuary Not Working for the Past Six Years
Body_Text:
NKAYI Rural District Hospital mortuary has not been operating properly for the past six years, forcing villagers to resort to traditional methods of preserving bodies.
The more well-to-do villagers have to travel over 120 kilometres to the nearest mortuary facility.

Original_Search_Title: Would-Be Redeemer Becomes Destroyer
Headline: Uganda: Would-Be Redeemer Becomes Destroyer
Body_Text:
I was born in a family of royal lineage.
That was the reason my paternal aunties were dissatisfied with my mother because she gave birth to only two children; my brother, Mulangira Junior, and I. I was the first born. This was unacceptable because a woman of my mother's time was supposed to give her husband seven or more children.

Original_Search_Title: Nutrition - Snacks With Staying Power
Headline: Rwanda: Nutrition - Snacks With Staying Power
Body_Text:
If you're fighting the urge to nap on your desk after lunch, then maybe that midday cup of coffee just isn't cutting it. Try these;
Lemon water:

Original_Search_Title: Addressing the Plight of Nigeria's Out of School Children
Headline: Nigeria: Addressing the Plight of Nigeria's Out of School Children
Body_Text:
Abuja — The plight of children like five-year old Hafsat, who is of school age but are not in school, has been a recurring debate among concerned individuals and groups.
Every morning while other kids of her age were busy in school, Hafsat, who lives in Masaka, a satellite town close to the federal capital territory (FCT) is usually seen in the neighbourhood gathering wood.

Original_Search_Title: NAFDAC Prosecutes Trader Over Production of Unregistered Custard
Headline: Nigeria: NAFDAC Prosecutes Trader Over Production of Unregistered Custard
Body_Text:
A Federal High Court in Lagos on Tuesday granted bail to a trader, Christopher Nduka, 42, standing trial for alleged production of unregistered custard.
Nduka, who is being prosecuted by the National Agency for Food, Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC), is facing a two-count charge of production of unregistered consumer product.

Original_Search_Title: School Memories - Mrs. Amanya's Rumoured Death
Headline: Rwanda: School Memories - Mrs. Amanya's Rumoured Death
Body_Text:
To say that we didn't get along with Mrs. Amanya is an understatement. We resented her and the feeling was mutual on her part. Every time she entered the classroom, we endured insults and spiteful remarks. In retaliation, we called her unpleasant nicknames and drew cartoons of her everywhere.
But the animosity worsened when on top of verbal abuse, she added physical torture; she started pulling our ears and slapping our faces. We tried to get rid of her by locking her out of the classroom but we ended up spending the day cleaning bathrooms and toilets. We posted complaints in suggestion boxes but received no response.

Original_Search_Title: Heavy Rains Leave Families Homeless
Headline: Tanzania: Heavy Rains Leave Families Homeless
Body_Text:
Bukoba — OVER 200 families in six villages in Kamachumu Division in Muleba district were rendered homeless following heavy rains which were accompanied by strong winds.
The Ward Councillor for Kamachumu, Mr Dastan Mutagahywa, said heavy rains which lasted for two hours on Monday morning unroofed several houses in six villages namely Kafunjo, Ilogero na Bushagaro, Bulembo, Kamachumu and Itongo.

Original_Search_Title: Motlaagana to Curb Unemployment
Headline: Botswana: Motlaagana to Curb Unemployment
Body_Text:
Serowe — The launch of Motlaagana Wa Banana Society textile project will go a long way in promoting the country's textile business, says Teemane Manufacturing Company human resources manager, Mr Tuwane Molatedi.
Speaking during the launch of the project by Botswana Post in Serowe recently, Mr Molatedi said not only would the project provide the much needed job skills to the youth but would also mould them into becoming responsible citizens. Botswana Post donated machinery and a vehicle valued at P280 430 to Motlaagana Wa Banana Society.

Original_Search_Title: 'Wazembe' Raises Storm At Ruto ICC Hearing
Headline: Kenya: 'Wazembe' Raises Storm At Ruto ICC Hearing
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Defence lawyer Karim Khan on Tuesday submitted video and newspaper clippings to demonstrate that Deputy President William Ruto did not incite Kalenjins during the memorial of freedom fighter Koitalel arap Samoei as alleged by the 27th prosecution witness at the International Criminal Court (ICC).
In one of the video clips played in court, Ruto was heard urging Kalenjins to welcome former President Mwai Kibaki, his wife and other guests attending the ceremony on October 19, 2005.

Original_Search_Title: Shattering Taboos On Menstruation, One Club At a Time
Headline: Rwanda: Shattering Taboos On Menstruation, One Club At a Time
Body_Text:
Rwandan high school students work to combat taboos surrounding menstruation and learn about local, sustainable methods of sanitary pad production.
The bus ride resounds with the shouting and laughter of 22 teenage secondary school students as it jostles over the village's rutted dirt roads. The students--all in their sixth and final year of secondary, or Rwandan high school--belong to SHE Club at Duha Secondary School. SHE Club, sponsored by Sustainable Health Enterprise, or SHE, is a social venture that manufactures low-cost menstrual pads out of banana leaf fibre

Original_Search_Title: Police Impound Counterfeit Goods in Kigali Shops
Headline: Rwanda: Police Impound Counterfeit Goods in Kigali Shops
Body_Text:
Police have impounded illicit and counterfeit goods worth Rwf14.5 million and arrested three people in connection with the goods.
The goods, impounded from various shops in Kigali, include 170 toner cartoons for printer, bed nets, drugs, computer sets, drinks, body oil, among others.

Original_Search_Title: Mining for a Dime
Headline: Uganda: Mining for a Dime
Body_Text:
He is all muddy and sweaty but he is in a jovial mood. Everest Ainemujuni, 34, is searching for gold in Mijera stream, Karembe Parish in Bihanga Sub County, in Buhweju District.
Squatting around the opencast mine filled up with water, Ainemujuni picks up a plastic basin, his most important piece of equipment. He is flanked by eight other men equipped with hand hoes for digging the mine, iron bars or pick axes to hit rocks, spades to scoop soil and basins from which they separate impurities from the gold deposits.

Original_Search_Title: Leadership Person of the Year 2014 - Chief Olusegun Obasanjo
Headline: Nigeria: Leadership Person of the Year 2014 - Chief Olusegun Obasanjo
Body_Text:
Our choice of Obasanjo was a difficult one, but we were compelled by the need to focus on the message rather than the messenger.
We are not unmindful of the fact that his choice would come to many of our readers as a surprise, if not disappointment. Yet it is precisely because we have held his feet to the fire whenever we deemed it necessary that we have the courage to acknowledge his message.

Original_Search_Title: Firing Mbabazi Small Issue, Says Museveni
Headline: Uganda: Firing Mbabazi Small Issue, Says Museveni
Body_Text:
On Saturday, December 6, Capital FM aired its Capital Gang show with President Museveni as the special guest.
Museveni discussed a wide range of issues including internal wrangles in the NRM and the party's forthcoming delegates' conference, corruption in the roads and railways sector and his political future.

Original_Search_Title: It Is No Longer Business As Usual for Sovereign Bond Issuers
Headline: Ethiopia: It Is No Longer Business As Usual for Sovereign Bond Issuers
Body_Text:
The recent move taken by the Government of Ethiopia to issue a sovereign bond in the international market has been seen by many as a bold decision.
Experts believe that the main reason for issuing the bond is to raise funds to finance mega projects. The absence of a vibrant financial sector in Ethiopia and fear of running down foreign exchange reserve are cited as rationales behind the move. In that regard, the government is hoping to acquire the funds to fulfill the colossal financial requirements of the Growth and Transformation Plan (GTP). Eyob Tesfaye (PhD) is a macro economist who served as head of the Public Financial Institutions Supervising Agency at the National Bank of Ethiopia. He is currently team leader, Economic Growth and Poverty Reduction at the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP). Asrat Seyoum of The Reporter sat down with Eyob at Elilly International Hotel located off Guinea Conakry Street behind UNECA to talk about the possible risks and the final results of issuing a sovereign bond in the complex international financial market. The views expressed in this interview only reflects the views of Eyob. It does not reflect the views of the UNDP or other UN agencies. Excerpts:

Original_Search_Title: Our Wonderful House Help Is Away...

She has been a hand that tends to a huge chunk of our home affairs and we all love her to bits. Rachael has an amazing personality. Her character and mannerisms are just awesome. Whenever she is away, our family is never the same.
I have had quarrels with her on several occasions, but it's normal for human beings to have conflicts now and then. Nevertheless, it has never ruined my relationship with her. We have maintained our respect and the period we've lived with her have been nothing but excellent. My incredible twins have come to love her as much. Literally, she has been their mother. She was at the hospital when I gave birth. Apart from the doctors at the delivery unit of the Kenyatta National Hospital, Rachael was among the very first two people to hold the newborns. She was there alongside my adoring husband to receive the incredible duo.

Original_Search_Title: The Right to Fair Hearing of an Elected Politician Must Be Respected in an Impeachment Proceedin
Headline: Nigeria: The Right to Fair Hearing of an Elected Politician Must Be Respected in an Impeachment Proceeding
Body_Text:
On 21st November 2014, the Supreme Court of the Federal Republic of Nigeria held that the impeachment of Alhaji Sani Abubakar Danladi, the erstwhile Deputy Governor of Taraba State ("the Appellant"), by the Taraba State House of Assembly on 4th October 2012, was null and void. According to the Supreme Court, this was because the impeachment panel that investigated the allegations of gross misconduct against the Deputy Governor infringed on his right to fair hearing. The Supreme Court therefore ordered that the Appellant should resume immediately as the Deputy Governor of Taraba State.
The decision of the Supreme Court is a landmark one which would have a tremendous effect on impeachment jurisprudence in Nigeria. This is because the issues addressed by the judgment of the Court are very important in understanding the proper procedure for impeaching an elected office holder. We shall therefore highlight some of these issues in this paper with a view to contributing to the ongoing debate on the impeachment of elected officeholders in Nigeria.

Original_Search_Title: New CJN, New Way Forward for Justice Delivery?
Headline: Nigeria: New CJN, New Way Forward for Justice Delivery?
Body_Text:
Just as our country prepares for elections next February and with all the political drama playing out on a daily basis, a new Chief Justice of Nigeria, Justice Mahmud Mohammed was sworn in on November 20th. He will guide the nation's judiciary till November 10, 2016. His gentle mien reminds me of Emperor Haile Selassie of Ethiopia who was referred to as the Lion of Judah. I have no doubt that in the two years that Justice Mohammed has, he will not only make his mark but may well prove to be our 'lion of the judiciary'. He has already hit the ground running and has said that he will sustain the judicial reforms introduced by his predecessor, Justice Aloma Mukhtar. He was specific about ensuring judicial autonomy, improved deployment of technology in court processes and promoting the use of Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) processes. He could not have said it better as he particularly singled out ADR as his preference for addressing the problem of delay in court processes, as the increasingly frustrating process of litigation has become ever more time-consuming, complex, expensive and cumbersome.
Focusing on the appropriate technology will assuredly render speedier and more effective and efficient justice delivery: to see judges still recording proceedings in long hand in this day and age is a truly worrisome sight and something that in itself contributes to the delay currently experienced, not to mention so many other factors that further add to making the entire court process from beginning to the end an extremely burdensome one. Lawyers are more often than not frustrated by the long delays in the courts. In some of the state High Courts as far back as in 2000, pending cases were in the region of 40,000; and one can barely imagine what the situation must be like now. It is currently taking an average of 5-9 years for a contested case to move from filing to judgment. Land matters are taking 7-8 years if not more, commercial cases 3-5 years and family cases 2-5 years. In the Family Court Division judges still treat applications under the Child Rights Law with long adjournments between hearing and the time of delivering the ruling, something that should be anathema to the court, particularly considering the sensitive nature of the subject matter under adjudication. The situation is not any better in the apex courts either, as everywhere the courts are generally overburdened and bursting at the seams.

Original_Search_Title: Somalia - the Quintessential Banana Republic Creeping in Once Again
Headline: Somalia - the Quintessential Banana Republic Creeping in Once Again
Body_Text:
In the Somalia republic, unlike in other parts of African countries and elsewhere, clannish psyche largely determines the power and the impending clan identity that keeps the political rift within them so apart.
In 1991 President Said Barre was overthrown by opposing clans. But they failed to agree on a replacement and plunged the country into lawlessness and clan warfare. Afterwards, Somalia turns to be a global icon of a failed state. Total submissiveness to a certain selfish ideology camouflaged with a vague and unambiguous self-imposed interpretation contrary to the "ideal Islamic school of thoughts" intentionally destined to sharpen the divisions and amplified further the uncertainty of realizing unity of purpose that would have otherwise paved the way to a stable and democratic state in Somalia long before all hell broke loose. All international communities' attempts to lay feet a stable government in Somalia did not materialize in the past and all endeavors were pulled down by the little known Mogadishu corporatocracy (Mega-business owners).
