I knew about the rotational transfer of leadership between Christians and Muslims, as conceived by President Julius Nyerere, and that he, President Kikwete, had succeeded a Christian. Now at the citadel of his country's political leadership, a man with a military background, and who had served as MP and Foreign Affairs Minister, President Kikwete, could in no way be described as 'little-known,' rather he was a child of a detribalised society; a country where tribe was not the alpha and omega of politics.

Original_Search_Title: Tambuwal - Whither Our Constitutional Conventions
Headline: Nigeria: Tambuwal - Whither Our Constitutional Conventions
Body_Text:
The ancient Greeks defined democracy as government of the people, for the people and by the people. However it is not always easy to involve all the people in government all the time.
It will be inefficient and unwieldy. Consequently, the concept of democracy has been modified to mean responsible and representative government. By this arrangement the will of the majority of the people is expressed through election of representatives, to whom a select group of persons charged with government on behalf of the people is responsible even if this group is also elected by the people. In-built into a system of representative and responsible government are checks and balance conceived by Montesquieu as a mechanism to prevent abuse of power. Apart from dividing power into three (3) arms of government the system of checks may also divide power among different levels of government such as in a federation. Now, the constitutional framework even where there is a written constitution is usually tied together by certain unwritten principles and conventions. A convention is an unwritten practice which political actors feel they are bound to follow for a normative reason and which is not enforceable by the courts. Such conventions are recognised as being part of the constitution. Unwritten principles are values which form the fundamental framework of the written constitution. To assist us with appreciation of the matter we shall discuss in little detail the Canadian constitutional experience to enable us draw persuasive inferences when we discuss the position under the Nigerian Constitution. Another reason for the foray into the jurisprudence of another country is the dearth of authority directly on the point in our jurisprudence.

Original_Search_Title: Reinstated Taraba Deputy Governor Takes Charge
Headline: Nigeria: Reinstated Taraba Deputy Governor Takes Charge
Body_Text:
In what may well change the political dynamics of 2015 election in Taraba State, the Supreme Court yesterday nullified the 2012 impeachment of the state's Deputy Governor, Alhaji Sani Abubakar Danladi and ordered his reinstatement as acting governor with immediate effect.
The court also ordered the hitherto acting governor, Alhaji Garba Umar to vacate office immediately. Danladi was impeached by the state House of Assembly on the 4th of October, 2012 on the grounds of gross misconduct and Umar appointed and sworn-in to replace him by Governor Danbaba Suntai. Few weeks after, Suntai was badly injured in an air crash and Umar became acting governor. Danladi arrived Government House yesterday evening and immediately took over despite the fact that he was yet to be sworn in. He also pledged his unflinching loyalty to the recuperating governor of the state. Addressing the people of the state at the Exco chamber of the Government House, Danladi said he would demonstrate his loyalty to Suntai not only by words but action.

Original_Search_Title: Presidency - Will the Ijaw Ever Support Ndigbo?
Headline: Nigeria: Presidency - Will the Ijaw Ever Support Ndigbo?
Body_Text:
In a famous article deservedly syndicated in the Nigerian newspapers as well as social media entitled "Ndigbo and support for Jonathan", C. Don Adinuba, a respected public affairs and communication consultant, wondered why Governor Serieka Dickson of Bayelsa State has always been enthusiastic to express gratitude to Vice President Namadi Sambo's for "supporting our son, President Goodluck Jonathan", but has never , like Ijaw leader Edwin Clark, uttered a word of appreciation to the Igbo people who have provided the bulwark of support to Jonathan. Adinuba asked why has the situation been like this, even when it is axiomatic that Sambo, who lost his ward in the 2011 general elections to the opposition Congress for Progressive Change, has added little or no electoral value to the Jonathan presidency, unlike the Igbo who have supported this administration more than any other ethnic group in the country, including those in Jonathan's Niger Delta region. I have waited for a moth since the article was published to see if any person could answer the Adinuba exceedingly thoughtful poser, but couldn't find anyone.
The Igbo political elite claim that their unflinching support for Jonathan without demanding the development of the Igboland in any way, including the rebuilding of collapsed infrastructure like federal roads in the Southeast, is targeted at achieving a rapprochement with the ijaw who have over the years displayed unpatrolled hostility towards the Igbo whom they accuse of internal colonialism in the First Republic. If this official explanation is believable--though many analysts believe that the support for Jonathan by the Igbo politicians has more to do with personal gains than anything else--then we should expect a reciprocal action from the ijaw political leaders.Rapprochement is not a one-way traffic. Parties on both sides of the divide must show the spirit of mea culpa, or atonement of sins committed against the other party. Whereas it is possible that the Igbo people oppressed or displayed a profound lack of sensitivity to the Ijaw and other minorities in the defunct Eastern Nigerian Region, it is also true that the Ijaw have hurt Igbo interests in an awful manner.

Original_Search_Title: Tambuwal - Saint or Sinner?
Headline: Nigeria: Tambuwal - Saint or Sinner?
Body_Text:
This piece was inspired by two recent developments: one was the decision of the Speaker of the House of Representatives Aminu Tambuwal to formally decamp to the APC from the PDP and two, was the news that some members of the House of Representatives had bought the N27.5m APC presidential nomination forms for Speaker Tambuwal to vie to be the party's flag bearer in next year's presidential election.
I had already completed the draft of this article when news came that Tambuwal had 'withdrawn' from the race. According to Tambuwal "Having consulted widely, taking into consideration the concerns of some elders of the party, I have decided to suspend my participation in the presidential contest for now. I have done so as a sacrifice for the cohesion and unity of the APC. I am suspending my participation in the presidential race for now because I do not have any inordinate ambition to occupy any office" (Vanguard 19 November 2014). I took special note of the fact that Tambuwal talked of "suspending" his candidacy "for now", not that he was withdrawing from the race.

Original_Search_Title: Defecting Speaker VS Vindictive President
Headline: Nigeria: Defecting Speaker VS Vindictive President
Body_Text:
As President Goodluck Jonathan of Nigeria clocked 57 years today, the nation witnessed one of the worst abuses of the nation's democracy in history. Members of parliament were teargassed and denied access into the House of Representatives chamber in order to prevent the speaker, Aminu Waziri Tambuwal, from presiding over the sitting.
For goodness' sake, value of the naira is not appreciating against the dollar and the price Uncle Ben's rice is not crashing against the price of Garri if the presidency allows thee speaker to preside over the sitting. Again, the speaker will not return to PDP because of this -- at least at the moment. But why are the ideals of our democracy toyed with in order to satiate one's vindictive ego?

Original_Search_Title: APC Will Defeat Jonathan in His Home State, Bayelsa - Oshiomhole

The Edo State Governor, Adams Oshiomhole, has challenged President Goodluck Jonathan to name the projects he and his party, the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, have executive for the benefit of the people of the state since he assumed office.
The governor also vowed that his party, the All progressives Congress, APC, would defeat Mr. Jonathan in his home state, Bayelsa, and other states controlled by the PDP in the South-South geo-political zones. The states are Delta, Cross River and Akwa Ibom.

Original_Search_Title: You Have No Scorecard to Show Nigerians, Oshiomhole Tells Jonathan
Headline: Nigeria: You Have No Scorecard to Show Nigerians, Oshiomhole Tells Jonathan
Body_Text:
Governor Adams Oshiomhole of Edo State has challenged President Goodluck Jonathan to show what his party, the Peoples Democratic Party has done in Edo State, saying the President only came go the stated to commission a resurfaced existing Ofosu-Benin axis of an expressway which was constructed by a military regime.
The Governor said the President's Sure-P programme is simply a Sure fraud where huge sums of money is handed to an individual to disturse at will without record, saying the war against corruption by the current government at the federal level can be likened to a gun that fires shots without casualties.

Original_Search_Title: Hold Leaders Accountable, Kagame Tells Rwandans
Headline: Rwanda: Hold Leaders Accountable, Kagame Tells Rwandans
Body_Text:
President Paul Kagame has urged Rwandans never to settle for less nor let go of their right to demand accountability from their leaders.
Addressing residents of Gasabo District during a visit to Jabana Sector yesterday, Kagame said leaders are there to serve the people and the latter should never hesitate to hold accountable leaders who have failed to deliver.

Original_Search_Title: Security Is the Foundation of All That We Have Achieved - President Kagame
Headline: Rwanda: Security Is the Foundation of All That We Have Achieved - President Kagame
Body_Text:
Jabana — Today, President Paul Kagame visited Jabana Sector in Gasabo District as part of the presidential routine citizen outreach.
Addressing thousands of residents who came to meet him at Bweramvura cell grounds, President Kagame highlighted the importance of security for national development and asked them to keep the momentum:

Original_Search_Title: Demand Accountability - Kagame
Headline: Rwanda: Demand Accountability - Kagame
Body_Text:
President Paul Kagame has called on Gasabo district residents to always demand quality services in education and healthcare, as well as access to electricity and clean water, among other areas.
He was today addressing thousands of residents who had gathered at Bweramvura ground in Jabana Sector, Gasabo to receive him during his visit to the area today.

Original_Search_Title: President Kagame Visits Jabana Sector
Headline: Rwanda: President Kagame Visits Jabana Sector
Body_Text:
Thousands of residents in Kigali City's Gasabo District are gathered at Bweramvura cell grounds in Jabana Sector to receive President Paul Kagame who is visiting them today as part of the Head of State's routine meetings with the people.
Jabana has gone from being one of the few areas without electricity to having 16 percent of households enjoying electricity within the last three years.

Original_Search_Title: 2015 Elections - Tambuwal As Game Changer
Headline: Nigeria: 2015 Elections - Tambuwal As Game Changer
Body_Text:
THE Right Honourable Aminu Tambuwal, incumbent Speaker of Nigerian's House of Representative, has undoubtedly established himself as a master of masters in the game of politics, albeit the Nigeria's muddy waters of politics.
The truth of the matter as evidenced, so far, by the way he emerged as Speaker of the House of Representatives in 2011 and the master stroke presentation of decamping from the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, to the All Progressives, APC, shall remain a good case study of political manoeuvring. Even the late great Kingsley Ozumba Mbadiwe would have paid tribute to this young man on his political sagacity.

Original_Search_Title: MDC-T Attacks Former Colleagues for Targeting Tsvangirai
Headline: Zimbabwe: MDC-T Attacks Former Colleagues for Targeting Tsvangirai
Body_Text:
THE opposition MDC-T party has strongly condemned the public insults being hurled at its leader Morgan Tsvangirai by the rival MDC Renewal Team.
The party's response comes a week after the MDC Renewal Team de facto leader Tendai Biti labelled Tsvangirai the new "Mobutu Sese Seko, a reference to the much loathed former Zaire strongman who was overthrown in 1997.

Original_Search_Title: Strange Bananas Join Kenya's Bunch of Fakes
Headline: Kenya: Strange Bananas Join Kenya's Bunch of Fakes
Body_Text:
THERE has been a spike in recent decades in this country of diseases such as cancer and renal problems that involve both kidney damage and failure.
Many Kenyans have blamed increasingly modern lifestyles and diets for these "new" ailments.

Original_Search_Title: Traders Use Chemicals to Ripen Bananas
Headline: Kenya: Traders Use Chemicals to Ripen Bananas
Body_Text:
DID you know the banana you buy by the roadside could be laced with harmful chemicals that can cause cancer?
Unscrupulous merchants have devised a quicker method to hasten the ripening of fruits. Sadly, this fruit-ripening chemical - calcium carbide - is carcinogenic, it can cause cancer.

Original_Search_Title: Trade and Investment - Sino-Tanzania Relations Growing Stronger
Headline: Tanzania: Trade and Investment - Sino-Tanzania Relations Growing Stronger
Body_Text:
"TANZANIA and China are good all-weather friends," said Chinese Vice- President, Li Yuanchao, during a reception in Beijing recently to commemorate the 50th anniversary of the diplomatic relations between the two countries.
Good all-weather friends the two countries indeed are as attested by the long standing friendship and cooperation that have withstood the test of time, thriving on the strong foundations built by the founding fathers of the nations, namely Mwalimu Julius Nyerere and Mao Zedong.

Original_Search_Title: Igwebuike - Presidency: Will Ijaw Support Ndigbo?
Headline: Nigeria: Igwebuike - Presidency: Will Ijaw Support Ndigbo?
Body_Text:
IN an article in the Nigerian newspapers as well as social media entitled "Ndigbo and support for Jonathan", C. Don Adinuba, a respected public affairs and communication consultant, wondered why Governor Serieka Dickson of Bayelsa State has always been enthusiastic to express gratitude to Vice President Namadi Sambo for "supporting our son, President Goodluck Jonathan", but has never , like Ijaw leader Edwin Clark, uttered a word of appreciation to the Igbo people who have provided the bulwark of support to Jonathan.
Adinuba asked why has the situation been like this, even when it is axiomatic that Sambo, who lost his ward in the 2011 general elections to the opposition Congress for Progressive Change, has added little or no electoral value to the Jonathan presidency, unlike the Igbo who have supported this administration more than any other ethnic group in the country, including those in Jonathan's Niger Delta region. I have waited since the article was published to see if any person could answer the Adinuba's thoughtful poser, but couldn't find anyone.

Original_Search_Title: Rate of Inflation Slows Down
Headline: Tanzania: Rate of Inflation Slows Down
Body_Text:
INFLATION during the month of October, this year further decreased to 5.9 per cent from 6.6 per cent as recorded in September this year, the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) has said.
Director of Population Census and Social Statistics with NBS, Mr Ephraim Kwesigabo, addressing journalists during a press conference in Dar es Salaam on Saturday said that the decrease is highly attributed to the reduction of prices of commodities.

Original_Search_Title: Food Value Chain Expo for Nairobi
Headline: Kenya: Food Value Chain Expo for Nairobi
Body_Text:
An expo targeting the whole food industry value chain, from production though to packaging and logistics, is slated for Nairobi in just over a week from today.
This will be the second edition, set for November 18-20 at the KICC, where participants are to share latest trends in the industry and showcase new technology.

Original_Search_Title: Is ODM in the Final Death Throes
Headline: Kenya: Is ODM in the Final Death Throes
Body_Text:
The Orange Democratic Movement, once a political juggernaught, has lately been in the news for all the wrong reasons. In a strange twist of events it ejected and rejected its CEO, Magerer Langat by frog-marching him out of Orange House in the full glare of ODM triumvirate leaders Raila Amolo Odinga and Stephen Kalonzo Musyoka. Langat, an ODM diehard had sacrificed and forsaken his personal, political and community interests in favour of ODM only to be driven out like a wild rabid Dingo.
What went wrong and how did ODM come to this? Have they run out of leadership ideas then stagnated and regressed to the Dark Ages? Why is a party replete with senior political scientists and senior legal counsel, inter alia behaving worse than a primitive village council? Have they taken leave of their senses?

Original_Search_Title: United Nations Envoy Power On UN Peacekeeping Reform
Headline: Africa: United Nations Envoy Power On UN Peacekeeping Reform
Publisher: United States Department of State (Washington, DC)
Body_Text:
New York — Remarks by Ambassador Samantha Power, U.S. Permanent Representative to the United Nations, at the American Enterprise Institute on UN Peacekeeping Reform
Hello everybody. I have come here today to talk about UN peacekeeping. There is a lot going on in the world right now. Elie Wiesel once - quite recently - shared with me the following thought: "The winds of madness are blowing." And I know that's how it feels. But the urgent, critical issues on our plate should not divert us from an important fact, which is that the United States has a vital interest - and a critical role to play - in strengthening peacekeeping to meet demands that peacekeepers are currently struggling to meet around the world.

Original_Search_Title: Etim Esin - My Greatest Regret As Footballer
Headline: Nigeria: Etim Esin - My Greatest Regret As Footballer
Body_Text:
Former Green Eagles midfielder, Etim Esin, in this interview with Augustine Akhilomen sheds light on his life as a footballer, what went wrong and how racism in Belgium halted his football career.
You were a fantastic player in your days with the national team, but it seems your talent was not exploited to the fullest.

Original_Search_Title: Civil Society in Rare Show of Unity With Police
Headline: Kenya: Civil Society in Rare Show of Unity With Police
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Civil society organizations under the umbrella of 'Mwamko Mpya Uongozi Bora' are calling for justice for the more than 20 Administration Police Officers killed in Kapedo, Turkana County at the weekend.
In a show of solidarity with the slain officers, the Non-Governmental Organisations marched to honour the fallen heroes saying the perpetrators must be brought to book.

Original_Search_Title: Open Letter to the Postmaster General
Headline: Kenya: Open Letter to the Postmaster General
Body_Text:
It is that time of the year again when students sit their national examinations. They need our support more than anything else.
I have two beautiful nieces sitting their Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education examinations.

Original_Search_Title: UN Expert Calls On Member States to Accelerate Fight Against Racism in Sports
Headline: Africa: UN Expert Calls On Member States to Accelerate Fight Against Racism in Sports
Body_Text:
An independent United Nations human rights expert has called on the world body's Member States and international sports federations "to harness the unique potential of sports" in the global fight against racism, urging them to promote an overall message of equality.
"Sports have demystified racial superiority discourses, making them an important and practical instrument for combating racism," the UN Special Rapporteur on racism, Mutuma Ruteere, told the UN General Assembly body dealing with social, humanitarian and cultural issues (Third Committee) yesterday during the presentation of his latest report .

Original_Search_Title: How Supplementation Made Two Women's Pregnancies Easy
Headline: Uganda: How Supplementation Made Two Women's Pregnancies Easy
Body_Text:
Tamara Nantale, 27, surprised a number of guests at her baby shower on October 4 - a few days before she gave birth - because at a time when many pregnant women are tired and low on energy, Nantale was so energetic; her energetic levels exceeded those of some of her non-pregnant guests at the baby shower.
One guest, so used to seeing weary pregnant women in their final trimester push and support themselves by the back, asked how Nantale had managed to remain so energetic.

Original_Search_Title: Re-Emergence of Super Govs in PDP
Headline: Nigeria: Re-Emergence of Super Govs in PDP
Body_Text:
Lagos — At the height of Nigerian governors' influence in May, 2013 - just before the contentious chairmanship election - there probably wasn't a more coherent and powerful political bloc in the country. Indeed, recognition of this point, according to analysts, precipitated attempts by the Presidency to whittle down their powers. As it turned out, it was a gargantuan task, executed at great cost to the ruling party whose fortunes have been on the decline ever since. However, the political wheel appears to be turning in favour of the remaining governors in the party as they get more concessions by the day from a desperate president whose eyes are set on 2015 - the crucial electoral year which, bookmakers have claimed, would decide Nigeria's future direction. Recent developments and information from sources appear to lend credence to reports which purport that President Goodluck Jonathan may have conceded control of the party at the state level to the governors whose interests would now override those of other stakeholders, especially in the selection of candidates for elective offices. This would seem to run again the earlier position of the party under former Bauchi governor, Adamu Muazu.
Muazu's avowed policy:

Original_Search_Title: Jonathan, Peter, Segun & Aminu - Parallax Rhapsodies
Headline: Nigeria: Jonathan, Peter, Segun & Aminu - Parallax Rhapsodies
Body_Text:
President Goodluck Jonathan, former Anambra State Governor Peter Obi, Ondo State Governor Olusegun Mimiko and outgoing Speaker of the House of Representatives, Aminu Tambuwal have something in common: the last three all recently dumped their respective parties that brought them to power for other parties and the first reserved the sole right to receive or send-forth with roses or scorpions as the case may be.
While the Presidency and leading lights of PDP organized a red carpet reception for Peter and Segun, the same presidency is deploying the coercive agencies of State to intimidate and harass Aminu for moving in the opposite direction; yet, you'll hear them shout ad nauseam that it isn't about politics and 2015!

Original_Search_Title: Fruit Prices Up By 50 Percent in E. Province
Headline: Rwanda: Fruit Prices Up By 50 Percent in E. Province
Body_Text:
Fruit and vegetable prices have skyrocketed in Eastern Province on an annual basis.
A mini-survey conducted by Business Times found that prices of cabbage, carrots, spinach and passion fruits, have increased by up to 50 per cent compared to those of a similar period last year.

Original_Search_Title: RC Gama Is Right On Alcohol Ban in Kilimanjaro But...
Headline: Tanzania: RC Gama Is Right On Alcohol Ban in Kilimanjaro But...
Body_Text:
THE Kilimanjaro Regional Commissioner, Mr Leonidas Gama, has banned the sale of alcohol in the region during work hours.
Without specifying the exact hours that residents in the region should abandon frequenting 'mbege' or 'viroba' joints, Mr Gama insisted that any person caught selling or drinking during work hours, shall be arrested and taken to court.

Original_Search_Title: APGA Leadership Still Licking Wounds of Obi's Exit
Headline: Nigeria: APGA Leadership Still Licking Wounds of Obi's Exit
Body_Text:
Onitsha — When Willie Obiano was sworn in as governor of Anambra State on March 17, 2014, there was the optimism that Anambra will be an Eldorado in a short time to come. This is because before then, erstwhile Governor Peter Obi had said in several public functions that out of power, he would devote more time to assist the state from the centre.
"Obiano will be in Anambra State midwifing the state, while I will be in Abuja giving him the covering fire", he had said. Everybody believed the then governor, especially with the spectacular commanding height Anambra reached with donor agencies and how he managed to steer the state to prosperity, despite poor internally generated revenue and governing the state under a minority All Progressives Grand Alliance (APGA).

Original_Search_Title: Why I Want to Succeed Fashola - Ikuforiji
Headline: Nigeria: Why I Want to Succeed Fashola - Ikuforiji
Body_Text:
Few hours after formally joining the governorship race last week, the Speaker of the Lagos State of Assembly, Adeyemi Ikuforiji, met with journalists, where he outlined his vision for the state, the challenges ahead and why he feels he is in the best position to take over from Governor Babatunde Raji Fashola. Senior Reporter, AKINWUNMI KING, was there. Excerpts:
Why do you think you are qualified to be Babatubde Fashola's successor?

Original_Search_Title: Today, Content Doesn't Sell in Music, 'Beats' Do
Headline: Nigeria: Today, Content Doesn't Sell in Music, 'Beats' Do
Body_Text:
'Nigerian musicians project very shameful lyrical contents in their song'
Today, content doesn't sell in music, 'beats' do.

Original_Search_Title: Lagos 2015 - Why Ambode Cannot Be Imposed On Lagos - Ikuforiji, House Speaker
Headline: Nigeria: Lagos 2015 - Why Ambode Cannot Be Imposed On Lagos - Ikuforiji, House Speaker
Body_Text:
IF election is conducted tomorrow, what are your chances?
Concerning my chances, there is no doubt that I will win on a landslide if primary election is conducted today in the APC. I am very sure that most of our party men are very interested in seeing Lagos governed by a man who understands party politics and who is passionate about the development of Lagos and its people. They know that as at today, there is nobody that can compete favourably against Ikuforiji as far as that is concerned.

Original_Search_Title: Tennis Teens Serve Into Dark Territory
Headline: Nigeria: Tennis Teens Serve Into Dark Territory
Body_Text:
Nigerian junior tennis players are wary of the bleak future that awaits them when they take the plunge into the professional circuit, which has not been rosy for their predecessors.
In the last two decades, Nigerian tennis has been on a free-fall, largely due to poor funding, scanty competitions, poor coaching and dwindling sponsorships for players to tour.

Original_Search_Title: Katikkiro Tightens Grip On Royal Family
Headline: Uganda: Katikkiro Tightens Grip On Royal Family
Body_Text:
With a row simmering between Buganda katikkiro and members of the royal family (Abalangira), Charles Peter Mayiga has moved to restrict princes' access to the Kabaka.
Addressing the Buganda Lukiiko (Parliament) at Bulange on Monday, Mayiga hinted he could enforce cultural norms that regulate the Kabaka's interaction with princes.

Original_Search_Title: 'Lagos Governorship Race Isn't for the Unserious'
Headline: Nigeria: 'Lagos Governorship Race Isn't for the Unserious'
Body_Text:
Adeyemi Ikuforiji, Speaker of the Lagos State House of Assembly, formally declared for the governor's position in the 2015 general elections, using the All Progressives Congress (APC) as his platform. The longest serving Speaker in the country spoke to journalists about his ambition and how to move the state to the next level of development. SEYE OLUMIDE reports.
WHAT informed and qualifies you to wanting to govern Lagos?

Original_Search_Title: NGO Trains 5,700 Elderly People On Rights
Headline: Tanzania: NGO Trains 5,700 Elderly People On Rights
Body_Text:
Karagwe — A NON-governmental organization, Saidia Wazee Karagwe (SAWAKA), has trained about 5,700 elderly citizens on their rights and how to claim them.
SAWAKA Co-ordinator, Mr Livingstone Byekwaso said elderly people in Kagera Region were facing a burden of caring for increasing number of orphans due to HIV/AIDS scourge.

Original_Search_Title: Savings Culture Credited With Changing Lives
Headline: Rwanda: Savings Culture Credited With Changing Lives
Body_Text:
For most of his active years, Deogratias Ngamijimana spent almost every penny he earned without saving for the next day.
The 40-year-old resident of Nyamirama Cell in Ngera Sector, Nyaruguru District, worked as a bicycle repairer for about ten years but doesn't remember getting anything tangible from the money he made.

Original_Search_Title: Rage At Cele Bus Stop in Lagos...
Headline: Nigeria: Rage At Cele Bus Stop in Lagos...
Body_Text:
THERE was heavy protest Monday at the popular Cele Bus Stop along Oshodi-Apapa Expressway, Lagos over the death of a bus conductor, who was reportedly crushed to death by a moving vehicle.
The bus conductor, an eyewitness said, was attempting to evade arrest by officials of the Lagos State Traffic Management Authority (LASTMA), when he mistakenly ran into the moving vehicle and was immediately crushed to death.

Original_Search_Title: I Want to Combine Serena's Tennis With Venus' Designs - - Oyin
Headline: Nigeria: I Want to Combine Serena's Tennis With Venus' Designs - - Oyin
Body_Text:
Rising tennis teenage sensation Oyin Quadre has spoken of her dream of combining Serena Williams' great tennis play with Venus Williams' fashion designs gift.
Oyin, a participant at the Chevron Tennis Clinic, which ended last weekend at the Lagos Lawn Tennis Club, told Sunday Vanguard Sports that her ultimate ambition in life was to become an internationally acclaimed tennis and fashion celebrity.

Original_Search_Title: Nigerians Should Brace for Turbulent Times Ahead - 2
Headline: Nigerians Should Brace for Turbulent Times Ahead - 2
Body_Text:
I think Nigeria and Nigerians should take caution. Serious caution. It is a trite fact that the GDP growth we boast about, the new super-size of the economy, the prosperity we sing about, has been greatly helped by the good fortune of crude oil and its consistent high prices, and nothing more.
It's our luck. But one cannot build a monument on a run of goodluck. What one should do with a run of good luck, is to save, to invest veeerry veeerry wisely, and anticipate the sudden disappearance of such luck. Like the saying goes 'the harder you work, the luckier you get'. Hard work plus good luck is what equates success. It is doubtful though, whether we can be said to have worked hard, or saved and invested wisely in the past 11 years! Have we found other sources of income in the event this one disappears? No, as much as we always bellyache about it in too many a seminar.

Original_Search_Title: Beware of Putinism
Headline: Nigeria: Beware of Putinism
Body_Text:
MY naïve little cousin during the Sochi winter Olympics did ask me ... "where is Russia? Is it in Africa?
Without hesitating, I vehemently replied, 'hell no, how would you think Russia is in Africa?' ignoring her age. 'Minutes later, I did feel sorry for my harsh response, but headlines like these "Congolese defender Christopher Samba banned for two matches by Russian Football Union for his response to racist abuse during a Russian league match," gives me justification for my reactions.

Original_Search_Title: One Out of 26 Bodies From Lake Kariba Disaster Retrieved
Headline: Zambia: One Out of 26 Bodies From Lake Kariba Disaster Retrieved
Body_Text:
Only one body out of the 26 people who drowned Friday in Lake Kariba, Gwembe area has so far been retrieved.
And the Zambia Army and police are struggling to retrieve other bodies and may fail due to lack of marine equipment.

Original_Search_Title: Federal Govt and Challenge of Purposefulness in the War Against Abduction
Headline: Nigeria: Federal Govt and Challenge of Purposefulness in the War Against Abduction
Body_Text:
Elated, could be the best way to describe the reaction of most Nigerians, even sympathizers all around the globe, when the news of the purported ongoing negotiation between the Nigerian government and the Boko Haram insurgents dropped in, more so as it concerned the abducted Chibok girls.
The source of the report even went further to inform anxious Nigerians that a deal had been struck for the girls to regain their freedom and was specific at announcing Monday, 20 October, 2014 as the very day that they would be physically handed over to the government.

Original_Search_Title: No Investment Is Bigger Than the Law
Headline: Zimbabwe: No Investment Is Bigger Than the Law
Body_Text:
The Chinese are here, the Russians came and now Britain is about to send its high-powered business delegation at the end of this month to explore investment opportunities that they shunned for over a decade.
These big economies are trying to outdo each other as they seek to get the nod in extracting untapped resources that Zimbabwe is endowed with, but that comes with its own challenges especially in environmental management.

Original_Search_Title: MP Pushes for Suspension of MV Victoria
Headline: Tanzania: MP Pushes for Suspension of MV Victoria
Body_Text:
Bukoba — A LEGISLATOR in Kagera Region has appealed for government intervention to suspend Mv Victoria from carrying passengers until it undergoes major rehabilitation.
The Member of Parliament for Muleba North, Mr Charles Mwijage, told reporters that the vessel's stability was pathetic endangering lives of passengers who depend on it.

Original_Search_Title: China Pacts to Bring Jobs Boom
Headline: Tanzania: China Pacts to Bring Jobs Boom
Body_Text:
Beijing — MORE than 77,300 jobs are expected to be created in Tanzania once over 500 Chinese projects worth 2,490.21 million US dollars registered in the country until December last year become operational.
Opening the Third China and Tanzania Business Forum on Thursday, President Jakaya Kikwete expressed his government's commitment to woo more Chinese investments as data indicate trade volume between the two countries in the past two years reached 1,595.16 million US dollars.

Original_Search_Title: Being Gay in Jo'burg - 'I Could Not Change My Feelings, So I Embraced Them'
Headline: South Africa: Being Gay in Jo'burg - 'I Could Not Change My Feelings, So I Embraced Them'
Body_Text:
Musa Mthethwa (30) sits behind a table, surrounded by nail products. He spends his days as a nail technician at the bustling Carlton Centre, in the heart of the Johannesburg CBD. Mthethwa, who has been openly gay for 15 years, spoke with PONTSHO PILANE about the daily negotiations he must perform in a society that still discriminates against gay people.
I am originally from eSikhawini, near Richards Bay. I am one of my mother's eight children. I have two brothers and five sisters.

Original_Search_Title: Finish Your Race - Giving Up Not an Option
Headline: Zimbabwe: Finish Your Race - Giving Up Not an Option
Body_Text:
THERE are times in life when things get so tough such that one wonders what else can be done to make them better; times when all people seem to desert us; pillars we had crumble right before our eyes and slowly it becomes clear that we have been forgotten. It can be in your marriage, family, at work, community or even in financial life.
Be of good courage, this article is for you. All else may seem to be failing, crumbling and withering around you 'But God, who is rich in mercy, for his great love wherewith he loved us', (Ephesians 2:4) will turn your story around.

Original_Search_Title: Dairy Farming Delight in Honde Valley
Headline: Zimbabwe: Dairy Farming Delight in Honde Valley
Body_Text:
Clouds waft again and again, their silver hue transforming light as it filters through the film of the silhouetted horizon, giving the Eastern Highlands a unique view.
Honde Valley is one of the premier tourist destinations in Zimbabwe, with a wide range of species difficult to find in most parts of Africa except in neighbouring Mozambique.

Original_Search_Title: Adefulire Commends Airtel on Empowerment Initiatives
Headline: Nigeria: Adefulire Commends Airtel on Empowerment Initiatives
Body_Text:
Lagos State Deputy Governor, Mrs. Adejoke Adefulire, has commended Airtel Nigeria for its far reaching social empowerment initiatives in the state.
Adefulire gave the commendation during a courtesy visit to the headquarters of Airtel Nigeria at Banana Island, Lagos recently, where she was received by the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of the telecoms company, Mr. Segun Ogunsanya, in his office. The visit was part of efforts by the state government towards partnering with the telecoms operator in tackling critical social challenges often encountered by citizens of the state of which infant and maternal mortality and unemployment are of greatest concern.

Original_Search_Title: Clash of Meanings in Namibia
Headline: Namibia: Clash of Meanings in Namibia
Body_Text:
In 1993 one of the most celebrated scholars of political theory, Samuel Huntington authored an article in the Journal of Foreign Affairs under the title: The Clash of Civilizations.
The good old professor hypothesized seminally that after the Cold War wherein nations were pitted against one another along the lines of the ideologies of East and West, the East represented by the then Soviet led Marxist-Leninist system on the one hand and the West represented by the liberal capitalist system led by the United States, the conflicts in the new world were no longer ideological as before, but cultural.

Original_Search_Title: Cleaning Addis Abeba - It Is a Sisyphean Task
Headline: Ethiopia: Cleaning Addis Abeba - It Is a Sisyphean Task
Body_Text:
More than 10,000 street cleaners are scrambling to clean a city that produces 200,000 tons of waste annually; but their job is a Sisyphean task
In one uncharacteristically dry late morning in July of this year, three women with straw hats, waterproof safety jackets and plastic boots walk at their own speed alongside Roosevelt Avenue at the heart of Addis Abeba.

Original_Search_Title: There Is a Huge Difference Evident in Today's World - - Lawson
Headline: Nigeria: There Is a Huge Difference Evident in Today's World - - Lawson
Body_Text:
Dabota Lawson, a Real Estate Public Relations event manager, business woman and the Head of Corporate Communication Sujimoto Construction, a real estate developing company that majors in luxury real estate.
Most people in the society know her as an ex beauty queen. However, she is an accountant by profession. In this interview with Esther Onyegbula she talks about life a public relation manager of a real estate firm and other sundry issues.

Original_Search_Title: Ebola Virus-Free Country - 15 Days of Ebola Survivors Horror in Isolation Centre!
Headline: Nigeria: Ebola Virus-Free Country - 15 Days of Ebola Survivors Horror in Isolation Centre!
Body_Text:
It is no longer news that Nigeria has not recorded any Ebola Virus Disease, EVD, case since the last Ebola patient was discharged from the isolation centre at the Infectious Disease Hospital, IDH, Mainland Hospital, Yaba, Lagos. What is news, however, is that many countries of the world are impressed about the strategies applied by Nigerian experts to contain the spread of the disease .
To this effect, Nigeria has received accolades from various organisations including the World Health Organisation, WHO. In fact, tomorrow, Nigeria will be officially declared by WHO Ebola-free.

Original_Search_Title: The Tale of the Defiant Woman
Headline: Ethiopia: The Tale of the Defiant Woman
Body_Text:
Author: Endalegeta Kebede Title: "Embita" Published: 2014
Place of publication: Addis Ababa

Original_Search_Title: One Dies, 14 Hurt in Lightning Strikes
Headline: Rwanda: One Dies, 14 Hurt in Lightning Strikes
Body_Text:
One person was killed while 14 others were injured after lightning struck different locations in Gakenke and Gatsibo districts on Wednesday.
At least five people were seriously injured after lightning struck a church they were praying from in Rwimbogo Sector, Gatsibo District.

Original_Search_Title: Mayiga Frustrated Over MoU With Museveni
Headline: Uganda: Mayiga Frustrated Over MoU With Museveni
Body_Text:
Amid the euphoria that greeted last year's agreement - then called a memorandum of understanding - between President Museveni and Kabaka Ronald Mutebi, critical observers warned that the real test lay in implementing it.
And at a closed-door meeting with Buganda MPs on Wednesday, Katikkiro Charles Peter Mayiga spoke about his frustration with government's reluctance to translate the ink on paper into reality on the ground.

Original_Search_Title: Ode to Our Military Leaders
Headline: Nigeria: Ode to Our Military Leaders
Body_Text:
Ralph Nwosu, National Chairman, African Democratic Congress ADC, pays tribute to the Nigerian Armed Forces for making 'extra-ordinary sacrifices' to deepen democracy and protect the territorial integrity of the nation...
2014 is a symbolic year for Nigeria. This year, the nation marked its Centenary - 100 years since the amalgamation of the Nigerian nation. We also celebrated the 54th Independence Anniversary. Democratic civil rule in Nigeria has entered its 16th year, more than three times the stretch of time we ever had as a democratic nation state. In spite of all our challenges as a nation: security failings, Boko Haram/insurgency, leadership failures, and collective ineptitude and buck-passing, we have cause to celebrate and to be thankful to God. Even as the nation struggles with various challenges of nation building, learning to celebrate our little successes is a way to keep hope alive, to recognize the few whose efforts are clearly manifest and encourage and challenge all of us to maintain focus and commit to building our dream nation.

Original_Search_Title: Lagos Deputy Governor Commends Airtel On Empowerment Initiatives
Headline: Nigeria: Lagos Deputy Governor Commends Airtel On Empowerment Initiatives
Body_Text:
Lagos State Deputy Governor, Mrs. Adejoke Adefulire, has commended leading telecoms operator, Airtel Nigeria, for its far reaching social empowerment initiatives in the state.
Mrs. Adefulire gave the commendation during a courtesy visit to the headquarters of Airtel Nigeria at Banana Island, Lagos on Tuesday where she was received by the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of the Telco, Mr. Segun Ogunsanya, in his office.

Original_Search_Title: Five Nurseries for Trans Nzoia Banana Farming
Headline: Kenya: Five Nurseries for Trans Nzoia Banana Farming
Body_Text:
The Trans Nzoia government has established five tissue culture banana hardening nurseries.
Governor Patrick Khaemba said the nurseries are erected in Kwanza, Kiminini, Cherangany and Endebess constituencies.

Original_Search_Title: Prioritising Tourism Development in Abuja
Headline: Nigeria: Prioritising Tourism Development in Abuja
Body_Text:
On the return to democratic rule in 1999, the then President Olusegun Obasanjo-led administration had ignited celebration amongst practitioners in the tourism industry when he said that his administration would accord tourism a pride of place in government affairs. Though, not much was achieved by that government in this regard, successive administrations have been working assiduously to up the ante of tourism development in Nigeria.
In view of its huge potentials as a foreign exchange earner, the Federal Capital Territory Administration under Sen. Bala Mohammed's superintendence has been working tirelessly to develop Abuja's tourism potentials. The first thing he did on assumption of office in 2010 was to set up a Committee headed by Otunba Runsewe to re-engineer the activities of Parks/Gardens in Abuja with a view to making them attractive to tourists and those in need of relaxation and fun.

Original_Search_Title: Tune Me What - Second Season Sunset Show
Headline: South Africa: Tune Me What - Second Season Sunset Show
Body_Text:
We can hardly believe that we have two seasons of Tune Me What? under our belt. As we've said before, we embarked on this journey with the intention of sharing the South African music we grew up with - the music we knew and loved - with the rest of the world. It was never really our intention to achieve more than having a bit of fun, but somehow the idea snowballed.
Before we knew it we were name-checked by Esquire magazine and asked to syndicate our show right here at Think Africa Press. In our first season we managed to secure interviews with many iconic names in the South African music business and this season was no different, culminating just a few weeks ago with the thrill of having Lloyd Ross of Shifty Records as a guest on our show.
Now, after more than 60 episodes - all archived for your listening pleasure at tunemewhat.com - we still feel we have only scratched the surface: there's still so much more South African music to uncover and so many new and fresh artists emerging every year.
Nakhane Toure's astoundingly good follow up to his brilliant debut 'Christopher' single, 'Fog', came out during the run of the show and was swiftly followed by a third amazing single 'In The Dark Room'. One of the things we're looking forward to in Season Three is news that he has a second album coming out. We've said it before and we'll say it again: Nakhane Toure is a major talent who should be poised for international recognition.
Keep up with the latest headlines on WhatsApp | LinkedIn
No Tune Me What? would be complete without a nod to Chris Letcher & Matthew van der Want, and the good news is that following their reunion for the Shifty September show, they have announced further gigs. Recently, a wonderful video of Matthew backed by Louis Mhlanga saw the light of day on YouTube (from 20 years ago when Matthew still had hair!) and is worth a listen.
In the final show, Brett got to be on the other side of the console for a change as a track from his 90s band One Large Banana was spun and a track from his new band, The Sighs of Monsters, was played first on Tune Me What?
Also featured on the show were: Anton Goosen, Black Coffee, Jo Edwards, JoBurg City Stars, Johnny Clegg and Savuka, Mahlatini and The Mahotella Queens, N. D. Hotshots, Slo Foot King Brothers, Springbok Nude Girls, and Zinkplaat.
It may have been the last official show for the season, but Tune Me What? will be back for one final New Year's Party special in late December. In the meantime, the team will take a well-deserved break before planning their return for Season Three!
Tune Me What? is a podcast and blog by Brett Lock and Leon Lazarus that highlights South African music and artists at home and around the world. For more information, visit tunemewhat.com or facebook.com/TuneMeWhat.

Original_Search_Title: Mis-Definition of Uganda's Problems Caused Past Instability - Museveni
Headline: Uganda: Mis-Definition of Uganda's Problems Caused Past Instability - Museveni
Body_Text:
Part XVIII of these series is the second segment of a speech that President Museveni made to the then Movement caucus retreat in Jinja district on December 9, 2000.
In the first segment of this speech, titled 'The Movement's Long Struggle for the Liberation of Uganda,' Museveni talked about the process of human social and political evolution. In the second segment, he talks - using his own personal experiences - about how what he describes as the mis-definition of Uganda's problems led to prolonged political instability: -

Original_Search_Title: MV Victoria Passengers Cheat Death After Engine Failure
Headline: Tanzania: MV Victoria Passengers Cheat Death After Engine Failure
Body_Text:
Bukoba — ABOUT 381 passengers who were aboard the Mv Victoria, plying between Bukoba-Mwanza via Kemondo Bay narrowly cheated death, after the ship's engines failed midway.
The passengers had to endure ordeal as Port officials at Kemondo made frantic efforts to tug the vessel to shallow waters. Mv. Victoria Captain Bembele Samson Bembele told a team of reporters at Kemondo Bay on Saturday that the automatic system failed suddenly after leaving Bukoba Port at around 9.00 pm on Friday.

Original_Search_Title: Jobseeker's Diary - Enough With Banana Taunts
Headline: Rwanda: Jobseeker's Diary - Enough With Banana Taunts
Body_Text:
When I heard that Carlo Tavecchio had been elected President of the Italian Football Federation back in August, I was disappointed that the man, who had referred to African football players as "Banana eaters" barely a month ago, had been rewarded instead of being reprimanded so as to deter others from making racist remarks.
I'm glad he has been handed a six-month ban by UEFA, rendering him ineligible for any official UEFA position during this period, although I believe a six-year ban would have been more appropriate.

Original_Search_Title: Start Small but Think Big - Supporting SMEs to Grow and Export (part II)
Headline: Rwanda: Start Small but Think Big - Supporting SMEs to Grow and Export (part II)
Body_Text:
Rwanda has substantially reduced the time and cost of filing tax returns, and moved up the ranking for paying taxes from 83rd in 2006 to 22nd in 2013. With VAT now filed quarterly, and an electronic filing system, payments required per year have fallen from 83 in 2006 to 22 by 2013, and the time spent filing taxes each year has fallen from 168 hours to 113.
Looking at SMEs, in particular, Rwanda's efforts to support start-ups have clearly paid off. The number of businesses registered per year increased from 9,068 in 2011, to 11,228 in 2013, a 24 per cent increase in two years. Up to 9,350 businesses have registered so far in 2014. In 2013, 4,169 companies registered online, almost four times more than in 2012. So far in 2014, 7,600 businesses have already registered online, 81 per cent of the total.

Original_Search_Title: Akujobi - National Assembly: Exemplification of Mediocrity
Headline: Nigeria: Akujobi - National Assembly: Exemplification of Mediocrity
Body_Text:
SINCE the return to democratic rule in 1999, it is becoming increasingly horrendous that the legislative arm has not been living to its full constitutional role as one would expect. It is either the principal officers are subjected to the banana pearl syndrome that characterized the earlier legislative periods, where Ghana- must-go-bags were freely and shamelessly displayed on the hallowed chambers, which were inducements from the executive arm. Bribe for budgets as typified in the Adolphus Wabara led senate at a time, leading to the disgraceful ouster of Prof. Fabian Osuji as minister of Education, the unceremonious resignation of Wabara himself, the removal of an erstwhile Vice chancellor of FUTO towards the tail end of his tenure, fingered as complicit in the affair.
There were instances where Assembly Committee members appointed to investigate and probe infractions by the executive arm, in turn get their hands burnt in very demeaning act of gratifications as in the case of Ndidi Elumelu-led committee on power, charged with probing the rural electrification projects. Honourable Ihembe adhoc committee on capital market, alleged to have solicited and got some gratifications; Farouk Lawan, widely referred to as Mr. Integrity owing to his tenacious debate and probing on the floor of the lower house. But he eventually capitulated when the nation needed his services most at probing the corruption riddled subsidy process. No thanks to Mr. Femi Otedola, chairman of Forte oil, who exposed his hypocrisy by filming him as he made turns, collecting crispy dollars as bribe to delist firms owned by Otedola from the scandals.

Original_Search_Title: Uganda Must Grow Up Fast
Headline: Uganda Must Grow Up Fast
Body_Text:
In the week preceding Independence day, President Museveni launched the reconstruction of a road linking Uganda to Rwanda, the banana value addition project in Bushenyi district, and the standard gauge railway, among other activities.
During the same week, the renovation of Mulago hospital, at the cost of Shs 128bn, also commenced.

Original_Search_Title: 2015 - Berates Igbo Leaders for Endorsing President
Headline: Nigeria: 2015 - Berates Igbo Leaders for Endorsing President
Body_Text:
Immediate past Majority leader, Imo State House of Assembly, Dr. John Egbuchulam, on Wednesday insisted that Ndigbo have all it takes to produce the President of Nigeria come 2015.
Egbuchulam stressed that Ndigbo cannot be taken for granted ahead of the forthcoming Presidential Election.

Original_Search_Title: Jonathan Must Tell Us Whole Truth On Seized Arms Funds - Rep
Headline: Nigeria: Jonathan Must Tell Us Whole Truth On Seized Arms Funds - Rep
Body_Text:
Lagos — Minority Leader of the House of Representatives, Femi Gbajabiamila, has challenged President Goodluck Jonathan to tell Nigerians the whole truth behind recent seizure of $5.7 billion by the South African government less than three weeks after a similar incident involving $9.3 million.
Gbajabiamila, who insisted that something was fundamentally wrong whole affair, noted that the details of the alleged arms deal are sketchy.

Original_Search_Title: Piaget Theory and Infant Art
Headline: Zimbabwe: Piaget Theory and Infant Art
Body_Text:
The late Jean Piaget, a Swiss developmental psychologist and philosopher, was best known for studies that led to what is now known as the cognitive development theory.
Piaget theorised that a child's mental models, or cognitive structures, are based on the child's activities.

Original_Search_Title: Senate Stability - David Makes His Mark
Headline: Nigeria: Senate Stability - David Makes His Mark
Body_Text:
THE Senate and upper legislative chambers in many democracies are referenced by the longevity of its members. However, cultural differences, rotation, intra party power fights among other issues make Nigeria an exception.
THE one time common news of the repeated removals of Senate Presidents may sound like ancient history to many, but it was really not too long ago. The last time a Senate President was removed was in 2005 when Senator Adolphus Wabara was forced to step down from office and Senator Ken Nnamani elected in his place following the former Senate President's alleged involvement in the bribe for budget scam in the Federal Ministry of Education.

Original_Search_Title: More Protein & Fiber Food Helps Fight Ebola
Headline: Liberia: More Protein & Fiber Food Helps Fight Ebola
Body_Text:
The Chief Medical Officer of John F. Kennedy Memorial Hospital, Dr. Billy C. Johnson, says the deadly Ebola virus can be defeated in the body when it meets the bodies build up with more protein and other fiber in the system.
The chief medical officer of the nation's premier medical center in Monrovia strongly advised against smoking, drinking of hard substance, consumption of excess sugar and other unwanted foods, saying this is leading young Liberians to their early graves. Notwithstanding, the medic indicated that the consumption of fresh and organic foods can help build the immune system of the body.

Original_Search_Title: Ekiti State - Heading Towards Chaos
Headline: Nigeria: Ekiti State - Heading Towards Chaos
Body_Text:
In the last few weeks Ekiti State has been in the news because of the invasion of its judiciary and attacks on Judges by thugs. The term thugs here strikes a sore note as Ekiti State prides itself in education, with the reputation of having the highest number of professors and doctorate degree holders of any state in the country. With the election of the Governor-elect Ayodele Fayose whose swearing in draws near on October 16, the two major political parties have steadily shown that all is not well in the polity and that this change of baton would certainly not be a smooth one, and therefore it was likely just a matter of time before the two political parties in the State locked horns and tore at each other's throat. With all sincerity no one expected whatever disagreements they may have had to rare its ugly head in the manner that it did at the Ekiti State High Court. These blatant attacks on the judiciary are warning signs that the country is degenerating into a banana republic.
How else does one explain how twice in four days, judicial activities ground to a shuddering halt in Ado-Ekiti High Court?

Original_Search_Title: Namibia Can Avoid the Afrikan Death Trap - Part 2
Headline: Namibia Can Avoid the Afrikan Death Trap - Part 2
Body_Text:
WE have learned enough from older Afrikan experiences with self-rule that there is a myriad of pitfalls that we can avoid if we continue to keep our eyes on the prize. Namibia has done very well thus far.
One of the deadly sins that we in Afrika can easily commit is to fail to distinguish the State from the Government and the ruling party at any given point in time. It is most unfortunate that in Namibia, 24 years after independence, we are going backwards in our nation-building efforts. This is so because the majority of political party members, particularly of the ruling party today, cannot distinguish between the ruling party, the Government and the State. In their naïve, over-zealous faith in the party, and let us add political opportunism, they consider the party higher than the Government and higher than the State.

Original_Search_Title: U.S Warns Zimbabwe Over U.S $3 Billion Russia Deal
Headline: Zimbabwe: U.S Warns Zimbabwe Over U.S $3 Billion Russia Deal
Body_Text:
THE United States has reportedly warned Zimbabwe about its growing economic dealings with Russia after Harare recently sealed an agreement with Moscow for a US$3billion platinum mine.
According to Herald columnist Nathaniel Manheru, who is thought to be President Robert Mugabe's spokesman George Charamba, the Obama administration threatened further sanctions against Zimbabwe over its ties with Russia.

Original_Search_Title: Bomet Body Mixup Leads to Cleansing
Headline: Kenya: Bomet Body Mixup Leads to Cleansing
Body_Text:
A Kipsigis cleansing ceremony had to be performed by elders before villagers exhumed the body of an 80-year-old woman buried on Monday.
The body of Leah Maina was wrongly buried by relatives of Leah Sang at Chepkochun village.

Original_Search_Title: Bogey Team for Gor
Headline: Kenya: Bogey Team for Gor
Body_Text:
The contrast in fortunes on the field between champions and league leaders Gor Mahia and relegation threatened Nairobi City Stars will count for little when the two collide at the Nairobi City Stadium this afternoon (4.15pm).
Gor, unbeaten in the second leg of the campaign so far, are the front runners in their bid to retain their title but will face a potential banana skin in the shape of the Kawangware outfit.

Original_Search_Title: Human Rights Commission Begins Inquiry Into Petitions From S-East Zone
Headline: Nigeria: Human Rights Commission Begins Inquiry Into Petitions From S-East Zone
Body_Text:
Enugu — National Human Rights Commission, NHRC, yesterday, commenced a two-day public inquiry into the petition filed against some state governments within the South East zone over alleged abuse of human rights, unwarranted demolitions and seizures of people's homes and lands.
At the inaugural sitting at the Federal High Court Enugu, Mr. Chibueze Okorie, counsel to Ogoni forced eviction victims against the Rivers State Government and four others, applied for an interim order to stop the Rivers State government from continuing further development in their alleged seized land being converted to a banana factory by an investor.

Original_Search_Title: Nigeria, a Toddler At 54, But... - - Sen Nworgu
Headline: Nigeria, a Toddler At 54, But... - - Sen Nworgu
Body_Text:
ABUJA--CHAIRMAN, Senate Committee on Gas, Senator Nkechi Nworgu, has decried the slow pace of the country's development 54 years after independence, likening it to a toddler.
Senator Nworgu, who represents Abia Central senatorial district and one of the governorship aspirants on the platform of Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, in Abia State, said the long stay of military in the administration of the country was also a big setback.

Original_Search_Title: Fawu Press Statement On the Current Strike Actions, Those Resolved and Those Contemplated

Body_Text:

The below is the summary of these strike actions as undertaken and resolved or as ongoing and still to see resolution or those that that are coming up.

Original_Search_Title: Ekiti Court Attacks - NJC to Constitute a Probe Panel Today
Headline: Nigeria: Ekiti Court Attacks - NJC to Constitute a Probe Panel Today
Body_Text:
ABUJA--Worried by the violence in Ekiti state and alleged assault on a High Court judge at the premises of Ado-Ekiti High Court, the National Judicial Council, NJC, will today, constitute a panel to investigate allegations by both the governor-elect of the state, Mr. Ayodele Fayose and the Chief Judge, Justice Ayodeji Daramola on the fracas.
This was even as a top source in the Council yesterday disclosed that the duo may be summoned to appear before the probe panel to substantiate their allegations against each other.

Original_Search_Title: Fighting Diseases and Sicknesses Spiritually
Headline: Zimbabwe: Fighting Diseases and Sicknesses Spiritually
Body_Text:
BEING a pastor for a long time has afforded me the opportunity to realise that people can be sick from non-physical ailments or diseases. Disease, meaning "dis-ease", is something that disrupts order in the system, causing disharmony, discomfort or distress. It can be physical system, mental or spiritual. This entails, therefore, the need for healing in one's life, and areas like emotions, marriages, relationships, businesses and finances.
Like I promised last week, today we will look at this non-physical side of sickness and how you can tap into the healing power of God for yourself and even loved ones.

Original_Search_Title: Nameless Reaches Out in Luganda
Headline: Uganda: Nameless Reaches Out in Luganda
Body_Text:
Kenyan artiste Nameless has just taken his love for the banana republic to the next level by releasing a Luganda song, Kiwojjolo (Butterfly).
Kiwojjolo is a love song between a Kenyan man and a Uganda girl. The man needs to learn her language in order to communicate his feelings, and later teaches her his language as well. This is definitely the first time a Kenyan or any artiste from another country has sung in Uganda's widely-spoken language.

Original_Search_Title: Kisii Chain Stores Told to Stock Local Produce
Headline: Kenya: Kisii Chain Stores Told to Stock Local Produce
Body_Text:
SUPERMARKETS in Kisii have been directed to stock locally produced goods.
Trade executive said the supermarkets should prioritise sale of banana crisps and traditional vegenatbles from the region.

Original_Search_Title: Swimming With Snakes in a Pool of Venom
Headline: Zimbabwe: Swimming With Snakes in a Pool of Venom
Body_Text:
Last night I had a dream! It was not a good dream, but still, I like it. I dreamt that I was a snake. A big red snake, full of power, and unstoppable. I know very well that a snake is not a good creature. A snake can be poisonous if it bites you. It is used by witches and my pastor also preaches that the Devil is a snake. But, I do not mind being the snake that I was in the dream -- a big flying snake.
In the dream, I flew swiftly to the village headman's home. I found him and his family about to have supper. I knocked over all their pots, dishes and plates, and put out the fire. I then coiled round the headman and squeezed him until his eyes popped out of their sockets. With my great tail, I lashed at his wives and children and their skins got cut into very fine thin ribbons. I am angry that I woke up before biting and killing the headman and his pig-of-a-son Onesimo!

Original_Search_Title: How PM Mbabazi Lost His Post
Headline: Uganda: The Fall of Mbabazi
Body_Text:
Inside look at how Museveni launched fight with former PM
The first indication that something unusual was happening at State House Entebbe was when President Yoweri Museveni on Wednesday Sept. 17 ended his meetings unusually early. The President retired for a quiet dinner with the First Lady, Mrs Janet Museveni. Sources tell The Independent that as they sat over a meal of beef, bananas, vegetables, and yoghurt, the President broke the silence: I have decided to end all this confusion about Mbabazi, the president reportedly said. The First Lady nodded in agreement. She was the first person to know of this development.

Original_Search_Title: Ekiti Crisis - CNPP Writes CJN, Urges Relocation of Courts to Abuja
Headline: Nigeria: Ekiti Crisis - CNPP Writes CJN, Urges Relocation of Courts to Abuja
Body_Text:
More reactions yesterday trailed the attack on a sitting judge in Ekiti State by suspected supporters of the state Governor-elect, Mr. Ayodele Fayose, with the Conference of Nigerian Political Parties (CNPP) expressing anger over the incident.
The CNPP called on the Chief Justice of Nigeria (CJN) and Chairman of the National Judicial Council (NJC), Justice Aloma Mukhtar, to relocate the two courts to the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Abuja, for speedy dispensation of justice.

Original_Search_Title: Dubai Talks Can Give EAC Fruitful Results
Headline: East Africa: Dubai Talks Can Give EAC Fruitful Results
Body_Text:
Until the East African Community signed the Economic Partnership Agreement (EPA) with the European Union last week, Kenya's fresh produce exporters were sweating buckets.
In very simple terms, an EPA is a reciprocal arrangement that neither country or economic bloc will impose taxes on the imports of the other.

Original_Search_Title: Ita Giwa's Daughter's Fairy-Tale Wedding
Headline: Nigeria: Ita Giwa's Daughter's Fairy-Tale Wedding
Body_Text:
The wedding that could be described as one of the most glamorous in the year 2014 took place two Saturdays ago when Kokoeka Ita-Giwa, daughter of Senator Florence Ita-Giwa, and her man, Chimaobi Shawcross-Obioha Jnr., took the centre stage.
The journey to the altar started at Ita-Giwa's Bellavista Tower, Banana Island, Ikoyi, Lagos, home on Sunday, August 3, followed by a coral and turquoise-coloured traditional wedding which held on Saturday, September 13, at Princess Villa, Diamond Hill, Calabar, Cross River State, and finally climaxed with a fairy-tale white wedding at the main hall of Eko Hotel and Suites, Victoria Island, Lagos. The colourful wedding reflected the culture of Calabar and the senator ensured all hands were on deck for her gorgeous daughter's special day.

Original_Search_Title: Watching Birds of Rwanda On Boat
Headline: Rwanda: Watching Birds of Rwanda On Boat
Body_Text:
I went bird watching (again!). This time round, I literally shot two birds with one stone. So did everybody else on the trip, actually.
Before the September 20th birding expedition to the Rugezi Marsh in the Northern Province, my last birding trip had been to Bugesera, in the Eastern Province.

Original_Search_Title: Is It Breakup Time for Jubilee?
Headline: Kenya: Is It Breakup Time for Jubilee?
Body_Text:
It was the week President Uhuru Kenyatta visited the US for the second time in consecutive months, this time for his maiden address to the UN General Assembly.
Unlike the last time - President Barack Obama's US-Africa Leadership Summit in early August - he was accompanied by First Lady Margaret Kenyatta.

Original_Search_Title: Politburo Showdown Looms Over Gono
Headline: Zimbabwe: Politburo Showdown Looms Over Gono
Body_Text:
A SHOWDOWN is looming in the Zanu PF politburo next week where senior party officials are expected to square off along factional lines over the issue of former Reserve Bank governor Gideon Gono, who has been disqualified by the Zimbabwe Electoral Commission (Zec) from filling the vacant Manicaland senatorial seat despite a resolution by the ruling party's decision-making administrative body.
Gono was disqualified on the grounds that he was not a registered voter in Buhera at the close of the voters' roll on July 10 ahead of the July 31 polls last year.

Original_Search_Title: Moyo Hits Back At 'Ignorant' Gono
Headline: Zimbabwe: Moyo Hits Back At 'Ignorant' Gono
Body_Text:
Below is the full statement by information minister Jonathan Moyo responding to comments made by former Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) governor Gideon Gono on his collapsed bid to join the Senate ...
PRESS STATEMENT

Original_Search_Title: At 23, Enterprising Joseph Mwesigwa Is Worth Sh80 Million
Headline: Uganda: At 23, Enterprising Joseph Mwesigwa Is Worth Sh80 Million
Body_Text:
Walking out of his father's house in a pink striped shirt, Joseph Mwesigwa looks more of a jovial, humble local peasant than the 23-year-old millionaire he is.
Mwesigwa, the fourth born to Samuel Ndawula, a welder in Seeta, Mukono district, and housewife Rose, is described by his parents as a business-minded, inventive person since his childhood.

Original_Search_Title: My Father Wanted Me in Priesthood, Not Politics
Headline: Uganda: My Father Wanted Me in Priesthood, Not Politics
Body_Text:
At 32 years of age, Gerald Kafureeka Karuhanga has already served as a member of Uganda's Parliament and as guild president of Makerere University. Yet when growing up, Karuhanga's father had completely different dreams for him.
On 91.3 Capital FM's Desert Island Discs, Karuhanga reveals to Simon Kasyate how his own dreams conflicted with those of his father and how he eventually carried the day and joined politics, against his father's wishes: -

Original_Search_Title: Loans for School Fees
Headline: Nigeria: Loans for School Fees, and Other Vanities?
Body_Text:
A new phenomenon is in town. Nigerian banks are now falling over themselves, advertising this new great idea - that they can loan people money to pay the school fees of their wards and children. It is no longer fashionable that the noble act of sourcing for those fees, so that both parents/guardians and the students alike will know that 'it is not an easy task', should be painful.
In fact, you are encouraged to stick your children in any of our exorbitant private schools, in the guise of giving them great education - when what you have in mind is how many business connections you want to make - and where that does not materialize, you cannot retract and you just keep sinking into debts while heaping the blame on your poor children; whom you are also probably spoiling rotten (for the loan disguises - or postpones - your trouble in paying the dollar-denominated fees while giving them the idea that their parents are as rich as the parents of their mates). It's a kinda fake lifestyle.

Original_Search_Title: Koko Ita-Giwa's Wedding Holds Today
Headline: Nigeria: Koko Ita-Giwa's Wedding Holds Today
Body_Text:
The nuptial of Koko Ita-Giwa, daughter of Senator Florence Ita-Giwa, to her man, Chimaobi Shawcross-Obioha Jnr., comes to a climax today with a white wedding ceremony.
The wedding which holds inside the main hall of Eko Hotel and Suites, Victoria Island, Lagos will complete a series of marriage rites which has heralded the much anticipated nuptial.

Original_Search_Title: Thousands Descend On Atlanta for Rwanda Day
Headline: Rwanda: Thousands Descend On Atlanta for Rwanda Day
Body_Text:
Over 3000 Rwandans living in North America will today meet Rwanda's President Paul Kagame in Atlanta, USA, during the sixth edition of Rwanda Day.
Former US President, Bill Clinton, a great friend of Rwanda, US Senators and Congressmen from Atlanta, movie and football stars have been invited to the event. In 2011, American civil rights activist and Baptist minister, Jesse Jackson attended the event.

Original_Search_Title: Nyusi Promises to Fight Nepotism
Headline: Mozambique: Nyusi Promises to Fight Nepotism
Body_Text:
Maputo — The candidate of the ruling Frelimo Party for the Mozambican presidential elections scheduled for 15 October, former defence minister Filipe Nyusi, pledged on Wednesday that, if elected, he will take measures to fight against nepotism which hinders development by placing incompetent people in key positions.
Speaking at Maputo's Polytechnic University, to an audience of university students, teachers and other education workers, Nyusi stressed that in making appointments under his administration, what would count “will be competence, not the person's surname”.

Original_Search_Title: Maternal Health Campaign Gets Sh400 Million Boost
Headline: Tanzania: Maternal Health Campaign Gets Sh400 Million Boost
Body_Text:
AMREF Health Africa has so far received pledges amounting to 400m/- through the "Stand up for African Mothers" fundraising drive that seeks to raise 1bn/- to train midwives to improve maternal health in the country.
The campaign, according to AMREF Health Africa's Country Director, Dr Festus Ilako, seeks to raise funds to train 15,000 midwives in 13 African countries and 3,800 in Tanzania by the year 2016.

Original_Search_Title: Dhlakama Makes Unexpected Appearance in Chimoio
Headline: Mozambique: Dhlakama Makes Unexpected Appearance in Chimoio
Body_Text:
Maputo — Afonso Dhlakama, leader of Mozambique's former rebel movement Renamo, on Friday surprised the population of Chimoio, capital of the central province of Manica, when he arrived unannounced at the local airport, according to a report in the Beira daily paper “Diario de Mocambique”.
Dhlakama was expected in Chimoio on Tuesday, to launch his campaign for the 15 October presidential election. But he decided to call in first at his home area of Mangunde, in Sofala province, where his father is “regulo” (chief) - and the easiest way of reaching Mangunde is via Chimoio.

Original_Search_Title: Beninoise, 33, in Court for Stealing Senator's N10 Million, Jewellery

Body_Text:

Amoussou, a resident of No. 221, Temple Heights, Flat 6, Banana Island, Lagos, is facing charges of conspiracy and stealing.

Original_Search_Title: Dimgba Igwe - Tears From Politicians?
Headline: Nigeria: Dimgba Igwe - Tears From Politicians?
Body_Text:
It is a season of sadness. Just a few days ago, Nigerians bade farewell to late Professor Dora Akunyili who died on the 7th of June, today, we are mourning ace journalist, Pastor Dimgba Igwe.
Amidst the hardship of daily existence, the uncertainties of daily life, the threat from the dreaded Ebola Virus Disease (EVD), the acute sense of insecurity in the land, we are still daily being confronted with incidents that make us question the vision those in positions of authority and influence have for citizens and generations to come.

Original_Search_Title: Litter-Free Environment Begins With You
Headline: Zimbabwe: Litter-Free Environment Begins With You
Body_Text:
IF everyone who dropped or threw litter onto the streets of central Harare was fined US$20 a lot of budget deficits would quickly be eliminated, so prevalent is the crime.
As it is the first week of a campaign by the Environmental Management Agency, the City of Harare and the police saw 93 pedestrians and motorists paying the admission of guilt fine and a promise that the campaign will continue, using where possible evidence gathered by the cameras now being installed to curb traffic offences.

Original_Search_Title: Help! We Are Losing the War Against Boko Haram
Headline: Nigeria: Help! We Are Losing the War Against Boko Haram
Body_Text:
We need to admit the truth; Nigeria no longer has an army worth its salt. The Nigerian army of today is a pathetic shadow of its glorious past. This explains why it is proving grossly inadequate at checkmating a Boko Haram army of some 10,000 men. As a matter of fact, ranged against the insurgency in the North-East, the army is now staring abject defeat in the face.
Gone are the glory days when the Nigerian army was at the forefront of international peace-keeping efforts, only surpassed by Pakistan, Bangladesh and India. The C.V. of Nigeria single-handedly initiating the ECOMOG (ECOWAS Monitoring Group) and orchestrating peacekeeping and peace-enforcement operations in Liberia and Sierra Leone is now in a dismal state of disrepair.

Original_Search_Title: Wolokoso - When Oulanyah Became Madam Speaker
Headline: Uganda: Wolokoso - When Oulanyah Became Madam Speaker
Body_Text:
Following or deputising a larger-than-life figure can be a daunting task. Despite being a bearded, balding, flamboyant man, deputy Speaker Jacob Oulanyah still has to remind MPs that he is not a 'madam' - the term used with Speaker Rebecca Kadaga.
However, Dokolo Woman MP and Opposition Chief Whip Cecilia Ogwal [FDC] recently found out that Oulanyah may be getting tired of reminding MPs about the fact he is a 'Mister'.

She is also the chairperson of Publish What You Pay-Uganda Chapter. On 91.3 Capital FM's Desert Island Discs with Simon Kasyate, Ngabiirwe shares her life story: -
Who is Winnie? Where were you born?

Headline: Uganda: Big Brother on Hold After Fire
Body_Text:
It should have been a day away today. The booze, jacuzzi and the drama.
One of the most anticipated seasons of TV viewing in Africa was upon us; Big Brother Africa was soon gracing our screens. Then, on Tuesday, September 2, the unlikely happened; the Big Brother house was razed by fire at the Sesani studios in Johannesburg!

Original_Search_Title: Murang'a Declared Jigger Free County

According to the statements released to the press by Ahadi Kenya Trust CEO Stanley Kamau and Murang'a governor Mwangi Wairia, the jigger eradication exercise by Ahadi Kenya and the department of public health has totally wiped the vermin out.

Original_Search_Title: Lilongwe Police in Sweeping Exercise

Body_Text:

In an interview with Malawi News Agency (Mana) Wednesday, Lilongwe Police Station Public Relations Officer Sub Inspector Kingsley Dandaula said the exercise targeted illegal vendors who sell their items in places like banks, filling stations and road junctions among others.
