The 5.8 feet (1.768 metres)-tall housewife weighed 148 kilogrammes, with a Body Mass Index (BMI) of 47.2.

Original_Search_Title: No Wonder Our Parents Insisted On Matchmaking
Headline: Uganda: No Wonder Our Parents Insisted On Matchmaking
Body_Text:
In this era of globalization and adoption of Western culture, one would think, "what is in a name?!"
Indeed, many modern parents improvise with children's names as they go along; I have heard, for example, that best friends Pastor Freda Sserwadda and Apostle Sarah Bunjo named the latter's baby girl Fresera (from their combined Christian names).

Original_Search_Title: The Plot Against Anyim
Headline: Nigeria: The Plot Against Anyim
Body_Text:
The voice of Dr. Sam Egwu, former governor of Ebonyi State, was hearty and vibrant as he answered his phone last Saturday. After the exchange of pleasantries, I congratulated him on his new assignment. He said: "Hia, Okey you have come again this early morning, what assignment are you talking about?" I answered: "The Anyim/Obi project, which the papers say that you are taking very seriously." Then Egwu asked what the Anyim/Obi meant and where it was taking place. In fact, he wondered whether it was in Anambra, Ebonyi or in Abuja. When I told him it had to do with his (Egwu's) onerous national assignment of working with the former governor of Anambra State Peter Obi to unseat the Secretary to the Government of the Federation (SGF), Anyim Pius Anyim, for the latter to take his job, Egwu roared with laughter, he was convinced that I was joking and said that I should also please tell him if someone was looking for shoe shiners and plate washers to keep his idle hands busy.
He then went on to ask about the family, having no doubt in his mind that I had just shared a joke before proceeding to the main reason for my call. Then I told him about a very elaborate story in the New Telegraph newspaper of that morning, detailing how he was at the head of a thickening plot to unseat Anyim. I read out part of the story detailing how he, Egwu, the former Governor of Ebonyi State, was said to be the chief campaigner for the aspirant to Anyim's office. Some equally powerful person were also said to be working to ensure that Anyim is not removed from office, while Chief Tony Anenih was somewhat undecided on whom to support.

Original_Search_Title: Banana Juice Leaves 125 in Hospital
Headline: Rwanda: Banana Juice Leaves 125 in Hospital
Body_Text:
Despite discharging 121 out of 125 people previously admitted to Mashesha Health Centre with cases of food poisoning last week, residents in Gitambi Sector, Rusizi District were reminded to observe proper hygiene and sanitation after suspicions of drinking contaminated banana juice at a wedding ceremony.
The ceremony didn't go down well as the attendants started complaining of severe fever, headaches, stomach pain and vomiting, among other symptoms of food poisoning.

Original_Search_Title: Orajiaku - Before Fifa Becomes a Nation
Headline: Nigeria: Orajiaku - Before Fifa Becomes a Nation
Body_Text:
HITHERTO, I remained a passive yet passionate supporter of sports affairs especially football in Nigeria. In this vein, I had become a bonafide member of Nigeria football supporters club with Comrade Rafiu Oladipo at the helm of affairs. Until recently, I chose to be in the spectators' corner, observing the on-goings from government's participation to the executive members of Nigeria Football Federation leadership and extended to the particular player who exerts their energy on the field to make the nation proud.
Obviously, this is not the best of times for the lovers of sport in Nigeria considering the seeming, unending brouhaha in the management of our football affairs. The dust raised especially in the wake of the sack of Alhaji Aminu Maigari, former president of NFF appears to be taking eternity before it could settle. Maigari was alleged to have mismanaged the funds received from F.I.F.A and meant for Nigeria's Super Eagles towards their participation at the just concluded World Cup tournament held in Brazil. He is equally accused of other gross misappropriation of funds, abuse of office, etc. Despite the fact that he remained innocent until proven otherwise by the court of competent jurisdiction, his employer deemed him unfit to continue to occupy the highly esteemed position as the president of N.F.F. How did the world football governing body FIFA react? What is position of Nigerians and the government in power regarding that despotic intervention? Where should we really premise the seeming unquestionable authority F.I.F.A wields in this and similar developments?

Original_Search_Title: KCC Shootout Woes - Thin Line Between Bad Luck and Fate
Headline: Uganda: KCC Shootout Woes - Thin Line Between Bad Luck and Fate
Body_Text:
A few days ago, George Nsimbe stood on the brink of history when he led KCC to the semifinal of the Cecafa Club championship, popularly known today as Cecafa Kagame Cup.
At stake was a place in the final, which would have been a first since 1979 as the club sought to win the title for the first time since their maiden triumph in 1978. In essence, KCC was just two games away from equalling a special feat only reserved for the likes of Jimmy Kirunda, Tom Lwanga, Jamil Kasirye, Moses Sentamu, Phillip Omondi, and Moses Nsereko, among others.

Original_Search_Title: Dynamite Fishing Poses Threat to Aquatic Species
Headline: Tanzania: Dynamite Fishing Poses Threat to Aquatic Species
Body_Text:

Members of Beach Management Units (BMWs) in Dar es Salaam have called for serious combat of the destructive dynamite fishing saying that out of 100 fish killed in a cruel manner hardly 20 pieces only (fish) are collected to reach unsuspecting consumers at their own expense.

So, the 33-year-old single mother of three gives the 15 to 20 pounds she makes every day, selling bananas in a market in Juba, to her sister, who lives in a suburb of capital.

Original_Search_Title: Africans Make Good in Hong Kong
Headline: African Endeavours in Chungking Mansions - Portrait of a Hong Kong Hub
Body_Text:
Hong Kong — Becoming a success in Hong Kong isn't easy, but for many African traders the potential to earn big makes it more than worth the effort.
The old couches, low tables placed in front of them, only amplify the smallness of the room where a 50-year-old Tanzanian lady is serving boiled bananas and chicken. A handful of African customers sit enjoying familiar meals and smatterings of Kiswahili can be heard being spoken. But despite appearances, this restaurant is a few thousand miles from any African shore. The room is on the 16th floor of Block D in the notorious Chungking Mansions of Hong Kong.
The Tanzanian lady who runs the restaurant as well as the guest house connected to it has been in Hong Kong for 5 years and is just one of many Africans who have long made their homes here. Modu Medard, a Ghanaian, has lived and worked at this well-known trade hub for 16 years, selling garments and shoes from his shop on the busy first floor of the Mansions. Not far from that store, Babangida, a Nigerian, sells jeans and shoes made in China, and has been operating his shop for 7 years.
According to some estimates, there are around 20,000 African migrants in Hong Kong which, alongside Guangzhou, Yihu, Macau, Shangai and Beijing, is one of the preferred destinations for Africans going to China. A few thousand of these are permanent residents, while a few hundred are refugees. Most are traders or operate businesses such as restaurants, but some have also started venturing into other activities such as teaching English and playing professional football.
Keep up with the latest headlines on WhatsApp | LinkedIn
Hong Kong's African market
Chungking Mansions is a five-block 17-storey building located in the Kowloon area of Hong Kong. Evidently aged, the building is filled with just about everything: wholesale and retail shops; clothes and shoes stores; groceries; shops selling all sorts of electronics, from radios to computer accessories. The complex also houses several budget hostels and restaurants. It seems that there is a money changer on every floor.
It is a hub for traders from Africa, India, and Pakistan, asylum seekers and illegal workers. These make up the 5,000 people who live in the building, while 10,000 are said to visit daily.
According to Gordon Mathews, professor of anthropology at the Chinese University of Hong Kong, a fifth of the mobile phones that reach sub-Saharan Africa may have passed through Chungking Mansions. Meanwhile, Issa Ssekito, the spokesperson for the Kampala City Traders Association (KACITA), estimates that some 70% of phones imported to Uganda come from China.
Hong Kong is an important hub of the rapidly growing trade between Africa and China that both sides are keen to emphasise. In 1995, China's trade with Africa was valued at $6 billion; by 2010, it had risen to $130 billion; and in 2013, it was estimated to have reached $210 billion.
However, while the figures are impressive, many African traders in China are less than reassured and report finding it increasingly difficult to buy and sell goods. Ssekito, who deals in Chinese electronics which he buys from Guangzhou and Shenzhen, believes the growing Sino-African trade relations are tilted in favour of the Chinese.
"China says it has a liberalised economy but it is closed. It is hard for an African to start a company or even open a retail shop in any of the Chinese cities unless you partner with a local," he says.
He suggests that about 70 Ugandan traders travel to China weekly to buy goods, ranging from cosmetics, electronics, footwear, clothes and ceramics. KACITA helps these traders gain visas, but Ssekito claims that for some groups such as women below 30, it is almost impossible to get a visa.
"They think such girls are going for prostitution," he says.
Indeed, China seems to be increasingly wary of visitors from Africa, and China and the Hong Kong Special Administrative region government recently tightened work and visa rules in a move aimed at barring illegal immigrants.
According to Babangida, Nigerians encounter similar problems over issues of visas in Mainland China. "It is hard for Africans to operate businesses in China," he says. "We tried... and failed...They deport several Nigerians even if they have a visa. It is not fair."
Challenges in China
For those traders who are able to acquire visas and remain for their agreed stays, the promise of profit awaits but not without challenges along the way. One particular issue is the language barrier, which can have repercussions beyond simple difficulties in communication.
"Most Chinese traders do not speak English. In some cases when we fail to understand each other, they ask us to show them our passports and when they realise you are Ugandan, they choose the grade of goods to sell you. Sometimes these goods are of poor quality," claims Ssekito.
KACITA, he says, also handles 5-7 cases a month of traders alleging that they received poorer quality goods than they had agreed.
But despite myriad challenges, many African traders still believe business in China is worth the trouble and lucrative. Ssekito, for instance, says that he is planning to start exporting Nile Perch swim bladders to Hong Kong, where he says they are in high demand due to their use in Chinese traditional medicine. The Tanzanian restaurant owner meanwhile explains that she earns several times more money in Hong Kong than she could back home and that she sends enough to her family to support them. And Medard says that he has also educated his children in Ghana using earnings from his shop in Chungking Mansions, and invested in real estate back home.
Despite the increasing difficulties, these migrants making tidy profits in Hong Kong are not about to give up any time soon.
This article was made possible by a grant from the China-Africa Reporting Project managed by the Journalism Department of the University of Witwatersrand.

Original_Search_Title: Debating Diaspora Voting
Headline: Nigeria: Debating Diaspora Voting
Body_Text:
The recent declaration by Professor Attahiru Jega, chairman of the Independent National Electoral Commission, that allowing Nigerians in the Diaspora to vote in 2015 would be "practically impossible" even if the Electoral Act 2010 is amended to allow for such, must have come as a huge disappointment to the proponents of Diaspora voting.
It should be recalled that President Jonathan, while in Gabon in August 2010 during the celebration of the country's 50 years of independence, assured Nigerians in the Diaspora that they would be able to exercise their franchise from the 2015 elections in the country. The President was quoted as promising that: "while it may not be feasible for Nigerians in the Diaspora to vote in the forthcoming 2011 polls, trends in modern technology like the electronic voting system have simplified the voting process and assured that Government will ensure that they exercise their franchise in subsequent elections"(The Nigerian Voice 19 August 2010).

Original_Search_Title: The Handicapped Character As a Stylistic Device
Headline: Kenya: The Handicapped Character As a Stylistic Device
Body_Text:
I do not remember what exactly was going through my mind when I wrote the poem, "Love Missive," where these lines appear at the beginning. What I do recall is that during the debate on the Constitution Referendum of 2005, I reflected a lot on the events of the day and wrote some poems as an escape, if only to capture that historical moment. The referendum reminded us of the elephant in the room. It exposed the hypocrisy that belies our gleeful muttering of such none consequential words as "my brother" at political rallies, and in our daily endeavors as denizens of Kenya. We were caught, like the naked night runner, his thing between the legs glowing in darkness, parading our rabid tribal inclinations. We were Bananas and Oranges. We suffered defeat. We celebrated victory. Yet we've squandered every opportunity to kick out the elephant. The nation remains subservient to tribe. We forge superficial unities in times of tragedy. My missive was written in 2005. Since I'm at times very perceptive, I speak only, distantly, detached, in the frame of allusions, about the plebiscite. I had not read this poem for many years. But looking at it this week, the words in the last two stanzas confounded me still:
Not known I, bred to music

Original_Search_Title: 15 Years Ago, This Wedding Shattered Ugandan Records
Headline: Uganda: 15 Years Ago, This Wedding Shattered Ugandan Records
Body_Text:
Today, August 27, 2014 marks exactly 15 years since Kabaka Ronald Muwenda Mutebi II and the Nnaabagereka Sylvia Nagginda tied the knot at St Paul's cathedral Namirembe.
This was the first royal wedding Ugandans were witnessing in 50 years and Robert Mugagga takes you down memory lane, compiling great moments that made this arguably Uganda's wedding of the century.

Original_Search_Title: Rusizi Food Poisoning Victims Discharged
Headline: Rwanda: Rusizi Food Poisoning Victims Discharged
Body_Text:
At least 121 out of the 125 people admitted at Mashesha Health Centre in the south-western district of Rusizi after drinking suspected contaminated banana juice at a wedding ceremony last weekend have been discharged, local authorities and medical sources have said.
The incident occurred in Gitambi Sector last Friday. Local authorities intervened after those who had consumed the juice started complaining of severe fever, headaches, stomach pain and vomiting, among other symptoms of food poisoning, according to Gervais Ndagijimana, the health centre director.

Original_Search_Title: Creative Geniuses Meet At Kuona Art Walk
Headline: Kenya: Creative Geniuses Meet At Kuona Art Walk
Body_Text:
For many people, inability to access art work has been one of the reasons fronted for failing to buy art they are interested in. This is what prompted Kuona Trust to hold their first Art Walk at their grounds off Likoni Road in Nairobi on August 17.
It was a way for Kuona Trust to promote young artists by exposing their work. The event saw the coming together of artists and buyers, who were exposed to a variety of art.

Original_Search_Title: Lightning Kills Four
Headline: Rwanda: Lightning Kills Four
Body_Text:
At least four people died while five others sustained serious injuries after they were struck by lightning during a heavy downpour on Sunday evening.
The Ministry of Disaster Management and Refugee Affairs indicates that Gatsibo District in the Eastern Province was the worst hit, after three people died while another person sustained serious injuries in Kabarole Sector.

Original_Search_Title: Ebonyi - As Elechi's Intervention Fails to Douse Assembly Crisis
Headline: Nigeria: Ebonyi - As Elechi's Intervention Fails to Douse Assembly Crisis
Body_Text:
The crisis rocking Ebonyi State House of Assembly seems to be far from over despite Governor Martin Elechi's intervention, writes Benjamin Nworie
For early one month, Ebonyi State House of Assembly has existed with two "speakers" following the controversial impeachment of Chukwuma Nwazunku as speaker by 18 lawmakers of the 24-member Assembly. The lawmakers had on July 21 sat as earlier as 6:30am sat and impeached Nwazunku, but Nwazunku never agreed he was impeached, as he said the legislators were on recess.

Original_Search_Title: Tamed By the Wild At Akagera National Park
Headline: Rwanda: Tamed By the Wild At Akagera National Park
Body_Text:
It is always good to have more than one perspective of something before you can authoritatively talk about. I can therefore confidently say that when it comes to Akagera National Park, I feel I am in that position. You see I've had the rare opportunity of visiting Akagera twice in less than a year.
Towards the end of last year I was privileged to visit the park after winning a complementary helicopter ride with the Akagera Aviation (not related to the park) and the very friendly pilot (Egide) gave me an aerial visit of the park.

Original_Search_Title: The Four Intelligence CEOs Since Independence
Headline: Kenya: The Four Intelligence CEOs Since Independence
Body_Text:
Like their presidents, Kenyans have had only four national spymasters since independence. The first one was the legendary James Kanyotu, who was trained by the British during the 1950s State of Emergency but served his post-independence masters, first and second presidents Jomo Kenyatta and Daniel arap Moi, with such total dedication and loyalty that he became a multibillionaire.
By the time he died in retirement in 2008, at the height of the post-election violence, Kanyotu had massive landholdings and investments in real estate, the hospitality sector and banking. In fact, Kanyotu's property portfolio overlapped seriously with Moi's.

Original_Search_Title: Experience With an Asthmatic Child
Headline: Zambia: Experience With an Asthmatic Child
Body_Text:
Developing countries around the world are undergoing an epidemiological transition from communicable or infectious to non-communicable diseases (NCDs), such as cardiovascular disease, chronic respiratory diseases,cancer and diabetes.
These NCDs were responsible for 60 per cent of all deaths globally in 2005, with more than 75 per cent occurring in developing countries.Unhealthy diet, physical inactivity, tobacco and alcohol use are important preventable major risk factors for chronic diseases that are related to lifestyle choices. (Source: WHO Country Office, Zambia Annual Report 2010).

Original_Search_Title: Cosatu North West and Saccawu Marching Against Cashbuild
Headline: South Africa: Cosatu North West and Saccawu Marching Against Cashbuild
Publisher: Congress of South African Trade Unions (Johannesburg)
Body_Text:
The Congress of South African Trade Unions and SACCAWU will be marching to Cashbuild against the unfair mass dismissal that took place in three NW stores - Mmabatho, the Crossing at Mafikeng and Rustenburg.
About 55 poor workers have been dismissed on allegations of theft of stock in those stores without being given any proof that they have stolen the company's stocks or materials.

Original_Search_Title: Furious Nyeri Woman Beats Up Husband After Catching Him Red Handed Having Sex With School Girls
Headline: Kenya: Furious Nyeri Woman Beats Up Husband After Catching Him Red Handed Having Sex With School Girls
Body_Text:
A woman wrestled her husband in public as the crowd cheered her on in Nyeri town over a family dispute.
The couple squared it publicly, an act that attracted residents who cheered the woman on in a 20 minute drama.

Original_Search_Title: Banana Hawker, 26, Raped At Jahi
Headline: Nigeria: Banana Hawker, 26, Raped At Jahi
Body_Text:
A 26 -year-old banana hawker (names withheld) was said to have been raped by three unknown men along Jahi road in Abuja.
The incident was said to have happened at about 7p.m. on Tuesday.

Original_Search_Title: Huye Pubs Turn to Pork As Demand Soars
Headline: Rwanda: Huye Pubs Turn to Pork As Demand Soars
Body_Text:
For three years now, Ignace Pokeya has been the cook behind a pork delicacy at one of the many pork joints in Butare town, Huye District.
Being one of the most popular pubs in the town, Pokeya knows he has to satisfy orders from dozens of patrons who he receives daily. So every morning when he wakes up, he knows a lot of work awaits him and he goes to his workplace well prepared for the orders that mainly come later in the day.

Original_Search_Title: Foretelling the Future
Headline: Nigeria: Foretelling the Future
Body_Text:
Just like Bill Gates, Africa's richest man, Aliko Dangote is not leaving anyone in doubt about his philanthropy, as he gives more to charity.
There is something inseparable about the Africa's richest man, Aliko Dangote and the World's richest man, Bill Gates. It's not about their vast wealth. It is their charity. Dangote, 57, has given more to charity in recent years more than anyone else in the country. But his ambition is much more than Nigeria and Africa. Just like Bill Gates, Dangote seems to be preparing for a life outside his vast business empire, the Dangote Group.

Original_Search_Title: We Can Live Up to 100 Years If We Set Right Plans
Headline: Tanzania: We Can Live Up to 100 Years If We Set Right Plans
Body_Text:
LAST month I lost my uncle Paul Ileti Mushi who used to live at Lawate Division in Siha District, Kilimanjaro Region. Mzee Paul died at 101 years, after developing some health complications caused largely by old age.
For us Christians, the Bible teaches us that an ordinary human being can live up to 70 years, but those who are stronger can even go to 80 years. My uncle might have been very blessed because he even surpassed the 80 stronger-age biblical limit.

Original_Search_Title: Mugabe Troubled By Monster He Created
Headline: Zimbabwe: Mugabe Troubled By Monster He Created
Body_Text:
Finally Zanu PF is now washing its dirty linen in the public which has given the public a window of opportunity to see behind closet.
President Robert Mugabe has now come face to face with the reality and conceded there were political machinations brewing in the ruling party ahead of its December congress.

Original_Search_Title: Kenya Ranking Drops
Headline: Kenya Ranking Drops
Body_Text:
Harambee Stars slip out of the top hundred in thye latest Fifa list
Kenya's Harambee Stars have fallen nine places to position 104 from 95 in the latest International football federation (Fifa) rankings released yesterday.

Headline: Gambia: Ministers, Diplomats Write to President Jammeh
Body_Text:
Some Gambian ministers and ambassadors representing the country abroad have written to the president of the Republic, His Excellency Sheikh Prof. Alh. Dr. Yahya Jammeh, congratulating him on the occasion of the 20th Anniversary of the July 22nd Revolution, as well as the just concluded Holy Month of Ramadan.

Original_Search_Title: Osita Chidoka in an Injury Time
Headline: Nigeria: Osita Chidoka in an Injury Time
Body_Text:
One of the most important people on earth I met recently is Keith Williams. Don't start to scratch your head about why Williams is so important that I have accorded him this right. As the Chief Executive Officer of the British Airways, Williams takes decision for a corporation whose business can impact negatively or positively on the lives millions of people across the world. We all know about British Airways, don't we? It's vast number of employees, the largest fleet in the world and the name it carries as well.
So to keep that and many other pride that the airline has, Williams knows his position is not a bed of rose. Even if it's one, he dares not sleep on it. He told me that much when I visited him in his office at the BA headquarters in London a few days back.

Original_Search_Title: Sure Bet - Man City to Win Premier League
Headline: Uganda: Sure Bet - Man City to Win Premier League
Body_Text:
So much for the fight against racism! At a time when a clampdown on this vice had taken centre stage amongst most European football bodies, from the FA in England to Uefa and Fifa, the Italians just contrived to give everyone else in that fight a kick in the teeth.
On Monday, new office bearers were elected to take charge of the Italian FA. But while that may not be of much concern to football lovers around the world, the circumstances surrounding this election are. You see, in the run-up to this election, a one Carlo Tavecchio, vying for the topmost position in the Italian FA, made a racist comment that the African players in the Italian leagues just come in and play after they were just eating bananas.

Original_Search_Title: Ebola Virus and the Salt Water of Ignorance
Headline: Nigeria: Ebola Virus and the Salt Water of Ignorance
Body_Text:
"A Medical Doctor who is a very senior officer with one of the key FGN agencies involved in managing this crisis confirmed... that he also bathed in salt! Can you imagine the implication of this... ?... This has brought to the fore how vulnerable our people are to narratives that could harm them! Any wonder then that our Muslim and Christian clerics have such awesome power to mislead once they go toxic?" - A contributor to an Online discussion on the mass salt bathe in Nigeria.
NIGERIA went into panic mode last week! From all corners of Nigeria, people were receiving and exchanging texts; Whatsapp and BB messages as well emails on the same issue: wake up in the night, get a dip into salt water and oh, don't forget to drink some too.

Original_Search_Title: Solidarity Between Different Generations of Central African Refugees
Headline: Cameroon: Solidarity Between Different Generations of Central African Refugees in Cameroon
Publisher: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees
Body_Text:
Gbiti — Motivated by a sense of solidarity, Bouba Mairama has been a mother figure to many of the thousands of refugees who fled Central African Republic earlier this year and crossed the border to the small town of Gbiti. She provided clothes to replace their rags; she gave them food and water, she took in and educated unaccompanied children and she offered helpful advice to women and girls emerging from the bush.
Mairama had made the journey herself, but eight years before in 2006, fleeing from Bozoum in the west of Central African Republic with her family because of growing lawlessness and attacks on her ethnic Peul community. "We fled at night. We did not even have shoes," she recalled, adding that her husband had later died of illness in Cameroon.

Original_Search_Title: The Disempowerment of Blacks By Blacks - Part 4
Headline: Zimbabwe: The Disempowerment of Blacks By Blacks - Part 4
Body_Text:
THREE things happened over the last few weeks that make Zimbabwe appear like a circus. A real circus, or a zoo, where you have to keep asking, so who is who in the zoo?
Before I go into that, it's important to underscore key issues about leadership in the twenty first century. First, in this millennium, if the change outside a country is faster than the change inside it, then the end is near. Second, this millennium requires leaders that are of sound mind and clarity of purpose. By purpose, I mean a clear vision of how they want to advance the country and make not only a tangible difference but a remarkable and transformational one too. Third, leadership in this millennium needs a leader who smells or can read the air, that means they get it. Japanese youths use the phrase "kuuki yomenai" to describe folks that cannot 'read' the air.

Original_Search_Title: Oliseh Hits Out At Tavecchio
Headline: Nigeria: Oliseh Hits Out At Tavecchio
Body_Text:
Former Nigeria captain, Sunday Oliseh has hit out at the Italian Football Federation after Carlo Tavecchio was elected as its new president.
It comes less than a month after he caused controversy by referring to African footballers in Italian football as banana eaters.

Original_Search_Title: PM Discourages Mixing Politics With Development
Headline: Tanzania: PM Discourages Mixing Politics With Development
Body_Text:
Mwanza — THE Prime Minister Mr Mizengo Pinda has strongly warned over what he termed as leaders entertaining politics when dealing with serious national issues especially now as the country invites investors.
PM Pinda said on Friday here as he officially closed the Nanenane exhibitions for Lake Zone that leaders have to shun playing political games that might discourage investors from introducing their investments in the country.

Headline: Ethiopia: In Defense of Ethiopia's Dam and Its National Interest
Body_Text:
PART 2
Aswan Dam and the lessons we learn

Original_Search_Title: Saruni the Saviour
Headline: Kenya: Saruni the Saviour
Body_Text:
Ulinzi Stars goalkeeper James Saruni once again came to the rescue of the soldiers yesterday.
His 70th minute penalty after Fred Ochieng had handled in the box helped Ulinzi secure a 1-1 draw against visiting Muhoroni Youth in a Kenya Premier League match at the Afraha stadium, Nakuru, yesterday afternoon.

Original_Search_Title: Kandie's Invite to American Investors
Headline: Kenya: Kandie's Invite to American Investors
Body_Text:
Tourism Secretary Phyllis Kandie has called for American investment in Kenya.
Kandie's speech was yesterday read on her behalf by Kenya Investment Authority managing director Dr Moses Ikiara.

Original_Search_Title: Ikorodu Monarch's Demise - a Widow Reflects
Headline: Nigeria: Ikorodu Monarch's Demise - a Widow Reflects
Body_Text:
The death on Saturday August 2 of the paramount ruler of Ikorodu, the Ayangburen, Oba Salaudeen Oyefusi, expectedly threw the entire town into mourning. Also not surprisingly the ancient town has been playing host to several eminent personalities, community leaders and concerned citizens coming to mourn the traditional ruler who died at the age of 83 after reigning for a period of 43 years.
While he was alive, Oba Salaudeen Oyefusi, was known to be a no-nonsense monarch who refused to fold his arms and see evil thrive in the ancient town.

Original_Search_Title: Flood Victims Need More Help
Headline: Burundi Flood Victims Need More Help
Body_Text:
Bujumbura — Thousands of people displaced by floods and a mudslide in the Burundian capital Bujumbura and surrounding areas in February 2014 need more help to reconstruct their homes and livelihoods, aid workers say.
"These people who haven't been resettled and even those who have gone back to their homes are in need of proper shelter, food and blankets," Methode Niyungeko, coordinator of the Platform for the Prevention of Risks, a local NGO, told IRIN.

Original_Search_Title: Maina Njenga Told to Stay Out of Kiambaa
Headline: Kenya: Maina Njenga Told to Stay Out of Kiambaa
Body_Text:
Tension has gripped Banana area, Kiambaa constituency amid reports that former Mungiki leader Maina Njenga plans to build a church and a home in the area.
Deputy county commissioner Wycliff Mulanda said police are on high alert over any threats to security.

Original_Search_Title: Where's Uganda Brand?
Headline: Uganda: Where's Uganda Brand?
Body_Text:
Representatives of the Uganda Tourism Board (UTB) have requested MPs to increase the budget for tourism promotion.
The head of marketing at UTB, Edwin Muzahura, stated: "In this financial year, Kenya has allocated $35m and Rwanda $17m on promoting tourism in their countries; Uganda allocated only $700,000."

Original_Search_Title: Kerina Joins Constitutional Changes Debate
Headline: Namibia: Kerina Joins Constitutional Changes Debate
Body_Text:
Windhoek — United Nations petitioner Professor Mburumba Kerina said the ongoing efforts to amend the country's constitution are not necessary as the current constitution is a 'beautiful' one. Kerina has also warned Namibians not to look each other through tribal lenses as this could fuel chaos in the country.
Kerina, who was among the first petitioners to the United Nations for Namibian independence on behalf of the Herero Chief's Council, made the remarks while delivering a motivational speech to Nipam students on Friday where he encouraged them to remain dedicated and committed to the nation.

Original_Search_Title: Ugandans May Be Quiet, but They Are Not Dumb
Headline: Ugandans May Be Quiet, but They Are Not Dumb
Body_Text:
The urge for East African Unity has been on for a long time. Why? First and foremost, unity is strength. The African as a race is the most enslaved, marginalized, despised, humiliated, disregarded, exploited and oppressed human being both at home and abroad by foreigners in his country and, to make matters worse, by his own governments at home. Our dignity is at stake.
Worse still, our emotions seem to be misplaced. When we are told that Hitler killed so many whites, which was inhuman in itself, we cry, but when we are reminded that 150 million Africans perished during the slave trade under the most horrible conditions, we shed no tear. When one Indian is killed in a riot, a minister is sent with mabugo, but no attention is paid to the many blacks killed or affected by the same riot.

Original_Search_Title: NMB Staff Hangs Himself in Mbagala
Headline: Tanzania: NMB Staff Hangs Himself in Mbagala
Body_Text:
FIVE people have died in separate incidents which occurred in Dar es Salaam between Thursday and Friday, the police have said. In the first incident, a 37 years old man identified as Wilhelim Swai committed suicide, hanging himself with a piece of rope.
Temeke Regional Police Commander (RPC), Englibert Kiondo said yesterday that the incident occurred at Masai Guest House, Mbagala in Temeke Municipality, saying the motive of the suicide was not yet established.

Original_Search_Title: Drug Hawkers Back in Abuja Buses
Headline: Nigeria: Drug Hawkers Back in Abuja Buses
Body_Text:
They enter the buses, first, like passengers.But they are not. Capitalising on lower prices, they sell a variety of drugs that they claim cure all kinds of sicknesses, to unsuspecting commuters. But how safe are these drugs, their method of distribution and sources?
'NO PREACHING, NO SMOKING ,NO HAWKING,'is a very familiar warning to virtually every commuter who rides any of Abuja's mass transit buses popular for conveying a large number of people bound for the city centre and back to the suburbs. This notice is placed at the front of the bus, making it an impossible sight to miss.

Original_Search_Title: The Kiremt Lifestyle
Headline: Ethiopia: The Kiremt Lifestyle
Body_Text:
The smell of roasted corn on the cob on the streets of Addis is a weather forecaster, a message of the gloomy and rainy days approaching.
Strolling in Addis and observing the corn vendors reminds everyone of the rain and the associated memory of wearing gabies (warm wraparound) and being with family.

Original_Search_Title: 'How Kennedy Okpara Arrested Me With DSS'
Headline: Nigeria: 'How Kennedy Okpara Arrested Me With DSS'
Body_Text:
Like a criminal on a wanted list, Ovie Edomi, the publisher of The South South International (TSSI) magazine was arrested by officials of Department Security Service (DSS) at Lagos Airport Hotel few minutes before the commencement of the 2014 edition of the magazine's annual lecture on Friday June 27, 2014. Edomi in this interview, days after his release, recounted his ordeal to Senior Reporter, Anthonia Soyingbe of his ordeal.
You were recently arrested and detained by the Department of State Security (DSS). Why were you arrested?

Original_Search_Title: Time to Take Off
Headline: Namibia: Time to Take Off
Body_Text:
While the board of directors of the Namibia Airports Company (NAC) were focusing on 'nice-to-have' projects, and becoming embroiled in the day-to-day running of the company, dishing out tenders, taking joyrides to China and embarking on dodgy restructuring, they flew Namibia's fledgling aviation industry into one hell of a storm.
The NAC owns and manages eight airports in Namibia. That's right, eight (8). Some are not even in operation and the rest are relatively small. It has three main objectives. Three (3).

Original_Search_Title: CORA Holds Sixth Book Party At Federal Palace Hotel Sunday
Headline: Nigeria: CORA Holds Sixth Book Party At Federal Palace Hotel Sunday
Body_Text:
ALL is set for the sixth edition of Committee for Relevant Art (CORA) Book Party in Lagos. For the past six years it has become the tradition of the cultural landscapers to host the 11 authors that make the longlist of The Nigeria Prize for Literature sponsored by Nigeria Liquified Natural Gas (NLNG) Limited, in an interface with the book-loving public. This interface provides booklovers opportunity to interrogate the authors on their creative muse; it also affords the authors to give insight into their writing.
The Book Party has become an avenue for close interaction between authors, publishers, critics and general booklovers. Longlisted authors for this year's drama category include John Friday Abba - Alekwu Night Dance, Patrick Ogbe Adaofuyi - Canterkerous Passengers, Soji Cole- Maybe Tomorrow, Paul Edema - A plague of Gadflies, Jude Idada - Oduduwa, King of the Edos and Onshore Ruth Momodu - No fault of Mine, others are Attah Isaac Ogezi - Under a Darkling Sky, Julie Okoh - Our wife Forever, Ade Solanke - Pandora's Box, Arnold Udoka - Akon and Sam Ukala - Iredi War.

Original_Search_Title: The Chibok Parents, PDP and a Message in the Bottle
Headline: Nigeria: The Chibok Parents, PDP and a Message in the Bottle
Body_Text:
The current angst radiating against many high political office holders is mainly as a result of the insidious disdain they apparently have over those who voted them into office in the first place.
Consequently, the widely reported snub by some Chibok parents directed at the highest echelons of temporal power in the country was a culmination of a seething apathy and not an act of political hijack executed by the federal government's opponents, as claimed by the ruling behemoth.

Original_Search_Title: Collabo Pap - Art Exhibition With a Burst of Colour
Headline: Kenya: Collabo Pap - Art Exhibition With a Burst of Colour
Body_Text:
The Little Art Gallery held an exhibition of five artists at the Village Market. Dubbed Collabo Pap, the exhibition brings together artists with different styles.
One of the artists is Yassir Ali from Sudan. His work is always inspired by movie and culture, something he considers as the oldest civilisation in the whole world. One thing about Ali is that he uses motifs from the old pyramids as a central part of his work. "I take the inspiration and the shape of different motifs to make my art even more abstract," he says. To him, the motifs are more about their beauty than their meaning.

Original_Search_Title: Ugborodo Indigenes Not Ready for Peace, Says Navy
Headline: Nigeria: Ugborodo Indigenes Not Ready for Peace, Says Navy
Body_Text:
Warri — The 21-man Interface Committee for the $16 billion Ugborodo Community gas city project has been accused of working against the entrenchment of lasting peace in the troubled community.
Commander, Nigerian Navy Ship, Delta, Captain Musa Gemu, made the allegations in Warri on Wednesday while speaking with Daily Independent on the refusal of the people in some communities in the area to allow the return of those who left because of the crisis, which bedevilled the community this year.

Original_Search_Title: Former School Dropout Found Success in Business
Headline: Rwanda: Former School Dropout Found Success in Business
Body_Text:
In 2011, unable to pay her school fees, Samilia Niyigena, a resident of Kabaya sector, Ngorolero district, dropped out of school from senior six. Unemployed, she lived in poverty.
But her life was transformed when she secured a loan of Frw 200,000 from Umuntanguha Microfinance Bank and ventured into the business of selling clothes.

Original_Search_Title: No to Anti-Insurgency External Borrowing
Headline: Nigeria: No to Anti-Insurgency External Borrowing
Body_Text:
PRESIDENT Goodluck Jonathan's letter to the National Assembly seeking approval to borrow US$1 billion from abroad as a result of the "urgent need to upgrade the equipment, training and logistics of our Armed Forces and Security Services to enable them more forcefully confront this serious threat" reveals the Jonathan administration's narrow notion of, and ad hoc awakening to the country's security needs. Government has the duty to eliminate any insurgencies in the land. Nevertheless, the National Assembly should summarily reject the President's request "for external borrowing of not more than $1 billion including government-to-government arrangements for this upgrade" for a number of reasons.
Firstly, the request, demeaning as it is, dresses the country in the garb of a beggarly banana republic. It is an obnoxious reminder of the apparent and intentional mismanagement of the resources of the country. Rather, there is need instead to draw the attention of the Jonathan administration to Nigeria's true security needs of constantly upgrading equipment, training and logistics to enable the military and security services to, at all times, not merely confront, as the President intends, but overpower and deter any acts of rebellion. The country possesses the wherewithal to fulfill the national security obligations as will be shown currently.

Original_Search_Title: Star Trail - Bobi Wine Causes Stampede
Headline: Uganda: Star Trail - Bobi Wine Causes Stampede
Body_Text:
Singer Bobi Wine's late arrival at Keron's burial ground at Matugga-Kiryagonja caused a lot of excitement among the locals.
Soon, he found himself surrounded by a crowd of people. This crowd expected Bobi Wine to at least address them. However, knowing that this wasn't a day for speeches, Bobi Wine rushed to the house where Keron's body was lying for public viewing.

Original_Search_Title: The State Must Do Its Part to Create Jobs
Headline: Kenya: The State Must Do Its Part to Create Jobs
Body_Text:
Unemployment among the youth has reached crisis proportions. According to Uganda's long-serving President, Yoweri Museveni, young people in Eastern Africa engage in conflict because of lack of jobs.
Now governments are talking and taking some action. But are governments doing enough?

Original_Search_Title: A Word for the President
Headline: Nigeria: A Word for the President
Body_Text:
SIR: This note is for President Goodluck Jonathan. I am 76 years old, a senior citizen, from Delta State. Mr. President, permit me to use this modest medium to give you some advice, albeit unpalatable, but in the very utmost interest of our nation, especially after reading a very soul-searching article titled "What if, Nigeria" by one Leonard Shilgba, alias "Sad Nationalist (SAN)", in "The Guardian" of Tuesday, June 10, 2014.
By May, 2015, you would have at your young age, by the very special grace of Almighty God, been the President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and Commander-in-Chief of her Armed Forces for six years and during the most turbulent post-civil war period. An enviable advanced democracy like Switzerland has a single five-year tenure for her Presidents. Whether anyone likes it or not, you would have performed to the very best of your ability during this very trying period.

Original_Search_Title: Toyosi Johnson Ornaments Banana Island
Headline: Nigeria: Toyosi Johnson Ornaments Banana Island
Body_Text:
Toyosi Johnson presents a spectacle; no matter the occasion and place, she emblazons that subtle, towering gust of individuality that distinguishes her from the middling crowd, ingeniously and quite brilliantly. The first daughter of late billionaire industrialist, Chief Wahab Iyanda Olayinka Folawiyo, is at it again.
As you read, the exclusive commune of Nigeria's filthy rich, Banana Island, plays host to its newest landlady, Toyosi. The latter recently moved into a magnificent edifice that cost a fortune to site in the newfound haven of the country's high society. The building, an architectural masterpiece, is ornamented with a rich mélange of subtle and elaborate finishing and interior décor that depicts the owner's depth of character and taste for urbane furnishing. To mark her arrival into the world of the superrich, Toyosi invited her closest family and friends to a house warming party at the mansion.

Original_Search_Title: Organic Nature Improves Quality of Life in Africa
Headline: Rwanda: Organic Nature Improves Quality of Life in Africa
Body_Text:
When you move from one hospital to another in western countries you meet many patients with a lot of chronic illnesses whereas the opposite is true in African health settings.
There has been a rise in cases of chronic illnesses in African health set-ups that can be attributed to adaptation of western lifestyle by many Africans.

Original_Search_Title: The Next Generation of Kenyan Exhibitions
Headline: Kenya: The Next Generation of Kenyan Exhibitions
Body_Text:
An art exhibition is something that we are yet to appreciate in Nairobi. For those who do not understand, an exhibition is a public display of works of art such as paintings and sculptures by artists.
The art objects may be showcased in halls, museums, art clubs, and at times in private art galleries. Some or all of the works are presented for sale, usually in private galleries. Sometimes exhibitions are organised on occasions like anniversary, a birthday, or commemoration.

Original_Search_Title: Born to Live and Die in a Country Is a Colonial Illusion
Headline: Tanzania: Born to Live and Die in a Country Is a Colonial Illusion
Body_Text:
THERE are no simple answers to this question but there is no doubt that the promise of a better life draws thousands of African emmigrants to try everything including crossing the dangerous Straits of Gibraltar to try their luck at the "pearly gates of heaven".
There are no easy answers because by the time a man or woman sells every cow or piece of land or came for that matter to attempt this dicey dance with death, they have lost all hope in the possibilities surrounding them and have built an impregnable belief that succour to the oppressed remains behind the deep blue sea and the all encompassing mother sun.

Original_Search_Title: Top Pros Kimani, Orende, Njoroge for 'Sweet Banana'
Headline: Kenya: Top Pros Kimani, Orende, Njoroge for 'Sweet Banana'
Body_Text:
Top local pros, Ali Kimani, Ali Orende and Brian Njoroge will be among a select group of professionals invited to spice up the "Sweet Banana" Golf day at Vet Lab Sports Club today.
The pros will play in a special category with a limited prize fund up for grabs.

Original_Search_Title: Plight of 'Married Bachelors'
Headline: Nigeria: Plight of 'Married Bachelors'
Body_Text:
Men's marital status comprises being either married or single also called bachelorhood. However, work, education, and illness among others have forced some married men to live short or long distances away from their wives.
This makes them to live like bachelors despite being married and they are called "Married Bachelors".

Original_Search_Title: Robbery Syndicate Nabbed
Headline: Ghana: Robbery Syndicate Nabbed
Body_Text:
The Dansoman Divisional Police Command has arrested three individuals believed to have embarked on a robbery spree in Dansoman and its environs.
The suspects, Mohamadu Mumuni, alias Osaman, 20, Ali Mohammed, aka Kosei, 20, both porters, and Nasiru Mohammed Tanko, aka Gaza, 28, all foreigners from Niger and Benin respectively, within a period of an hour and a half robbed three persons at different __cpLocations, according to the police.

Original_Search_Title: Know Your History - the Birth of Nyamirambo
Headline: Rwanda: Know Your History - the Birth of Nyamirambo
Body_Text:
In 1920, the Belgians had already started to occupy Quartier Matheus and its surroundings in Nyarugenge District. The area served as both an administrative and business centre for the colonialists. But in the interest of their businesses, the colonialists decided that their staff - assistants, drivers, messengers and other workers - should live within walkable distance from their work place.
And Nyamirambo happened to be their first choice.

Original_Search_Title: Alert - Families in the Cold After Zanu-PF Arson Attack
Headline: Zimbabwe: Alert - Families in the Cold After Zanu-PF Arson Attack
Body_Text:
A Zanu PF supporter, Simon Makaza, on Saturday burnt over 35 houses belonging to MDC supporters at Banana Groove plot, Ruwa exposing the families including children to the current harsh weather conditions.
Makaza sent in a truck with six men, who burnt the houses forcing the families off the land. An attempt to make a report at Ruwa police station were futile as the officer in charge, said the issue was political hence they could not attend to it. Subsequently, they refused to take down the complainants' report.

Original_Search_Title: Kenya Diverts Qat Exports to Somalia After British Ban
Headline: Kenya Diverts Qat Exports to Somalia After British Ban
Body_Text:
Mogadishu — Imports of qat into Mogadishu from Kenya have soared in recent weeks, ever since the British government implemented a ban on the stimulant last month.
Prior to the ban, the United Kingdom was Kenya's biggest export market for qat, known locally as miraa.

Original_Search_Title: Afolabi - Every Political Party Is Embroiled in Perpetual Crisis
Headline: Nigeria: Afolabi - Every Political Party Is Embroiled in Perpetual Crisis
Body_Text:
Dr. Olugbemiga Afolabi is an Assistant Professor at Department of Political Science, Obafemi Awolowo University, Ile-Ife (OAU). He told TUNDE AKINOLA that lack of internal democracy afflicts all the political parties in the country.
Do you see the APC as losing ground?

Original_Search_Title: Strawberry Flavoured Condoms a Hit
Headline: Zimbabwe: Strawberry Flavoured Condoms a Hit
Body_Text:
NEW flavoured Protector Plus condoms with a sleeker and more stylish packaging have been introduced on the market, replacing the old Protector Plus condoms.
The old condoms, whose packaging featured a happy couple, had become out of date and sometimes unpopular because of their unappealing latex smell.

Original_Search_Title: Walking Down World's Largest Beach Front Garden
Headline: Nigeria: Walking Down World's Largest Beach Front Garden
Body_Text:
Santos, Brazil — One of life's thrilling moment is relieving an event in the Guinness Book of Records and walking down the 5,335 KM length of Santos Beach Garden presented the perfect opportunity for that. OMOLOLA ITAYEMI writes about her day at the beach, the journey there and unique finds in the ancient city of Santos despite the chilly and drizzly weather
When choosing where to go in a new destination, I always look first for something that speaks of the place, either of its history or the life of the people who live there now. On a recent stop in the coastal city of Santos, Brazil, I found a place that does both: it strongly reflects the history of the country as well as the daily lives of Brazilians today and Santos is the testament to that fact. Founded in 1546, Santos is a municipality in the São Paulo state of Brazil. An old city, it is a significant tourist center with a Coffee Museum, where, once, coffee prices were negotiated, a football memorial, dedicated to the city's greatest players, amongst which is Pelé and has large industrial complexes and shipping centers, which handle most of the world's sugar exports and a large portion of the world's coffee exports; as well as a number of other Brazilian exports including oranges, bananas and cotton. It is partially located on the island of São Vicente which harbors both the city of Santos and the city of São Vicente, and partially on the mainland. It is the main city in Paulista Coast. As of 2006, its population was estimated at 418,375. Santos has the biggest seaport in Latin America;

Original_Search_Title: Kabwe Council Pounces On Street Vendors
Headline: Zambia: Kabwe Council Pounces On Street Vendors
Body_Text:
KABWE Municipal Council has confiscated street vendor's merchandise that is left along the shop corridors overnight in the town centre.
The council police in conjunction with the state police raided the town centre and confiscated various items that were left in the corridors.

Original_Search_Title: Etisalat Begins Title Defence As Airtel Recharges for First Title
Headline: Nigeria: Etisalat Begins Title Defence As Airtel Recharges for First Title
Body_Text:
THE 2014 Nigeria Telecommunication Games (NTG) kicks off tomorrow with a novelty match between two finance industry giant, Zenith Bank and Konga, a leading online trading company.
MediaVision Limited, organisers of the games are upbeat about the opening day, which will they disclosed will also feature a colorful ceremony.

Original_Search_Title: Etisalat Begins Title Defence As Airtel Recharges for Honour
Headline: Nigeria: Etisalat Begins Title Defence As Airtel Recharges for Honour
Body_Text:

MediaVision Limited, organisers of the games have been upbeat about the opening day which they disclosed would also feature a colourful ceremony.

Original_Search_Title: LGCAS - SIEC, PDP in Verbal War Over Referendum for LGs Creation
Headline: Nigeria: LGCAS - SIEC, PDP in Verbal War Over Referendum for LGs Creation
Body_Text:
Contrary to allegation by the Ekiti State Peoples Democratic Party that the Independent Electoral Commission(SIEC) has no legal basis, the Commission has declared that it has no legal encumbrance to conduct the Saturday's referendum as part of the procedures for the creation of the proposed Local Government Council Areas initiated by Governor Kayode Fayemi-led Government.
At a press conference in Ado Ekiti yesterday, the Ekiti SIEC Chairman, Mrs Cecilia Adelusi, said the referendum, as part of the procedures for the creation of LGCAs, will be conducted in 137 wards across twelve Local Governments as directed by the House of Assembly in the areas where the new Councils were created.

Original_Search_Title: Impeachment of Opposition Governors Threatens 2015 Polls - Group
Headline: Nigeria: Impeachment of Opposition Governors Threatens 2015 Polls - Group
Body_Text:
Mr. Nyako was impeached by the Adamawa State House of Assembly on Tuesday while his deputy, Bala Ngilari, resigned his position.
Stating that the governor's removal has set a new template for vindictive politics in Nigeria, VOTAS warned that if not checked, the impeachment gale could have spill-over effect in other states where legislators were already pumping the war drums.

Original_Search_Title: Talking to a Rooftop Refugee
Headline: Africa: Talking to a Rooftop Refugee
Body_Text:
Dressed in a lilac golf shirt and blue jeans, Adam is a far cry from other fearless cap and keffiyeh wearing refugees waving from the roof of Gerhart-Hauptmann School in Berlin's artsy Kreuzberg district.
He is one of 40 to 50 refugees from Africa and the Middle East including Tunisia, Chad, Mali and Syria who have occupied the disused school, resisting an eviction order from the local council. After 18 months of up to 500 refugees and migrants living in the school, district authorities ordered the mass removal of people, but a small group of refugees resisted the move.

Original_Search_Title: Rwanda's Artistic Renaissance
Headline: Rwanda's Artistic Renaissance
Body_Text:
I am sitting in the highly acclaimed Heaven Restaurant, Kiyovu district, Kigali. Night has fallen. Behind me lights sparkle across gentle hills and undulating canyons. In front of me a Rwandan jazz band is playing - urbane, tight and soulful. The food is delicious - an international menu prepared with traditional Rwandan ingredients. Tonight, however, I am not here to enjoy the delights of live music and fine dining: I have come to witness the opening of Rwanda's first ever art gallery.
The gallery is located at the front of the restaurant. Its walls painted with colourful rectangles: you cannot miss it. According to Innocent Nkurunziza, cofounder of the gallery and Inema Arts Centre, "Today marks an incredible milestone not only for Inema, but for our country. The Inema Gallery at Heaven Restaurant will be Rwanda's first gallery exclusively devoted to the sale of art." - big news for a country that just twenty years ago experienced the most efficient episode of genocidal massacre the world has ever seen.

Original_Search_Title: Fruit At Musanze's Snow Hotel
Headline: Rwanda: Fruit At Musanze's Snow Hotel
Body_Text:
This is one hotel you won't miss on the approach to Musnze town's central business district from the Kigali side. The tiled, L-shaped three storey structure stands rather imposingly against the backdrop of some of the jagged peaks of the Volcanoes.
Beyond the tiled parking lot and as one walks towards the reception, there is a splattering of murals and art works depicting the country's flora and wildlife -especially the wildlife; huge artistic impressions of mountain gorillas and grey monkeys.

Original_Search_Title: Renamo Violence Blocking Crucial Medicines
Headline: Mozambique: Renamo Violence Blocking Crucial Medicines
Body_Text:
Maputo — Life-saving drugs are not getting through to areas hit by politico-military tension in the centre of Mozambique.
According to the non-governmental organisation RENSIDA (Network of People living with HIV/AIDS), people in Manica and Sofala provinces are being deprived of life prolonging anti-retroviral drugs due to attacks on convoys of vehicle along the country's main north-south highway.

Original_Search_Title: Bussi Island, Where the Hope for Saving Lake Victoria Lies
Headline: Uganda: Bussi Island, Where the Hope for Saving Lake Victoria Lies
Body_Text:
Bussi is not that popular an island. In fact, on hearing of it for the first time, one could think it is one of the 84 Ssese islands, part of which form Kalangala district.
Yet the island, which can be reached within one hour by a simple wooden canoe from Nakiwogo landing site in Entebbe, is actually part of Wakiso district. The island is only about 10 kilometres from Entebbe town. At Bussi, Entebbe international airport remains within sight, and the skies above the island occasionally get noisy as planes land and fly out of Entebbe.

Original_Search_Title: Revealing the Secrets Behind Akpabio's Works
Headline: Nigeria: Revealing the Secrets Behind Akpabio's Works
Body_Text:
Nsikak Idongesit, a public affairs analyst, avers that contrary to critics' claims, Akwa Ibom State Governor Godswill Akpabio acknowledges the contributions of members of his star studded cabinet to the uncommon transformation accomplished by his administration...
Some cynics in Akwa Ibom are of the view that Governor Godswill Akpabio is running a one-man government in Akwa Ibom. For them, unlike Olusegun Obasanjo's government of between 1999 and 2007, where some individual functionaries in the government were spotted out for outstanding performance in that era, all credits in the perceived feat attained in Akwa Ibom since the emergence of Akpabio on the driving seat of the government of the state go to the governor.

Original_Search_Title: Open Land Rwanda Where Rwandan Cultural Symbols Are Preserved

Headline: Nigeria: Police Arraign Two for Joint Act, Criminal Force
Body_Text:
The police on Friday arraigned two men in the Wuse Zone 2, Senior Magistrates' Court, Abuja, for joint act, criminal force, assault, intimidation and mischief.

Original_Search_Title: Kampala Fashion Intro 2014 a Roaring Success
Headline: Uganda: Kampala Fashion Intro 2014 a Roaring Success
Body_Text:
East African fashion scene is becoming more vibrant and dynamic. The Kampala Fashion Intro 2014 took it a notch higher recently. There was a big treat for fashion lovers as exquisite designs from some of the top creative names in the region were showcased. The aim of the event was to promote fashion and the arts as a serious business, while promoting unity in the region.
The event at Kampala Serena Hotel was the precursor to this year's regional fashion weeks to be held in August in Burundi and Rwanda in early November. This will be followed by Uganda's Kampala Fashion Week slated for November. The events will provide networking opportunities for those in the fashion industry.

Original_Search_Title: Simple Home Remedies for Soft, Happy Feet
Headline: Kenya: Simple Home Remedies for Soft, Happy Feet
Body_Text:
Dry, cracked feet are a ghastly sight and living in some parts of Nairobi doesn't make it any easier for your feet. We were all born with soft feet and then dust happened. It is called when dust happens to good ol' soft feet. Wearing sandals over prolonged periods of time is a no-no! Why? Because cracks will replace your feet. You are lucky if you are always in socks and closed shoes as you perambulate around town.
Feet should be clean, soft and attractive so if you happen to suffer from dry, beat up, cracked up feet here are some home remedies to help you get your feet all soft, cute and happy.

Original_Search_Title: Judge Underpins Right to Life in Death Sentence
Headline: Tanzania: Judge Underpins Right to Life in Death Sentence
Body_Text:
Meting out a death sentence is not an easy task. Some justices of the Court of Appeal have different views on whether the punishment befits the offence or violates the Constitution and indeed human rights. This is evident in a case involving Dominick Damian, a resident of Bukoba. Staff Writer FAUSTINE KAPAMA reports:
ON August 27, 2007, at around 6.00 am, while asleep, one Tereza Tryphone heard a woman crying for a help.

Original_Search_Title: Army, UN Failed to Stop Massacre - Apparent Ethnic Attack Kills 30 Civilians
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Army, UN Failed to Stop Massacre - Apparent Ethnic Attack Kills 30 Civilians
Body_Text:
Goma — Congolese forces and United Nations peacekeepers failed to intervene to stop a nearby attack that killed at least 30 civilians. The attack was in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo's South Kivu province on June 6, 2014.
Human Rights Watch research found that a senior Congolese army officer stationed nearby, who had been repeatedly warned and informed of the attack by armed assailants in the village of Mutarule, did not act to stop the killings. Members of the UN peacekeeping mission in Congo, MONUSCO, based 9 kilometers away, were also aware of the attack but did not intervene, and only visited Mutarule two days after the massacre.

Original_Search_Title: Court Slaps Man With Nine Years Jail Term for Defilement

Body_Text:
Mangochi — Mangochi Second Grade Magistrate's Court on Friday convicted and sentenced Sailesi Chibingu to serve nine years imprisonment with hard labour upon finding him guilty of an offence of defilement.
The offence of defilement which is contrary to section 138 of the penal code attracts a maximum punishment of death or life imprisonment when one is found guilty and convicted of the offence.

Original_Search_Title: Nigeria Out Heads High
Headline: Cameroon/Nigeria: Nigeria Out Heads High
Body_Text:
Though they lost 2 - 0 to France, they gave a good account of themselves.
It is without any complex that the Super Eagles of Nigeria went into the round of sixteen encounter that pitted her against France. Playing at the Estadio in Brasilia, the Eagles portrayed a more matured style of coordination especially at the midfield where Mikel Obi and Onazi orchestrated up front.

Body_Text:
To health experts, fasting is of ideal for the proper functioning of the body in some way. To the faithful, it is a show of commitment, a period of meditation when man intercedes with God for so much in the world he lives in and will not be eating food during the intercession.
For whatever reason it may be, going without food is one of the hardest experiences. Like the Star of Kenya published online, we mark the start of Ramadan by looking into how to reduce fatigue, minimise dehydration and stay healthy during the fasting month, especially during the run-up to the World Cup finals.

Original_Search_Title: Ghana Was Disgraced in Brazil
Headline: Ghana Was Disgraced in Brazil
Body_Text:
The Black Stars went to Brazil with the blessing of the 25 million population at the Centre of the Earth to go beyond the quarter-final stage of the 2014 World Cup, and thus, advertise the potential of this nation to challenge for global honours.The President of the Ghana Football Association (GFA), Mr. Kwasi Nyantakyi, National Coach James Kwasi Appiah, as well as Captain Asamoah Gyan, all promised a successful campaign in Brazil.
On Saturday, the first batch of the contingent, made up of sports ambassadors and ministers of state and other state officials, arrived at the Kotoka International Airport in Accra, following the team's failure to reach the knock-out stage of the competition, after losing 1-2 to Portugal. The players themselves were said to have been stranded at their base in Maceio, as GFA officials tried hard to get a connecting flight to take the team back home.

Original_Search_Title: Mchicha Maua! World's Most Delicious Green Indigenous to Africa, Hiding and Free

ABUJA--A third deadly explosion in the Federal capital territory, Abuja in two months, yesterday, exploded at a shopping mall, EMAB Plaza, adjacent BANEX Plaza, Aminu Kano Crescent, Wuse II, at 3. 50 pm. It killed no fewer than 21 persons and injured 17 others. The extremist Boko Haram sect claimed responsibility for the two explosions in April and May in Nyanya, a suburb of the capital.
The quick intervention of soldiers saved the city from more blasts as they gunned down a suspect that was carrying another explosives-laden bag on a power bike. It was later defused by the police anti-bomb squad.

Original_Search_Title: Jonathan and the Igbo Question
Headline: Nigeria: Jonathan and the Igbo Question
Body_Text:
Indeed, the Igbo, easily the most industrious, itinerant and business-inclined ethnic group in Nigeria, had been marginalized, persecuted and witch-hunted by the larger Nigerian society to warrant any further inquiry. This is either because the people defended themselves against an engulfing onslaught from those who hate them without any just cause or because they record tremendous successes against all odds in all fields of human endeavour. During the Administration of Chief Olusegun Obasanjo (1999-2007) when the South East geo-political zone predominately occupied by the Igbo even produced the President of the Senate, it was as if Obasanjo, the first amongst equals in the administration, was out to rubbish the Igbo. Chief Evan Enwerem, the first Senate President was removed in a less than elegant fashion. Obasanjo who ultimately held the Igbo in disdain, also set banana peels before Enwerem's successor, Dr. Chuba Okadigbo, a quintessential Igbo intellectual and was subsequently removed in controversial circumstance. Okadigbo was frustrated out of the ruling Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) which he co-founded to join the opposition party, the All Nigeria Peoples Party (ANPP). We are all conversant with the way he died after being allegedly tear-gassed by security forces loyal to Obasanjo.
Okadigbo's fall from the Olympian height resulted in the ascendancy of Senator Adulphus Wabara as Senate President. We all know how Wabara was framed and disgraced out of office in controversial circumstances. Anyim Pius Anyim stepped in as the new Senate President but was able to weather the storm and gave Obasanjo a good fight but also left without a second term. Then, came the ebullient and fearless Senator Ken Nnamani who fought Obasanjo to a standstill and presided over the latter's disgrace from the Presidency when the Senate rejected Obasanjo's third term bid. While he was fighting Igbo leaders at the National Assembly, he was equally setting the Igbo nation ablaze.

Original_Search_Title: Foreign Investors Have More Faith in Kenya
Headline: Kenya: Foreign Investors Have More Faith in Kenya
Body_Text:
This week, two things of note occurred; one, a bunch of misguided Kenyans set upon fellow citizens in an orgy of destruction and plunder, in the coastal area of Lamu and in the Rift region of Baringo. Second, a battery of foreign investors oversubscribed Kenya's first Eurobond four-fold, offering close to Sh700 billion against the up to Sh174 billion Kenya was seeking.
While the second instance demonstrates enormous confidence in Kenya's fortunes by foreign investors, the first one, and the angry but divergent reactions of Kenyans that followed, demonstrated a worrying lack of faith in the national experiment that is the Republic of Kenya.

Original_Search_Title: The Patient Dog
Headline: Nigeria: The Patient Dog
Body_Text:
I love dogs. In fact, I am crazy about dogs. This craze dates back to my teen. As I grew up, the craze doubled and tripled when I enlisted into the Federal Road Safety Commission. As the Americans would say, I went banana, acquiring all sorts; rott, durbarman, alsthesian, German shepherd. My only check was my take- home. At a point, I had six breeds .I had become a regular so much so that once I point, I pick for the asking from my favorite dog dealer in Abuja named doggy. The costs for these dogs were hidden in so much secrecy from both my wife and my late mother. Both women would have dragged me for spiritual cleansing. My wife would have been the worse. She hates dogs, but loves the puppies for the money they bring-very typical Delta-Ibo that she is. My boys however, are my pride-taking after me as they have caught the dog bug.
Today, I am still excited over this addiction even though I am now contended with just two lovely dogs; ziddane, and shakira.But if you think I am crazy, ask celebrities like Sony Irabo. Like every dog lover, I have had my sad moments. On two occasions I lost Rambo, Rex and Master. Their death broke my heart. Ask my wife and she would tell you how much these dogs mean to my and my household. Each time you ask her to name members of the family; the list was never complete without our dogs' names after our sons'.Well, today's piece is not about dogs although it provides the trust as it reminds me of an advert I stumbled on way back University days. I think that advert was displayed somewhere around Allen/Opebi during the glittering days of the emergence of this highbrow area. It read,' the patient dog eats no bone" in contrast to the old saying that,' a patient dog eats the fattest bone. I can't recollect the agency that came up with the advert. I can't even fathom the product or even the client. All I can remember is that it was a beautiful concept that for me ranks alongside the one on Gulder max beer-"a man was here" and the one on Johnny Walker, 'keep walking". Now I have dogs and I know that when they are hungry or thirsty, patience is not one virtue that they display. When my dogs sight me after work, patience disappears from their dictionary as they wag their tails to show love and to welcome me home. Such gestures, however disappear when they see you as a threat So, to describe dogs as patient was for me misplacing, although it was appropriate in preaching about patience. Therefore, for any creative mind to stand this saying on its head as far back as the 1980s was indeed prophetic as today that advert vividly captures our driving habits which is devoid of respect for one another while patience, a necessary virtue in redressing road crash is rarely imbibed.

Original_Search_Title: Karua Makes Comeback, Supports 'Baba' On Dialogue

Original_Search_Title: ... and Justice for All?
Headline: Nigeria: ... and Justice for All?
Body_Text:
The Criminal Justice System is the set of agencies and processes established by government to control crime and impose penalties on those who violate laws. The system has 5 component parts namely: Law Enforcement, Prosecution, Lawyers, Courts and Reformative Institutions such as prisons, each playing a pivotal role in the criminal justice process. The two legislations dealing with crime are the Penal Code Act applicable in the 19 Northern States of the Federation, Federal Capital Territory and the Criminal Code Act applicable in the Southern States. These two Acts, one must say, are now outdated. Today Nigeria uses a tripartite system of criminal law and justice: the Criminal Code (based on English Common Law and legal practice); the Penal Code (based on Maliki Law and a Muslim system of law and justice); and Customary law (based on the customs and traditions of our people).
There is indeed a dire need for a criminal code that is of universal application across all 36 States and the Federal Capital Territory. Under the Federal system which we practice, the States themselves assume major responsibilities and powers towards their residents, as a result of which there is then no unified criminal justice system in Nigeria. Each State enacts its own laws, though similar with respect to the administration of criminal justice, in its territory. Crime in Nigeria today has taken on a new and modern day dimension, one that our antiquated Criminal Justice System as it stands today, cannot cope with, such is the current level and nature of crime from terrorism, kidnapping and other grave and heinous crimes of our modern age.

Bad governance, massive poverty has transformed tolerance into tinder. Our bird has jumped the snare so many times the paranoia has set it on edge. If we must recapture the essence of our nation, we must rebuild trust and entente. The confusional conference going on is not doing the trick, if anything at all, for the illegality and frivolity that it is, it is exacerbating it.
Here is conventional wisdom out there.

Original_Search_Title: The Football Madness Begins
Headline: Africa: The Football Madness Begins
Body_Text:
The madness is about to begin - in Brazil. Brazil, the nation of carnivals and funfair. This, to them, is another carnival without a carnival queen. Thirty-two countries have spent millions to prepare a select group of men to go to Brazil and do what you would expect children to do in the playground. They would run after an inflated rubber, kick it to their teammates in a passing game until they kick it past the goalkeeper for a goal.
The unfortunate thing about the fiesta is that on every occasion, millions who desperately need exercise would watch twenty-tired men who desperately need rest on television. Some of the watchers would die from heart attack when their teams lose and some would go back home peevish and vent their anger on others. Some would cry themselves to sleep. Some would bet and lose money. It is all part of the madness.

Original_Search_Title: Governor Nyako On Banana Peels
Headline: Nigeria: Governor Nyako On Banana Peels
Body_Text:
Adamawa State Governor, Murtala Nyako is fighting a battle of his life, probably the kind he never fought throughout his career in the navy, leaving on a clean slate. This time round, the slate may not be all that clean. This is not a conventional battle where deadly weapons are tactically deployed. It is a battle fought on the political tuff. No armament, no gloves but your negotiating skills, convincing tongue, wide network of contacts and, of course, a lot of cash to be deployed to the right places at the right time.
Only a naive person will say the retired naval general has not learnt enough political lessons in the last seven years that he has been in the saddle in Adamawa. A deft combination of his military tactics and political experience may see him out of these troubled waters. But if he chooses to behave like the typical African military general and allows pride to take the better part of him then he will get the Balarabe Musa treatment. In politics, 12 months is still very far, so, the state House of Assembly can choose to embarrass him even if it's a couple of weeks to May 29, 2015. If that happens, all his years as governor of the state are rubbished and in the eyes of the law, he was never a governor of the state. Nyako is clearly swimming against the tide, given the legion of forces against him in and out of the state. With the entire 25 state legislators belonging to the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), following the defection of the three All Progressives Congress (APC) members earlier in the week, and his recent controversial secret memo to his fellow Northern governors, where he allegedly accused President Goodluck Jonathan of genocide, giving secret support to the Boko Haram sect, except his fellow Northern governors put aside party affiliation and rally round one of their own whom they see as an elder, I don't see Nyako surviving the present onslaught. But will the PDP governors in the fold be sincere in fighting a battle to save the neck of someone who dumped them for the opposition party? Especially given the fact that the loss of APC means their gain. This is a mission accomplished with an all PDP-member Assembly. The environment is too hostile for Nyako and the ground too hot for him to step on. The case gets worse for Nyako with the political bigwigs in the state, immediate past national chairman of the PDP, Alhaji Bamanga Tukur, former Governor, Boni Haruna, former minister and senator, Jubril Aminu, his political son and former Minister of State for Health, Dr Idi Song, and, of course, returnee former Lagos MILAD, Brig. Gen. Buba Marwa, all formidable forces belonging to the PDP, battling him. Unfortunately, he has no notable politician on his side in the APC except former Vice President, Atiku Abubakar, and they have mutual suspicion for each other over the control of party structures in Adamawa. So, Nyako is more or less like an orphan in Adamawa. What a pity for Baba mangoro!

Original_Search_Title: Fairtrade - How Fair Is It?

If you've read my previous review, you'd know I'm already quite the fan of Shimmy's lunch and dinner menus and the exquisite venue, but picture this: Saturday morning, a lovely sunny day with a slight autumn breeze, fantastic company, the fresh sea air and view of the ocean, and all the while enjoying a five-star breakfast fit for royalty (because that's what you are at Shimmy!).
