Original_Search_Title: Destiny for Nandwa, Gor Out to Rise
Headline: Kenya: Destiny for Nandwa, Gor Out to Rise
Body_Text:
Nairobi — By all accounts, James Nandwa, is a dead man walking at the AFC Leopards' head coach hot seat if reports of his imminent departure are to be believed. With time running out on his tenure, the midweek Kenyan Premier League clash against inform Sony Sugar could not have come at a worse time.
There has been no respite for the Harambee Stars assistant head coach with Ingwe winless in four league matches thus far, a catastrophe considering they were nominated title contenders ahead of the current campaign.

Original_Search_Title: Eviction: No More Chase for Jase
Headline: South Africa: Eviction: No More Chase for Jase
Body_Text:
So after all is said and done, Jase was the weakest link and, as such, had to take the walk of shame like only a good loser can. But loser is such a harsh word. We prefer to call this young and jolly fellow a team player.
As Lungsta crossed over live to the House, the time had come for us to get on with the business of the day. As the Nominated three stood to attention, Lexi's name was the first to be announced as safe; something that Mzamo neither expected nor desired as he grumpily threw himself over the couch and covered his head with a pillow. Yikes Mzamo! You could have shown a little more tact with your 'fabulousity'. Whichever way, this diva was going nowhere.

Original_Search_Title: Communities Where They Couldn't Bury Their Dead!
Headline: Nigeria: Communities Where They Couldn't Bury Their Dead!
Body_Text:
Benin City — Politicians from the People's Democratic Party (PDP) and the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Edo State may have issues with Governor Adams Oshiomhole in the urban Benin-City due to political interests, but people in the rural communities, particularly in Imeagbe, Imiakagbu, Okpekpe and many other communities in Etsako West, East and Etsako Central Local Government Councils all in Edo North see Oshiomhole as a messiah due to what they described as the miraculous jobs he did in their areas. The people go to the mosque, church and shrine praying for the governor to become the President of Nigeria.
One can only understand the reason for their prayers against the backdrop of the history of some of the communities before the coming of the administration of Oshiomhole. The stories of some of the communities were quite emotional when they were told last week by their elders when the governor went to inspect road projects and bridges there.

Original_Search_Title: Yiga Paints His Life
Headline: Uganda: Yiga Paints His Life
Body_Text:
There are many forms of visual arts such as ceramics, drawing, painting, sculpture and photography, among others.
In Uganda, painting is literally the most marketed form of visual arts -with towering names such as Noah Nyanzi, Daudi Karungi, Juuko Hoods and Xenson Ssenkaaba. Thus when Umoja art gallery in Kamwokya organized a joint art exhibition codenamed "The Random Xpression" by Yiga Robert and Maria Laidlaw, I imagined it would be good to try out some new names for a change.

Original_Search_Title: Ice Cream, Love in Butare's Inzozi Nziza
Headline: Rwanda: Ice Cream, Love in Butare's Inzozi Nziza
Body_Text:
In the four years of its existence, Inzozi Nziza ice cream parlor in Butare has amassed a wealth of reputation.
When it opened to business in 2010, presenters at the National University Radio, Salus went into overdrive: "Now that this place is open, we will have more pregnancies on campus." "If you have not taken your girl to this place, then you're not romantic." Such were the taunts thrown at the university students, and it was not for nothing.

Original_Search_Title: Are We Slowly Losing Track Here in Nairobi?
Headline: Kenya: Are We Slowly Losing Track Here in Nairobi?
Body_Text:
Nairobi is certainly a city of contrasts. On the one hand, you have the suburbs as diverse as Karen, Runda, or Muthaiga where residents live a lifestyle that can pretty much be compared to any of the world's major metropolis. On the other hand, you have the hoi polloi, who barely manage to get by on a daily basis. For such a lot, each day is lived at a time. It really would not be far fetched to say that these two different lots do not live in the same country.
For Nairobi Governor Evans Kidero, the challenge then is to act as the middleman between these two diverse groups, and everyone in between. For a man who came from a corporate background, he has really tried to blend in with the masses, although he still has a long way to go. It doesn't matter much that he may be correct in his arguments; that he needs to raise extra revenue in his county. It even does not matter that he makes sensible argument. The real issue is his approach to the Nairobi affairs.

Original_Search_Title: Sweet Poison - Illegal Ripening of Fruits Exposes Millions of Kenyans to Cancer
Headline: Kenya: Sweet Poison - Illegal Ripening of Fruits Exposes Millions of Kenyans to Cancer
Body_Text:
Dr. Mahesh Zagade, Food Commissioner, Maharashtra state, Food and Drug Administration, India
In an investigation in Kenya and India, writer Kiprotich Koros, discovers how corrupt businessmen use cancer-causing chemicals to ripen fruits. Officials now warn millions of people risk early grave by eating fruits laced with these chemicals.

Original_Search_Title: 'Bongo Movie' to Celebrate Three Years, Honour Kanumba

MEMBERS of the domestic film industry, 'Bongo Movie', will mark three years anniversary cerebrations on March 28, this year, when some actors/actress stars, including the late Steven Kanumba and several others, will be honoured.

Bongo Movie's Chairman Steven Mengere, alias Steve Nyerere, said that the cerebrations, which are sponsored by Mabibo Company through its new beer brand, Windhoek Draught, will be held at Arcade Building along Mwai Kibaki Road, formerly Bagamoyo Road, in the city.

Original_Search_Title: Over 100 Women in Nyamirambo Graduate in Craft Skills
Headline: Rwanda: Over 100 Women in Nyamirambo Graduate in Craft Skills
Body_Text:
With the support of Rwanda Association of University Women and the Peace Institute from the Republic of Slovenia,Nyamirambo women in Nyarugege district have formed a center for training in crafts skills. After six months, over 100 women are graduating in craft skills.
Women are trained in making chairs, necklaces, and baskets out of banana fiber, and clothing, earrings,and pillowcase as their main income-generating activities tofight poverty.

The unpredictable Buccaneers lock horns with Timo Tjongarero's youthful outfit fresh from their 3-1 mauling of title chasing African Stars in their last league outing, while the Buccaneers enjoyed a productive weekend, courtesy of a 2-1 away win against relegation-threatened Eleven Arrows.

Original_Search_Title: Nigeria, Africa's Growing Economic Powerhouse
Headline: Africa: Nigeria, Africa's Growing Economic Powerhouse
Body_Text:
With a projected GDP of between $384 billion and $424 billion, the country is set to outclass South Africa.
Reports say Nigeria's economy is expected to grow strongly in 2014, with expansion continuing to be driven by high oil prices and robust domestic demand. This is likely to see the Africa's top oil-producing nation dethrone South Africa and become the continent's largest economy in terms of its GDP size.

Original_Search_Title: Suspect in Mutomo Killings Surrenders

The police are resolving last Thursday's night arson in which five members of a family were burned to ashes at Ngengeta village in Mutomo subcounty, Kitui.
Mutomo police boss Thomas Ngeiywa yesterday said a key suspect in the killings is being held by police in Mombasa county where he gave himself up. He said the suspect could have raided the home with his brother, who is on the run.

Original_Search_Title: Business, Nigeria, Featured, Key Personnel Overhaul That Rattled Aviation Sector
Headline: Nigeria: Business, Nigeria, Featured, Key Personnel Overhaul That Rattled Aviation Sector
Body_Text:
Barely two weeks after the removal of the former Aviation Minister, Stella Oduah, the federal government has sacked some aviation agencies' heads and made new appointments. The sudden removal of these officers keeps industry watchers pondering, Chinedu Eze writes.
It was a mixed grill when the news filtered in on Tuesday afternoon that the Director General of the Nigerian Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA), Captain Fola Akinkuotu; the Managing Director of the Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria (FAAN), George Uriesi; the Managing Director of the Nigerian Airspace Management Agency (NAMA), Nnamdi Udoh, and the Rector of the Nigerian College of Aviation Technology (NCAT), Captain Chinyere Kalu, had been removed. Akinkuotu, Uriesi, Kalu and Captain Usman Muktar, the then Commissioner of the Accident Investigation and Prevention Bureau (AIB), who was deployed as the Director General designate of NCAA, were being hosted by the Director General of the Nigeria Meteorological Agency (NIMET), Dr Anthony Anuforom, who organised a conference on 2014 Seasonal Rainfall Prediction (SRP) at the Nicon Luxury Hotel in Abuja. Unless these agency heads were great dramatists, none of them seemed to have any inclination of what happened few hours later.

Original_Search_Title: Masakadza Looks Beyond Ireland

ZIMBABWE'S clash with Ireland in the preliminary stages of the International Cricket Council (ICC) T-20 World Cup is touted as the potential banana skin for the full-member side, but vice-captain and opening batsman Hamilton Masakadza is already looking beyond the encounter.

Original_Search_Title: Anti-Littering Campaigns Vital

Residents seem to ignore the bins, choosing to dump litter on the streets and pavements.

Maputo — When, in February, the Government and Renamo reached consensus on the revision of the electoral legislation, the Mozambicans started to dream of peace once again, after being under threat for a year.
The agreement meant the end of the clashes between the Defense & Security Forces and the armed men of the Partridge party, and of the attacks along a portion of the Save-Muxúnguè river, where the crossing was being carried out by military escorts. But it did not last long...

Original_Search_Title: Zambezi Issues Flood Alert
Headline: Namibia: Zambezi Issues Flood Alert
Body_Text:
Windhoek — Thousands of villagers in the flood-prone Kabbe Constituency in the Zambezi Region are being advised to prepare themselves and their belongings for possible evacuation to higher, safer ground over the next two weeks should the Zambezi River continue to rise.
Kabbe Constituency Councillor, Raphael Mbala, who is the chairperson of the Zambezi Regional Disaster Risk Management Committee yesterday warned of the possibility of heavy floods should heavy rain keep falling in the Zambezi River catchment areas.

Original_Search_Title: Gay Africa - Casualty of a Different Power Struggle
Headline: Africa: Gay Africa - Casualty of a Different Power Struggle
Body_Text:
Uganda's war over homosexuality threatens to spread to other African countries and has further damaged the increasingly strained relationship between Africa and Western donors. For the donors it is a matter of human rights for minorities - a corner stone of democracy.
For Africa it is part of the push back against the Western donors and the assertion of an African agenda. In Africa's very religious - Christian or Muslim - societies, it is a matter of morality. At best it is a battle between Western human rights and African morality but both suspect the other - quite rightly - of more cynical agendas.

Original_Search_Title: Rwanda Sets Up Cluster Industries
Headline: Rwanda Sets Up Cluster Industries
Body_Text:
Kigali — The government has adopted the cluster system to help Small Medium Enterprises (SMEs) steadily grow.
"So far, we have 20 SME clusters developed and among these, six of them which include the Leather SME cluster, banana wine cluster, Irish potato cluster, Dairy, Wood and Tailoring clusters will be implemented this year 2014", Annoncée Kuradusenge, the Director of Entrepreneurship Skill at the Ministry of Trade and Industry said last week.

Original_Search_Title: How the Presidency Became Vulnerable - Eyiboh, Ex-House of Reps Spokesman
Headline: Nigeria: How the Presidency Became Vulnerable - Eyiboh, Ex-House of Reps Spokesman
Body_Text:
ESEME Eyiboh, a former member of the House of Representatives, in this interview with Charles Kumolu, describes the gale of defections in the National Assembly as an infection on Nigeria's democracy. Eyiboh, quick to add that the development spurred the Peoples Democratic into a self reinventing mission, says the failed attempt by the opposition All Progressives Congress, APC, senators to decamp was outside the ambit of the law. He also speaks on other issues.
You were in the House of Representatives where you left a mark in the last dispensation. Today the gale of defections among lawmakers appears to be having a negative impact on legislative business in same House. Can we know what this augurs for the polity?

Original_Search_Title: ACP Knocks EU for Preconditioning Political Interests
Headline: East Africa: ACP Knocks EU for Preconditioning Political Interests
Body_Text:
The African, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) group states denounced the European Union (EU) for the political conditions the union arrays in trade and economic relations.
Representatives of the East African region under the ACP discussed the fate of the block beyond 2020 and held a three-day consultative meeting at the UNECA this the week. The meeting concerned the future of the ACP group and its relations with the EU, which was founded some 30 years ago aiming to eradicate poverty and enhance the trade and economic muscles of some 79 countries across the African, Caribbean and Pacific countries. Established in 1975, at Georgetown University, ACP was deemed to harness solidarity amongst the member states in the block. For realizing that objective, the then European Economic Community -the current EU- has been in tact funding the ACP block. Out of the several partnership agreements both sides had sustained for years, the Cotonou Partnership Agreement is the current governing arrangement, signed some 20 years ago and nearing expiration in 2020. Following the deadline, regional consultations have been taking place including the East African region talks held in the capital, Addis Ababa from Monday to Wednesday this week. During the meeting, ambassadors, MPs and representatives of civil societies and the private sector of the region, expressed concerns over the existing relations with the EU. Countries like the Sudan furiously condemned the EU for conditioning economic relations for their political interests. Both MPs and ambassadors of the Sudan said that the EU is plunging International Criminal Court (ICC) in a wrong place.

Original_Search_Title: Let's Purge the Ills Plaguing Our Education
Headline: Zimbabwe: Let's Purge the Ills Plaguing Our Education
Body_Text:
Yes, a mathematics lesson was being conducted at a secluded spot in the park where only God knows whether this very lesson did not turn into a biology experiment. So ungodly was the distance between these two that I almost bumped into other people as I kept on walking with eyes transfixed on developments on the park bench.
But I was not the only one suspicious.

Original_Search_Title: 'I Felt Helpless in the Face of Cancer'
Headline: Namibia: 'I Felt Helpless in the Face of Cancer'
Body_Text:
Tuyeimo Haidula accompanied Emma Kambangula on one of her visits to a cancer patient who stays in Klein Windhoek, and she writes about that experience.
ONE of Emma Kambangula's home-based care patients stays with her two daughters in Klein Windhoek. The woman, in her 60s, has stomach cancer.

Original_Search_Title: Of Amaechi Complaints and Obasanjo's 'K-Leg' Regrets
Headline: Nigeria: Of Amaechi Complaints and Obasanjo's 'K-Leg' Regrets
Body_Text:
Former President Olusegun Obasanjo last week visited Port Harcourt at the request of the State Governor,Mr. Rotimi Chibuke Amaechi. Osa Okhomina, who was in Port Harcourt examines the two days visit, the commissioning of projects and the effect on the good governance in the state. Governor Rotimi Amaechi is probably in the best of moods after the last week two-days visit of the Former President,Chief Olusegun Obasanjo to the state.
While the visit showcased the development efforts of the Rivers State Government under the leadership of Governor Rotimi Amaechi,it affords the world and the Rivers people the opportunity to rate the Amaechi's performance through the critical eyes of the Former President.

Original_Search_Title: Food Stalls Erected Near Bunge Demolished
Headline: Tanzania: Food Stalls Erected Near Bunge Demolished
Body_Text:
Dodoma — JUST a day after food vendors brought services near the Parliament, the recently constructed food stalls were on Wednesday morning demolished.
The demolitions are alleged to have been conducted by the Capital Development Authority (CDA) early in the morning, reasons being that the vendors did not have permits to build the stalls in the area.

Original_Search_Title: Zimbabwe's Anti-Gay Stance Is Regrettable
Headline: Zimbabwe's Anti-Gay Stance Is Regrettable
Body_Text:
The continued persecution of LGBT persons and the government's intolerance of Zimbabwe's sexual and gender minorities violates basic rights guaranteed by the constitution and international law. The state must stop wasting time and resources suppressing an essentially private issue
The persecution and discrimination of Lesbians, Gays, Bisexual, Transgender and Intersex (LGBTI) persons has become a common feature in Zimbabwe and Africa in general.

Original_Search_Title: Fighting Fire Disasters
Headline: Nigeria: Fighting Fire Disasters
Body_Text:
Lagos has been the scene of many major fires. Lessons learnt are being put to use to tackle future recurrence, writes Olaleye Akintola.
Daniel Padonu is an unlettered artisan. He nerves himself daily to raise his ebbing standards of living, yet his meagre earnings betray his expectations to live the 'good life' like his educated siblings living in luxury at Magodo-GRA and Banana Island. Daniel's socio-economic background leaves him in forlorn hope around Ebute-Metta in Yaba Local Government Area, where he is a resident in a single-room apartment, with his wife and two kids.

Original_Search_Title: Indian Neuro-Surgeon Arrested for Practising Without Licence
Headline: Nigeria: Indian Neuro-Surgeon Arrested for Practising Without Licence
Body_Text:
Abuja — An Indian Neurosurgeon, Dr. Raju Bhuvaneswara Basina, has been arrested by officials of the Medical and Dental Council of Nigeria, MDCN, for operating without a practice licence.
The 53-year-old doctor was arrested by the Police from Apo Resettlement Division, on Monday, at Asokoro District Hospital in the Federal Capital Territory, Abuja, following a fresh round of onslaught by the MDCN against quackery.

Original_Search_Title: Unions Make Proposals On Aviation Minister Appointment
Headline: Nigeria: Unions Make Proposals On Aviation Minister Appointment
Body_Text:
Lagos — SEVERAL aviation unions in the country have urged President Goodluck Jonathan to appoint an aviation professional as the new Aviation Minister.
At a joint stakeholders' forum in the Lagos, the unions insisted that the President should appoint a versatile aviation professional from among them and not a “mere politician” who they say would “bastardize”the aviation sector.

Original_Search_Title: Indian Neurosurgeon Arrested for Practicing Without Licence
Headline: Nigeria: Indian Neurosurgeon Arrested for Practicing Without Licence
Body_Text:
The Medical and Dental Council of Nigeria (MDCN) has arrested an Indian Neurosurgeon, Dr. Raju Bhuvaneswara for operating without a practice licence.
The council who went with policemen from Apo resettlement Division on Monday in Abuja, arrested the 53 years old doctor at Asokoro District Hospital in the Federal Capital Territory.

Original_Search_Title: Using God As a Lamp Post
Headline: Nigeria: Using God As a Lamp Post
Body_Text:
You may have heard the wicked anecdote about Nigeria's West African neighbours who went to God on a protest mission. The countries which included Benin, Gambia, Senegal, Chad, Niger, Burkina Faso, among others, wanted to know why the Almighty was so partial to Nigeria in the distribution of His earthly bounties. "Look at Nigeria," the protesters began. "She has every natural resource imaginable: oil, gas, gold, silver, uranium, coal, iron ore, bauxite, zinc, you name it. In fact, Nigeria is endowed with nearly 50 of the world's most strategic minerals! And look at us, Almighty Father; you gave us piddling little or nothing!" God is said to have burst out laughing.
After a hearty laugh, God turned to the delegation and asked: "But have you considered the kind of people I put in Nigeria?" This story, apocryphal as it may sound, does one thing for me. It confirms my long held belief that there is nothing wrong with Nigeria except Nigerians. And although we like to hold our leaders exclusively responsible for all our ills, I hold all of us, the leaders and the led, complicit in our failures. God in His infinite mercies gave Nigeria an awful lot more than many great nations can ever have dreamt of, and yet they moved on to greater heights notwithstanding. Indeed I recall the words of an Abuja taxi driver who once said that the only thing Nigeria does not have is NOTHING! I have always held up the example of Japan, a nation that produced nothing out of the ground or the sea other than tuna fish; a nation defeated in the Second World War and atomised by the first and only known atomic bombs used against any nation. Yet in relatively short order, Japan went on to become the workshop of the world, and until a few years ago when the Chinese dragon leap-frogged her, Japan was the world's second largest economy, only next to the USA. There are many other examples of big countries and small nation states that have dug deep into their minds when they had nothing to dig out of the ground. Come to think of it, Britain that struck out across hostile seas to colonise us, what natural resources did they have except coal and until very recently North Sea oil? At the other end of the spectrum there is Dubai, the tiny city state that has seen as much private Nigerian wealth as any major Western capital. Dubai used to be an oil-producing country. But when oil dried up, their rulers faced the challenge of life without their vital natural resource. They dug into their minds, and in their resourcefulness, they turned to real estate, tourism and more recently the new technologies of the knowledge economy that will rule tomorrow's world All these demonstrate quite simply the power of the human mind over minerals and other natural resources. It captures the challenge that man faces when he has perished his perishable natural resources, or had none at all. And that calls to mind what Barack Obama told the world last year: that the future of nations will depend "not on what comes out of the ground, but what comes out of the human mind." And this must raise a question or two for Nigeria as to how it has applied her mind to the development and use of the numerous bounties that God endowed her with. It draws our attention to two ironic examples that should agitate our mind. One is that Venezuela, which has one of the world's largest proven reserves of oil, is also one of the poorest countries in Latin America. The other is a question: why is it that Saudi Arabia, the world's largest oil producer, a nation that literally oils the machinery of the industrialised world, is not counted among the most developed G8 countries in the world? For Nigeria, these raise further serious questions as to why, for instance, in 53 years of independence and mineral exploration, she is still struggling to establish a viable iron and steel industry.

Original_Search_Title: Dangers of Demography 20 Years After Genocide
Headline: Rwanda Twenty Years On - the Dangers of Demography
Body_Text:
April 2014 marks the twentieth anniversary of the Rwandan genocide, three months of violence which saw the massacre of over 800,000 members of the Tutsi ethnic group at the hands of their Hutu compatriots.
Thousands of Hutu moderates were also eliminated in a killing spree which left a third of the country's population either dead or in refugee camps abroad.

Original_Search_Title: Fresh Attack On Izge - Gunmen Level Borno Village in Three Hours
Headline: Nigeria: Fresh Attack On Izge - Gunmen Level Borno Village in Three Hours
Body_Text:
Despite assurances of military strength and capacity in Borno State by the presidency, the insurgents yesterday attacked Izge village again, shooting sporadically and burning the village.
Residents of Izge told the Hausa Service of the BBC that the sect stormed the village, killing several people and burning many houses with impunity in an attack that lasted three hours and 22 minutes, adding that, presently, many had fled the troubled zone to the neighbouring Adamawa State.

Original_Search_Title: Dusi Champ Zondi Wants Non-Stop Title As Well
Headline: South Africa: Dusi Champ Zondi Wants Non-Stop Title As Well
Body_Text:
With less than two weeks' turnaround time between this year's Dusi and Non-Stop Dusi Canoe Marathons, aches and pains have gotten the better of half of the 2014 Dusi winning combination, forcing Andy Birkett to opt out of this year's Non-Stop Dusi and leave his partner, Sbonelo Zondi, to tackle the challenge alone.
Like the Dusi, the Non-Stop takes place between Pietermaritzburg and Durban, but instead of it being a three-day race it is a one-day event on a route that is one of the toughest in the world of canoeing.

Original_Search_Title: Limpopo Aligning Development Strategy to NDP
Headline: South Africa: Limpopo Aligning Development Strategy to NDP
Body_Text:
The Limpopo Provincial Government is in the process of reviewing and aligning its growth and development strategy to the National Development Plan to put the province on a higher trajectory of economic development.
This is according to Limpopo Premier Stanley Mathabatha, who delivered his State of the Province Address at the provincial Legislature on Thursday.

Original_Search_Title: Mourinho Is Uncultured
Headline: Uganda: Mourinho Is Uncultured
Body_Text:
With twelve games left in the English Premiership season, the pressures are expected to rise amongst the top contenders.
That is Arsenal, Man-City, Chelsea and Liverpool. But probably no one expected the pressure to set in first on Jose Mourinho to a point he has reached now.

Original_Search_Title: Boko Haram and a Nation At War
Headline: Nigeria: Boko Haram and a Nation At War
Body_Text:
The Governor of Borno State, Alhaji Kashim Shettima on Monday stirred the hornet's nest when, after visiting President Goodluck Jonathan at the State House, he said: "what we are being confronted with is that we are in a state of war. It is what I came to update Mr. President (about). The sooner we stop playing the ostrich and rise up to the challenges of the day and marshall all resources towards stopping the antics of Boko Haram, the better for all of us."
Shettima was not done: "The bottom line is that we need more resources. In all fairness to the officers and men of the Nigerian Army and police, they are doing their best given the circumstances they have found themselves in. But honestly, Boko Haram are better armed and are better motivated than our own troops. And believe me, I am an eternal optimist as I have always said but I am also a realist. Given the present state of affairs, it is absolutely impossible for us to defeat Boko Haram. Anybody who is following events in this country can attest to the fact that they have a very smooth sail in running over communities, killing people. Have we ever succeeded in thwarting any of their plans? They went to Konduga and did what they wanted to do; they held sway for over four hours before they left. They were in Kauri, Izge and I don't blame the Nigerian military, honestly. We the leaders should be held responsible for our failure in leadership."

Original_Search_Title: The Transformation Nigeria Needs
Headline: Nigeria: The Transformation Nigeria Needs
Body_Text:
Sam Nwaobasi is of the view that contrary to the familiar abuse of power and office, any leadership that observes the rule of law with an exemplary attitudinal disposition is the one that symbolises transformation
To transform, literally speaking is "to give a different form to or change the character of or to alter the nature of something." So, for us in Nigeria, transformation will mean that we must change from what we are used to, to something different, something new. This process of change cannot be an easy one. In Nigeria, we have our definition of what political power must be and how those in positions of authority must behave. For instance, to come away to us in Nigerian as a president, the individual must be hard, loud, tough, and possess the capacity to whip men into line and have his way in all situations.

Original_Search_Title: Obasanjo Says He Is PDP Card Carrying Member
Headline: Nigeria: Obasanjo Says He Is PDP Card Carrying Member
Body_Text:
Port Harcourt — Nigeria's former President, Chief Olusegun Obasanjo, has declared that he is a card carrying member of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) even as he inaugurated projects of Rivers government.
Obasanjo made this known when he spoke with newsmen at the end of his two-day visit in Port Harcourt on Tuesday.

Original_Search_Title: Tigers Ready to Lay Into Rookies United
Headline: Namibia: Tigers Ready to Lay Into Rookies United
Body_Text:
Windhoek — Following an enforced fortnight hiatus, MTC Premiership log leaders Tigers are cautious not to slip on a potential banana peel when the in-form Donkerhoek outfit welcome unpredictable league rookies United Stars, in the second of a double header at Windhoek's Independence Stadium on Saturday 16h00.
Bobby Samaria's charges have been flying high at the top of the country's flagship league's log table since the start of the current campaign and a likely comprehensive win against the league's new kids on the block will put the Shadumbala Boys firmly in the driving seat and all but erase any doubts about their title credentials.

Original_Search_Title: Remembering General Chui Wa Mararo - Was Mau Mau a Revolution Betrayed?
Headline: Kenya: Remembering General Chui Wa Mararo - Was Mau Mau a Revolution Betrayed?
Body_Text:
Little has been written about General Chui wa Mararo (1927 - 1956), the unique and charismatic fighter during Kenya's war of independence, yet he worked hand-in-hand with Field Marshall Dedan Kimathi Wachiuri, the overall commander of Kenya Land and Freedom Army, otherwise called Mau Mau fighters.
Kibara wa Mararo, later General Chui who came from Kamuiru village of Mutira location, Ndia Division, of the present day Kirinyaga County, became a household name and a hero to the Africans after the famous Mbaara ya Roe Roiro (battle of river Ruiru). In this war of 1953, which took place on the border of Nyeri and the old Embu district, Kibara wa Mararo camouflaged as the overall Inspector of the police.

Original_Search_Title: I Remain in PDP - Olusegun Obasanjo

Body_Text:
Nigeria's former President, Olusegun Obasanjo, on Tuesday said he is still a card carrying member of the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, even as he inaugurated projects of the Rivers government.
Mr. Obasanjo made this known when he spoke with journalists at the end of his two-day visit in Port Harcourt on Tuesday.

Nigeria's former President, Chief Olusegun Obasanjo, has declared that he is a card carrying member of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) even as he inaugurated projects of Rivers government.
