Original_Search_Title: Couple Poisons Sick Baby
Headline: Zimbabwe: Couple Poisons Sick Baby
Body_Text:
A Chinamhora couple connived to kill their one-year-old baby by feeding him with a banana sprinkled with rat killer after establishing that his illness was getting worse last Friday. Mashonaland East acting provincial police spokesperson Assistant Inspector Nobert Muzondo confirmed the incident.
He said they had since arrested Kudzi Jeremiah (26) and his wife Agnes Masunga (17) from Mungate Village under Chief Chinamhora in connection with the murder case.

Original_Search_Title: Bonzer Breakfast At the Bronte
Headline: Zimbabwe: Bonzer Breakfast At the Bronte
Body_Text:
For the first time in ages the sun was shining when I drowsily opened the curtains of my cottage at dawn on Wednesday.
No solid grey skies, no drizzle, no rumbling thunder; the trees had even stopped dripping torrents from the usual overnight heavy precipitation.

Original_Search_Title: Yanga Rout Komorozine At Home
Headline: Tanzania: Yanga Rout Komorozine At Home
Body_Text:
MAINLAND envoys in CAF Champion League, Young Africans have confirmed their date with record African champions, Al Ahly of Egypt; with a 5-2 rout of host Comoro's Komorozine in the preliminary round second leg match in Moroni.
Yanga marched into the first round of the continental premier club championship with a 12-2 aggregate victory, after a 7-0 annihilation of the Comorians in the first leg encounter at the National Stadium in Dar es Salaam last Saturday.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni to Kadaga, MPs On Homosexuality Bill - What Sort of Parliament Is This?
Headline: Uganda: Museveni to Kadaga, MPs On Homosexuality Bill - What Sort of Parliament Is This?
Body_Text:
I have received the bill you passed against homosexuals.
Before I comment about the bill, however, I would like to first discuss about the method of work and the general plan for recovery and development ofUganda as conceived by the NRM for the last 48 years.

Original_Search_Title: Growth in Industrial Output Is Good News
Headline: Rwanda: Growth in Industrial Output Is Good News
Body_Text:
That the Rwandan economy posted impressive growth in the wake of a weaker global economy in 2013, is neither new nor a surprise.
Even though the economy grew at a slower pace (below 6 per cent or there about) compared with last year's 8 per cent, creation of wealth in the country nonetheless expanded above the global average GDP growth of 3 per cent. That too, is not news.

Original_Search_Title: 'New' Sudan - Back to the Future
Headline: Sudan: 'New' Sudan - Back to the Future
Body_Text:
The sight of Hassan al-Turabi in his impeccably white jellabiya, socks and shoes, seated in the front row next to Sadiq al-Mahdi and Ghazi al-Attabani, awaiting President Bashir to ascend the podium and deliver his 'surprise speech' on 27 January, was sufficient to signal to Khartoum's political pundits that a grand bargain was underway in the capital's saloons. Seated in the comfortable Chinese-built hall were essentially all the President's love victims: Sadiq al-Mahdi the prime minister deposed in the 1989 coup; Hassan al-Turabi, the veteran sheikh evicted from power in the 1998/1999 showdown; Ghazi al-Attabani, the aspiring Islamist moderniser disciplined out of the NCP last November into the wilderness of an abortive sectarian split; Ali Osman Mohamed Taha and Nafie Ali Nafie, the sacrificial lambs of the NCP's reform agenda, effectively purged out of government in the company of allies last December, in addition of course to the honourable Mahdi and Mirghani juniors, Sadiq al-Mahdi's oldest Abd al-Rahman and Mohamed Osman al-Mirghani's youngest Jaafar al-Sadiq, training on the job as assistants to the President. Order prevailed in the first and second rows, but from the third backward hierarchy was hard to ascertain, the scene closer to male congregations in a Sudanese bika (mourning house) than the audience of a state sovereign. Indeed, the event only took off when the President finished speaking and the guests could mingle at will, the well-ironed jellabiyas swooshing in tandem in the glow of the neon lamps.
The seventy years old President strained to read what his cooks had prepared, a speech written in the artificial Arabic of themuthagafattiya, a dismissive term used to refer to pretentious intellectuals obsessed with convoluted form, and peppered with words dug out of a medieval dictionary as a measure of Islamic ornamentation. Had the President been in the class of an Arabic teacher of an old mould he would have surely failed 'reading', the vexing sentence structures and demanding grammar had him almost prostrating were it not for the intensive air-conditioning. Turabi, grimly composed, could not hide his disregard for the dyslexic officer seated above at presidential distance, grimacing in his distinctive manner in response to the embarrassing flow of errors. The backbenchers just chatted the time away undeterred by the passage of the cameras. The press had primed the public for a 'surprise', speculations founded on a remark by the former US president Jimmy Carter during his visit to Khartoum a week before and actively nourished by the NCP's high priests. Carter told reporters after meeting President Bashir that the President would soon make "important" decisions. Predictions were that the President would resign and pass the baton to his new deputy Bakri Hassan Salih as head of a 'transitional' all-parties government. Apart from the injunction to place national interests above petty partisan concerns, ripped out of the pages of the 'patriotic education' curriculum that Sudanese pupils had to endure under Nimayri's reign, the President offered the political club gathered under his watch an abstract invitation to dialogue with the ruling party on reform of the political system through the drafting of a new constitution and multi-party elections scheduled for 2015, peaceful resolution of armed conflicts in the country, economic reform, and closure on the contentious issue of Sudanese identity.

Original_Search_Title: Storm Unroofs Over 85 Houses in Hai District
Headline: Tanzania: Storm Unroofs Over 85 Houses in Hai District
Body_Text:
Hai — A STORM that has been raging in Kilimanjaro region has unroofed more than 85 houses in Hai district.Strong winds accompanied by heavy rains swept through residential areas, unroofing classrooms and teachers' offices in several schools, and killing a person in its wake.
Reports which were confirmed by Hai District Commissioner, Mr Novatus Makunga, had it that 85 households were going without shelter.

Original_Search_Title: Confident Azam Depart for Beira
Headline: Tanzania: Confident Azam Depart for Beira
Body_Text:
MAINLAND representatives in CAF Confederation Cup Azam FC departed on Wednesday for Beira, Mozambique ahead of their return-leg match against hosts Ferroviario de Beira scheduled for Saturday.
Azam, under the tutelage of Cameroonian Joseph Marius Omog, headed to Beira looking to defend their 1-0 first-leg win as they seek to reach the first round of the continental second tier for a second year running. The Premier League leaders left Julius Nyerere International Airport on Wenesday morning with a 26-man contingent comprising 21 players and five officials for the decisive second-leg encounter.

Original_Search_Title: Artists Seek to Transform Nairobi Art Scene
Headline: Kenya: Artists Seek to Transform Nairobi Art Scene
Body_Text:
On February 2, a group of four artists came together for an art exhibition at the Four Cafe in Gigiri.
Gemini Vaghela, Poonam Suryavanshi, Natasha Joshi and Onyis Martin have been interested in art since they were kids, and they are grateful for the spaces they have to display their creativity.

Original_Search_Title: Trades Blame MCAS for Taxes
Headline: Kenya: Trades Blame MCAS for Taxes
Body_Text:
Levy woes are far from over after blame shifted from Kiambu Governor William Kabogo to members of the county assembly.
More than 300 traders in Banana town staged a protest yesterday against the high levy charges by the county government blaming the members of the county assembly for passing the finance bill without involving them and was hurting their businesses.

Original_Search_Title: Timeout for Simba to Lick Wounds
Headline: Tanzania: Timeout for Simba to Lick Wounds
Body_Text:
MAINLAND giants Simba are in the odd position of being outside the top group in the Premier League and are likely to miss out on continental football for the second year running.
The former league champions rest in fourth place on the standings with 31 points from 17 games and are five points adrift of league leaders Azam, who, however, could extend that gap to eight points if they win their game on hand.

Original_Search_Title: If Our Youth Won't Read, Let Them Play
Headline: Rwanda: If Our Youth Won't Read, Let Them Play
Body_Text:
In the halcyon days when education was education and not something that a computer game could sneer at, I joined the class of a select few. These were a different species from the mortals that trod refugee camps, those days, known as Abasiniya.
In the refugee camps of Uganda in the 1960s, when Umusiniya (one) passed, a hush always fell over any group of people in conversation. People started throwing furtive glances at you and whispering: "That's the son/daughter of Kanaka. Isn't that father a lucky man?"

Original_Search_Title: How Obasanjo, APC Leaders Wanted Me to Rot in Jail in Benin Republic -- Dokubo-Asari
Headline: Nigeria: How Obasanjo, APC Leaders Wanted Me to Rot in Jail in Benin Republic -- Dokubo-Asari
Body_Text:
The leader of the Niger Delta People's Volunteer Force, NDPVF, Mujahid Dokubo-Asari has alleged that former President Olusegun Obasanjo as well as some leaders of the All Progressives Congress, APC, wanted him to rot away in detention in Benin Republic to enable them perpetrate electoral malpractices in the coming general elections.
He specifically alleged that Mr. Obasanjo plotted his arrest recently in the Republic of Benin, when he (Obasanjo) sent his name alongside others to the INTERPOL, asking it to arrest and detain the neighboring West African country.

Original_Search_Title: Little Somalia - Home Away From Home
Headline: Somalia: Little Somalia - Home Away From Home
Body_Text:
The troubles in Somalia and the surrounding regions have led to many fleeing in search of a better or more stable life. Addis Ababa is often the first destination as plans are made for the future and travel arrangements finalized.
One area in particular has become home for many Somalis, and Tibebeselassie Tigabu visited it to find out more about the vibrant and unique place.

Original_Search_Title: Genocide Survivor's Chilling Tale of Kabuye Massacre
Headline: Rwanda: Genocide Survivor's Chilling Tale of Kabuye Massacre
Body_Text:
At the time she lost her siblings and many other relatives at the hands of a genocidal regime and its allied militia, Francine Kampororo was aged 23.
She vividly remembers how her loved ones were slaughtered on a small hill in Gisagara District, the place where thousands of Tutsis were led to before their untimely death during the 1994 Genocide against the Tutsi.

Original_Search_Title: Central African Republic - Seleka Fighters Regroup in North - Rogue Chadian Peacekeepers Facilit
Headline: Central African Republic - Seleka Fighters Regroup in North - Rogue Chadian Peacekeepers Facilitate Armed Leaders' Movement
Body_Text:
Bangui — Armed Seleka commanders and fighters are leaving their bases in Bangui, the capital of the Central African Republic, regrouping in northeastern towns, and engaging in a new wave of horrific attacks against civilians. In some cases, Chadian peacekeeping troops have facilitated the movement of armed Seleka leaders complicit in grave abuses.
Seleka forces in January 2014 tortured and killed civilians in and around the town of Sibut, where the former rebels have been regrouping, Human Rights Watch said. Seleka forces were able to leave bases to which they had been confined by African Union peacekeepers by using bush roads to circumvent checkpoints or by traveling with Chadian troops in heavily armed convoys.

Original_Search_Title: Time to Invest in Somalia
Headline: Somalia: Time to Invest in Somalia
Body_Text:
"I will tell you how to become rich. Close the doors. Be fearful when others are greedy. Be greedy when others are fearful." - Warren Buffett.
That seems to be a good adage for opportunities that abound for neighbor and sister country Somalia. Somalia is planning on their second Somalia Investment Summit (SIS) 2014 slated for Dubai in the United Arab Emirates from April 6-7.

Original_Search_Title: What If We Celebrate 62 Years of Football?
Headline: Gambia: What If We Celebrate 62 Years of Football?
Body_Text:
The founding members of the Gambia Football Federation would be excited with the celebration of their efforts after the establishment of the GFA in 1952 in the form of a Gala and award dinner, where the GFF could raise funds and honour administrators, coaches, team doctors, referees, players, journalists as well as government, public and private institutions who have played an important role in the promotion and development of football in the Gambia including Confederation of African Football (CAF) and FIFA.
Sixty-two years of football development in the country is a unique and special achievement that represents concrete and meaningful progress and it is only natural to award and applaud the steadfast efforts and commitment of the past (some deceased) and present field generals of our sport in a weekend of reflection, reunion and renewal, and the baby face administrators at the Football House would win applause and admiration for this unique initiative.

Original_Search_Title: Duty Waiver Scandal Revealed (II)
Headline: Nigeria: Duty Waiver Scandal Revealed (II) - the Gap Between Theory and Practice - II
Body_Text:
"In addition some waivers and exemptions make up the gaps in our economy; for example waivers to bring in vehicles for sporting events and conferences." Dr Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala
Typical of Dr Ngozi Okonjp-Iweala, she provided no evidence that those sporting events or conferences would have been scrapped without granting those waivers. I live and work in Lagos and even watched some of the events; I saw the vehicles bringing sports people, officials and media. I cannot remember seeing a vehicle which could not have been available without Duty Waiver. Perhaps somebody will, after this article, show me one.

Original_Search_Title: The Epidemic That Killed 71 People
Headline: Nigeria: The Epidemic That Killed 71 People
Body_Text:
CHOLERA EPIDEMIC RECENTLY RAVAGED COMMUNITIES IN KANO STATE, KILLING ABOUT 71 PERSONS. IT CAME AT THE HEELS OF SIMILAR OCCURENCE IN LAGOS STATE WHERE SOME PEOPLE LOST THEIR LIVES. EXPERTS AND INTERNATIONAL HEALTH ORGANISATIONS HAVE SAID THAT THE EPIDEMIC IN KANO IS A REFLECTION OF THE HEALTH INADEQUACIES OF THE COUNTRY.
Health officials and international health organisations were overwhelmed at the recent cholera outbreak that ravaged some communities in Kano State leaving about 71 people dead and scores of others still receiving medication in health institutions. The cholera outbreak in Kano, experts said, was caused by consumption of unhygienic foods by the residents, especially with the high dependency on fruits by the residents.

Original_Search_Title: Mr President, Rivers State Could Be Your Banana Peel
Headline: Nigeria: Mr President, Rivers State Could Be Your Banana Peel
Body_Text:
I get uncomfortable, peeved even, when I hear the sweeping statement that the Presidency has achieved nothing in what? Six years? It is a statement that is shallow at best and, stretched further, uncharitable. Even Lucky Igbinedion, as incompetent as he was, achieved 'something' in Edo State.
An unbiased assessment of the critical areas of the polity--the economy, security, democracy, institutions of governance--will show that this administration has not fared any worse than its predecessors. It might not be the transformational government it is touting itself to be and might even be described as mediocre given the national resources, but I do not in all honesty, believe that we are worse off in critical areas (save for corruption) than in Yar'Adua's or even Obasanjo's governments.

Original_Search_Title: Kibeho - When the 'Holy Land' Was Turned Into 'Butcher Land'
Headline: Rwanda: Kibeho - When the 'Holy Land' Was Turned Into 'Butcher Land'
Body_Text:
THIRTEEN YEARS after the famous Kibeho apparitions, the 1994 Genocide against the Tutsi swept across the country, leaving more than a million people dead in its wake.
Kibeho, which had since the apparitions become a 'Holy Site' for Christians, was not spared once the killings erupted. It soon turned into a major site of massacres.

Original_Search_Title: Zone Six Table Tennis On
Headline: Zambia: Zone Six Table Tennis On
Body_Text:
THE Confederation of Southern Africa Table Tennis Association (CATTA) Zone Six Senior Championship opens today at the Olympic Youth Development Centre (OYDC) with Zambia banking medal hopes on 22 players.
But the 22, including rising star Choolwe Hamalambo, will have to fight off tough opposition in the form of defending champions, South Africa.

Original_Search_Title: Partnership With Dole Fruit to Boost Banana Production
Headline: Mozambique: Partnership With Dole Fruit to Boost Banana Production
Body_Text:
Maputo — The company Dole Fresh Fruit Europe has announced a long term partnership with Matanuska Africa Ltd, aimed at increasing banana production in Mozambique.
Matanuska Africa Ltd is a subsidiary of the Rift Valley Corporation, which is active in Mozambique, Zimbabwe and Tanzania.

Original_Search_Title: Transport Minister Inspects Fako Airstrips
Headline: Cameroon: Transport Minister Inspects Fako Airstrips
Body_Text:
Prof. Robert Nkili Tuesday January 27 evaluated infrastructure in Tiko and Buea.
In continuation of his nationwide deployment to redynamise airspace transportation, the Minister of Transport, Professor Robert Nkili, made a stop-over in Fako Division on 28 January where he inspected the old-time airstrip of Tiko and helicopter landing spaces in Buea. Minister Nkili instructed that the entrance street to the Tiko airstrip be enlarged to enable easier access and hitch-free use during the reunification anniversary event.

Original_Search_Title: Mengo Names New Ssabanyala
Headline: Uganda: Mengo Names New Ssabanyala
Body_Text:
In a move that could significantly test the marked thaw in Buganda kingdom's relations with the central government, the Mengo establishment this week named its own recognised cultural leader of the Banyala people.
Prince Fred Kayemba is Mengo's choice for 'Ssabanyala', a title Buganda does not recognize, instead preferring Namuyonjo. But this could also complicate relations with Banyala secessionists led by a retired UPDF Major Baker Kimeze, hitherto the only significant claimant to the Ssabanyala throne. Prince Kayemba, a rival to Kimeze, was introduced to the Banyala on January 29 at the climax of Kabaka Ronald Mutebi's three-day tour of Bugerere (Kayunga district).

Original_Search_Title: Kenyan President Roots for African Army
Headline: Africa: Uhuru Roots for an African Army
Body_Text:
Addis Ababa — President Uhuru Kenyatta has challenged African countries to scale up their contribution to peace and stability in the continent.
He backed the call made by the African Ministers of Defence for all countries to meet their obligations in ensuring an African Standby Force is ready for deployment by 2015.

Original_Search_Title: President Mugabe Calls for African Renaissance
Headline: Africa: President Mugabe Calls for African Renaissance
Body_Text:
President Mugabe has implored Africa to re-examine its independence from colonial rule and urged fellow African leaders to be politically-conscious in order to realise the continent's long-held vision of development. The President appealed to the African Union to spearhead unity and co-operation across the continent.
Speaking at a briefing with senior Government officials who attended the AU Foreign Ministers Retreat here, President Mugabe said taking charge of natural resources was the first step towards the African renaissance.

Original_Search_Title: Fish Farmers Berate Govt's Support in Kebbi
Headline: Nigeria: Fish Farmers Berate Govt's Support in Kebbi
Body_Text:
Birnin Kebbi — The increase in the 2014 budget allocation to N23 million from N13 million in 2013 for fishery by Governor Saidu Dakingari of Kebbi State was greeted by wide commendation in the state.
The governor made the announcement when he presented the 2014 budget proposal to the House of Assembly in Birnin Kebbi the state capital.

Original_Search_Title: Mu'azu Must Avoid Pitfalls of His Predecessors - Ugboho
Headline: Nigeria: Mu'azu Must Avoid Pitfalls of His Predecessors - Ugboho
Body_Text:
The president of National Consolidation Group, Hon. Ajon Ugboho, has advised the new national chairman of Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), Alhaji Adamu Mu'azu, to avoid those banana peels that caused the ouster of his predecessors by ensuring internal democracy and fair play within the party.
Speaking with LEADERSHIP in Abuja yesterday, Hon. Ugboho said Mu'azu should carry every stakeholder along in the activities of the PDP, in order to give members a sense of belonging, noting that lack of internal democracy had been the major cause of crisis in the party.

Original_Search_Title: CERAC Communes With Leprosy Victims
Headline: Cameroon: CERAC Communes With Leprosy Victims
Body_Text:
Former patients at Ayos in the Centre Region yesterday, January 26, 2014, received medical and material gifts from the First Lady's association.
"You are part of the citizens of Cameroon. Yes, dear former leprosy patients at the Dou'ou Bekolo neighbourhood in Ayos, you are part of the Cameroonian society. You have your place in the society, so feel free".

Original_Search_Title: Yuguda, Mu'azu and Banana Peel
Headline: Nigeria: Yuguda, Mu'azu and Banana Peel
Body_Text:
Bauchi State Governor Isa Yuguda did the unthinkable on Monday, agreeing to the emergence of the new Peoples Democratic Party Chairman Adamu Mu'azu from his state. Yuguda in fact moved the motion for the appointment of Mu'azu at the party's Special National Executive Committee meeting in Abuja.
He refused to toe the line of some other PDP governors, past and present, who clearly opposed the idea of picking PDP chairman from their states. It was to avoid the kind of bitter rivalry that usually plays out between governors and PDP chairmen from the same states. Almost throughout his tenure, former PDP chairman Okwesilieze Nwodo fought with Enugu State Governor Sullivan Chime over the party structure in the state. That rivalry between the two largely contributed to the fall of Nwodo as chairman. It was the same story recently between the immediate-past PDP Chairman Bamanga Tukur and Adamawa State Governor Murtala Nyako. The relationship between the two got so bad that it engendered two parallel PDP executives in the state until Nyako left the party for the All Progressives Congress. Will the story be different for Yuguda and Mu'azu in the fullness of time?

Original_Search_Title: Rwanda Has More Than Just Gorillas
Headline: Rwanda Has More Than Just Gorillas
Body_Text:
Let me start with a confession: The only things that I knew about Rwanda as I boarded a plane from Germany to Kigali last summer was genocide and gorillas.
On this one, I am not alone because most average Europeans know this country by only these two things despite abundance of information online.

Original_Search_Title: Mara Road Repair Plan Focuses On Tourism
Headline: Tanzania: Mara Road Repair Plan Focuses On Tourism
Body_Text:
MARA regional leaders are planning to upgrade Tarime- Mugumu road from gravel to tarmac level in an effort to speed up tourism activities in western Serengeti.
The plan comes at a time when the number of tourists entering the country through the Tanzanian / Kenyan Sirari border and later using Mugumu route, on their way to Serengeti National Park (SENAPA), has increased sharply.

Original_Search_Title: Joburg's Latest Mystery - a Rocket-Head of Possibilities
Headline: South Africa: Joburg's Latest Mystery - a Rocket-Head of Possibilities
Body_Text:
The press release didn't mention whether one of the two men arrested inside the Mercedes-Benz was stroking a white Persian cat when the cops pounced, but it did say that a "rocket head" worth R8-million was found inside the vehicle and promptly (and carefully) confiscated.
A rocket head. Worth R8 million. In downtown Johannesburg. The mind of ALEX ELISEEV was suddenly alive with possibilities.

Original_Search_Title: Jonathan Will Contest in 2015 - Birmah
Headline: Nigeria: Jonathan Will Contest in 2015 - Birmah
Body_Text:
Alhaji Dauda Birmah, a former minister of education and three times presidential aspirant of the defunct ANPP is now in the PDP. He speaks on the intrigues that led to the resignation of Alhaji Bamanga Tukur as the PDP national chairman, and also says President Jonathan will seek re-election in 2015. Excerpts:
What do you make of the recent resignation of Alhaji Bamanga Tukur as the PDP national chairman?

Original_Search_Title: Russian Orthodox Christmas and New Year Party
Headline: Kenya: Russian Orthodox Christmas and New Year Party
Body_Text:
One of the interesting pieces of art on display at the Russian Embassy during their orthodox new year celebrations.
Radio Africa Group vice chairman Sudi Vidyarthi, his wife Nana and ICMAS consultant obstrecian and gynaecologist Dr Rafique Parkar at the Russian Embassy during their orthodox new year celebrations.

Original_Search_Title: Mu'azu Sworn-in Chairman of PDP, Pledges Reconciliation
Headline: Nigeria: Mu'azu Sworn-in Chairman of PDP, Pledges Reconciliation
Body_Text:
The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) Monday opened a new chapter in its history with the ratification, by the party's National Executive Committee (NEC), of former Bauchi State Governor, Alhaji Adamu Mu'azu, as its national chairman.
Mu'azu, who emerged the consensus candidate after a series of meeting between President Goodluck Jonathan, governors elected on the platform of the party and other party bigwigs, the last of which ended in the wee hours of Monday, succeeded Alhaji Bamanga Tukur who "stepped aside" last Thursday.

Original_Search_Title: Traders Protest 400 Per Cent Rate Rise in Kongowea
Headline: Kenya: Traders Protest 400 Per Cent Rate Rise in Kongowea
Body_Text:
NOT BOUNTY: Traders at Kongowea market Mombasa in September 2010. Photo/FILE
Traders in Kongowea market have vowed to block food delievered from other regions into Mombasa county. They are protesting a move by the county government to increase rates charged on food in the market by up to 400 per cent.

Original_Search_Title: Power Thief Dies On Pole
Headline: Liberia: Power Thief Dies On Pole
Body_Text:
"99 days for rouge, one day for master", says a famous Liberian parlance, and such was the case when a power theft suspect Acqua Massaquoi's remains were horribly found hanging upside down with bare feet on a light pole of the Liberia Electricity Corporation or LEC in New Kru Town on Bushrod Island early Sunday, January 19, 2014.
Hundreds of New Kru Town residents and surroundings mixed with kids, gathered yesterday morning at the incident scene to view the victim's corpse hanging to the LEC power pole.

Original_Search_Title: Nothing Sinister About 'Munotidako' Lyrics - Moyana
Headline: Zimbabwe: Nothing Sinister About 'Munotidako' Lyrics - Moyana
Body_Text:
Sometime in early 2013 in the Beitbridge suburb of Baobab Jacob Moyana was invited for lunch by his friend-cum-manager Joshua Musimbe.
As he and his band members waited for the meal, relaxing on an orchard lawn, Musimbe called out and Moyana responded; "Munotidako here Bra Josh?" inviting rapturous laughter.

Original_Search_Title: Family Minister Announces Completion of Caxicane Village in July
Headline: Angola: Family Minister Announces Completion of Caxicane Village in July
Body_Text:
Icolo e Bengo - The 600 residences of the Caxicane new village project may be completed by next July and will house 480 families who live in rural area, most of them in dangerous zones, Angop learnt on Friday from the Minister of Family and Women Promotion, Filomena Delgado.
The Cabinet minister who made this morning a working visit to the new village of Caxicane where 300 peasant families are now living, checked the conclusion of the integrated center for public services, the training center for entrepreneurs "Saber Fazer" that includes rooms for computers training, cooking , sewing and maintenance of their homes.

Original_Search_Title: Protesters Shooting U.S Zuma Pressure
Headline: South Africa: Protesters Shooting U.S Zuma Pressure
Body_Text:
THE South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC) is meeting President Jacob Zuma's government about increased police brutality in South Africa, which some observers are now equating to the jackboot tactics last experienced during the dark ages of apartheid.
This follows the death of three protesters in Mothutlung in the North West province during service delivery protests this week.

Original_Search_Title: Diplomats Behaving Badly
Headline: Kenya: Diplomats Behaving Badly
Body_Text:
Diplomats, normally discreet figures who rarely court publicity, have been in the news a lot lately, for all the wrong reasons. Two recent arrests of diplomats by their host countries have put a spotlight on the justification for, and limits of, the immunity from local law that such officials typically enjoy.
In the first case, Dmitri Borodin, the minister councilor at the Russian embassy in The Hague, was arrested late one night in October of last year, after neighbors alerted the Dutch police that Borodin, allegedly in a drunken state, was beating his two small children. He was handcuffed in his own home and taken to the police station.

Original_Search_Title: Brazil 2014 - Nigeria Wary of Bosnian Danger
Headline: Nigeria: Brazil 2014 - Nigeria Wary of Bosnian Danger
Body_Text:
The Nigeria Football Federation (NFF) has warned that Bosnia-Herzegovina is a potential banana peel and a team that cannot be waved off by the Super Eagles at this year's FIFA World Cup finals in Brazil.
Nigeria will meet the former conflicts-torn European side at the World Cup in Cuiaba on June 21 in Group F that also boasts of Iran and Argentina.

Original_Search_Title: South Sudan Conflict Squeezes Regional Economy
Headline: East Africa: South Sudan Conflict Squeezes Regional Economy
Body_Text:
Kampala — The conflict in South Sudan is sending ripples across the regional economy. In Uganda, one of South Sudan's biggest trading partners, tens of thousands of merchants are feeling the loss of cross-border trade.
In late December, Dan Ssesanga was herded into a Ugandan military truck and, with gunfire ringing in his ears, given safe passage out of South Sudan's capital, Juba. But Ssesanga sells eggs, and his merchandise stayed behind.

Original_Search_Title: Surgeon-General - Health Workers Threaten Legal Action
Headline: Nigeria: Surgeon-General - Health Workers Threaten Legal Action
Body_Text:
Health workers in the nation's tertiary health institutions have threatened to employ all options available to them, including legal action, to thwart President Goodluck Jonathan's decision to appoint a surgeon-general of the federation.
The leaders of the health workers, under the aegis of the Assembly of Health Workers and Joint Health Workers Union (JOHESU), disclosed this yesterday when they paid a courtesy visit to the corporate headquarters of LEADERSHIP Newspapers Ltd in Abuja.

Original_Search_Title: Police Arraign Street Cleaner for Assaulting Seven-Year-Old Banana Hawker
Headline: Nigeria: Police Arraign Street Cleaner for Assaulting Seven-Year-Old Banana Hawker
Body_Text:
The police on Monday arraigned a street cleaner, Dauda Amos, 30, in a Grade 1 Area Court in Mararaba, Nasarawa State, for assaulting a seven-year-old banana hawker.
Amos is being charged with criminal force with intent to outrage modesty under Section 168 of the Penal Code.

Original_Search_Title: Kilimanjaro to Revamp
Headline: Tanzania: Kilimanjaro to Revamp
Body_Text:
Dar es Salaam — Kilimanjaro region is planning to build a special commercial and industrial town at a Lokolova area in a bid to boost the value of its products.
This was revealed last week by the Kilimanjaro Regional Commissioner, Mr. Leonidas Gama.

Original_Search_Title: Rice Prices Decrease By Rwf100
Headline: Rwanda: Rice Prices Decrease By Rwf100
Body_Text:
Prices of rice have reduced by Rwf100 in markets and retail shops across the City of Kigali, a mini-survey conducted by Business Times indicates.
A kilogramme of imported rice is now Rwf900 in Kimironko, Kicukiro and Remera markets compared to from Rwf1,000 last week. That of locally-produced rice goes for Rwf650 compared to Rwf700.

Original_Search_Title: UP Heads for Vacuum - Snowe
Headline: Liberia: UP Heads for Vacuum - Snowe
Body_Text:
The governing Unity Party may face a serious vacuum in 2017, as President Ellen Johnson-Sirleaf steps down, a lawmaker predicts here.
Montserrado County District #7 Representative Edwin Melvin Snowe, Jr. claims there is no discussion within the corridors of power to create the way for a successor after Madam Sirleaf.

Original_Search_Title: Analysis - Never Forget Jan Rivombo
Headline: South Africa: Analysis - Never Forget Jan Rivombo
Body_Text:
This month, Parliament will start deliberating whether Robert McBride is the right man to lead the country's police watchdog Ipid. The murder of 20-year-old fruit trader, Foster Jan Rivombo, allegedly by Tshwane Metro Police officers, should serve as an urgent and haunting reminder of what's at stake.
Foster Jan Rivombo sold apples and bananas to earn a living. He had a three-month-old son and an older brother - also a street trader - whom cared for him deeply. Jan Rivombo, barely out of his teens, was apparently a quiet young man who didn't smoke, drink or cause any trouble.

Original_Search_Title: The Po-Lemi-Cs of the Red Door
Headline: Nigeria: The Po-Lemi-Cs of the Red Door
Body_Text:
Agate slid open into an enclosure floored with interlocking blocks. No, it wasn't that "Open Sesame" kind of opening. A uniformed security guard had indeed opened it. He just - albeit momentarily - happened not to be the main attraction. Dutifully, he indicated a cuboid two-storey building's entrance door to the enquirer. Bola Ashiru, who runs the place, was yet to arrive, he informed the latter. The manager would attend to his needs. Enter an exhilarating white interior. From behind a counter, two ladies peered curiously at the late morning visitor. Would he wish to sit down somewhere to wait for the gallery's owner?
The visitor opted to tour the space and see the ongoing exhibition. On since Sunday, December 22, this exhibition ends on Wednesday, January 15. Titled Po-Lemi-Cs, it "tracks the journey of Lemi Ghariokwu - a legendary artist and a design genius over a 40-year period," according to the catalogue. The visitor's instincts guided him to a part of the split-level ground floor area which juts out towards the fence. Here, where the journey starts, an ancient gramophone right out of the recesses of memory rests on a pedestal beside album covers of old vinyl LP records. Complementing this display on the walls of this section are displayed the album cover designs of Fela Anikulapo-Kuti's classic hits. The artist, in the catalogue, narrates how in 1974 he had earned the late Afro-beat legend's "trust and friendship". "It actually started with a drawing of Fela I had in my portfolio prior to my chance meeting with 'Tunde Harrison, that showed the musician dancing on a mishmash of mud and rubbish," goes the account. From designing the Alagbon Close's album cover in 1974, Lemi soon found himself frenetically designing other album covers for the legendary musician. One came on the heels of each other... The following year, there were Kalakuta Show and Monkey Banana. Then he designed the covers of Yellow Fever, Ikoyi Blindness, Zombie and Upside Down in 1976. In 1977, he did Fear Not for Man and Johnny Just Drop (JJD). Other Fela albums were: Sorrow, Tears & Blood (1978), Open & Close (Remix) (1986), Chop & Quench (1986) Beasts of No Nation (1988) and Confusion Break Bone (1990).

Original_Search_Title: 2014-Gazing Into the Crystal Ball
Headline: Nigeria: 2014-Gazing Into the Crystal Ball
Body_Text:
It is a mere 12 days into 2014, an appropriate time to do some star gazing or crystal ball reading to see what is in store for Nigeria in the year. Now, I make no personal claims to any ability to knowing or reading the future, I am no fortune teller; neither am I clairvoyance or prophetic, so the following observations are just deductions, extrapolations, guesses and suppositions. So here we go:-
Democracy--Nigeria's democracy has subsisted for all of 14 years now. O yes, party political campaigns and voting had been going on all these 14 unbroken years, never mind that the electoral process has been bedevilled by all manner of regrettable shortcomings as happened in Anambra state recently which make the whole thing look like a burlesques of the real thing. The only difference is that sometimes these shortcomings can occasion deadly serious results and tragic outcomes such as when people die and get maimed.

Original_Search_Title: Where Is TFF Leading Us To?
Headline: Tanzania: Where Is TFF Leading Us To?
Body_Text:
THE other day as I was enjoying a cup of coffee at a famous 'kijiwe' (social meeting place) at Kariakoo in Dar es Salaam, I heard someone talking aloud about the Balimi Football Federation.
Then another person intercepted and said it was actually the Bukoba Football Federation, while another said it was Banana (the staple food of the people of Bukoba) Football Federation.

Original_Search_Title: Three Killed in Mbeya
Headline: Tanzania: Three Killed in Mbeya
Body_Text:
THREE people died in Mbeya Region including a fifty five year old woman identified as Magreth Ijumba, who died on the spot after she was attacked by unidentified people with a heavy object on Thursday morning.
Mbeya Regional Police Commander (RPC), Ahmedi Msangi said that the incident occurred at around 06:00hrs when she was resting at home at Ilenge village in the region.

Original_Search_Title: Judge Clears Former MP Over 'Gay' Mugabe Comment
Headline: Zimbabwe: Judge Clears Former MP Over 'Gay' Mugabe Comment
Body_Text:
A magistrate in Chimanimani on Tuesday freed a former MDC-T legislator, who in 2011 commented that the notoriously homophobic Robert Mugabe was in a homosexual relationship.
Lynette Karenyi, the former Chimanimani West MP was due to stand trial for 'undermining' the authority of the President, after being charged under Zimbabwe's controversial insult laws

Original_Search_Title: Sure Bet - Pay Back Time for Arsenal At Villa Park
Headline: Uganda: Sure Bet - Pay Back Time for Arsenal At Villa Park
Body_Text:
Aston Villa humiliated Arsenal on the opening weekend of this season.
As they meet again at Villa Park, Arsene Wenger and his charges will have one thing in mind, to set the record straight...

Original_Search_Title: Mugabe Gay Jibe Ex-Legislator Freed
Headline: Zimbabwe: Mugabe Gay Jibe Ex-Legislator Freed
Body_Text:
A FORMER lawmaker has been set free on charges of allegedly insulting or undermining the authority of President Robert Mugabe after State prosecutors withdrew the case.
Lynette Karenyi, a former MDC-T legislator for Chimanimani West who was due to stand trial on Tuesday.

Original_Search_Title: Glimpses of Eden At Bosun Oredola and Yinka Oshai's Wedding
Headline: Nigeria: Glimpses of Eden At Bosun Oredola and Yinka Oshai's Wedding
Body_Text:
Top businessman, Bosun Oredola and his beautiful wife Yinka Oredola beamed majestically, as they say "I do". Two simple vows and a grand matchless love - which makes them one of the happiest couples right now. A glance behind the glitter. A dreamy, colourful assemblage of family and friends. It was indeed a grand day for the famous couple who dug into the depth of their vast social contacts to make their day as remarkable and grand as it could ever be. Both the bride and groom joyously beamed with smiles while the audience marvelled and rejoiced with them and wishing for their kind of life. The joy of the newlywed couple knew no bounds as they swooned with joy and excitement on the dance floor.
The pair waltzed across the reception hall like two royal scions at the threshold of their dreams. Every detail about their wedding was undeniably gorgeous. The rich and enviable social contacts of Bosun Oredola, amongst other things, was fully displayed at the occasion. The reception took place at the prestigious Oriental Hotels in Lekki, Lagos where there was a surplus of the best floral arrangement, food and champagnes money could buy. The very rich and extremely private groom, Bosun Oredola is into Oil and Gas, Defence and Real Estate. He lives in the prestigious Banana Island Ikoyi Lagos where he owns some luxurious blocks of apartments. To make his new wife Yinka live like his true Queen, Bosun is leaving no stone unturned as he relocates his new wife and family into his latest acquisition, a luxurious and opulent purposely designed mansion also in Banana Island, Ikoyi Lagos.

Original_Search_Title: Open Letter to a New Generation
Headline: Nigeria: Open Letter to a New Generation
Body_Text:
The thing about age is, it is catching. It's like a hysterical jester lying in wait for the fool. I want to tell you about Mrs Okoro. Before l turned nine, school was a vaguely irritating distraction from the pursuit of happiness in play and adventure. Every school day, I'd wear my red checked dress and burgundy beret uniform and passively submit to school. l was not a rebellious child. I was a bored child who daydreamed through classes until lunch when the school served asaro and chicken with bananas and ground nuts as snacks. That was until l got to Mrs Okoro's class.
Mrs Okoro made letters become words, words which became stories, stories which became my life. I loved her dearly, perhaps it was transference as l'd recently lost my mother but at nine, l started going to school because she was there. One day walking out the gates after school, l saw Mrs Okoro getting into a bus ahead of me so l ran across the road to get into the same bus. I didn't bother checking for traffic. The next thing l remember is thinking heaven looked rather like Akoka road. I had been hit by a car and was staring up at the concerned faces of Mrs Okoro and others. The driver was distraught; he was a student at Unilag and in the moment before pain cut through my adrenalin, l remember being happy l had been hit by a grand university student not some infernal danfo bus driver.

Original_Search_Title: Wolokoso - Restaurant Bill Angers Minister
Headline: Uganda: Wolokoso - Restaurant Bill Angers Minister
Body_Text:
Anyone who thought that ministers and NRM cadres 'are in the thing' and therefore splash the cash with abandon may have to think again.
At least not with the rising commodity prices in Kampala. For as prices rise and some businesspeople become hungrier, there is a temptation to target big shots for making a quick buck. One person they will find with eyes wide open is Vincent Nyanzi, the state minister in the office of the vice-president.

Original_Search_Title: How to Detox After Food Binge
Headline: Rwanda: How to Detox After Food Binge
Body_Text:
The festive season comes with merry-making and too much food to eat. Some of the foods, however, may cause stomach discomfort. Here are common conditions people experience and how they can be managed.
Stomach gas

Original_Search_Title: Acidity in the Mouth Spoils the Teeth
Headline: Rwanda: Acidity in the Mouth Spoils the Teeth
Body_Text:
Mouth acidity, caused by sugary foods, fizzy drinks and other acidic food items, can damage the teeth by softening their roots, a study by leading oral hygiene brand Pepsodent said.
"Acidity is not just something that occurs in the stomach during digestion; it begins in the mouth and causes the teeth to soften. Even a healthy diet can cause acid formation in the mouth and help the growth of harmful bacteria," said the report.

Original_Search_Title: Happy New Cows and Rice
Headline: Nigeria: Happy New Cows and Rice
Body_Text:
Fellow Nigerians, let's start the year on this hilarious note before we dovetail again into serious issues. After-all, this season is the period we indulge in wild fantasies and live under the grand delusion that in this New Year things would change for the better this time around. More often than not, we discover at the end of the year that not much has changed and indeed, hopes evaporate and vamoose without any warning. In reality, what's the difference between 2013 and 2014? The answer simply put is: you slept in one year and woke up in the other. No more.
But the world has learnt to celebrate one powerful calendar date even if a few countries and religions still struggle to keep theirs alive. Capitalism is in full bloom and ruling the world in unlimited territories. I don't know if you watched CNN as the New Year meandered and navigated its way from one continent to the other and a geographical location to another. If you did, you would have seen, and possibly witnessed, some spectacular displays, the type never paraded before. You would have been stunned, like me, about the advancement of science and technology in these interesting times. You would probably have marvelled, and seriously wondered, if indeed we live on the same planet or we are the original inhabitants of the Planet of Apes.

Original_Search_Title: With Its New Ruling Elite Somalia Has Lost Destiny
Headline: Somalia: With Its New Ruling Elite Somalia Has Lost Destiny
Body_Text:
Somalia's unending conflict may finally be waning. Analysts and internationalists are singing odes to a "prosperous Somalia at peace with itself and the world".
The neighbouring countries, IGAD and the United Nations are pleased that they have managed to control the discourse on Somalia. The various interests can now create Somalia in their own image.

Original_Search_Title: Food Prices Drop in Northern Province
Headline: Rwanda: Food Prices Drop in Northern Province
Body_Text:
Residents in the Northern Province, celebrated the festive season with a sigh of relief following a reduction in food prices compared to the previous year.
For instance a kilogramme of Irish Potatoes cost between Rwf100 and Rwf120 in Musanze Market and other markets in the Province, down from Rwf170.

Original_Search_Title: Sure Bet - North London Derby Tips Scale in Fa Cup
Headline: Uganda: Sure Bet - North London Derby Tips Scale in Fa Cup
Body_Text:
For the second time this season, Arsenal and Tottenham meet again in a pulsating encounter.
But this time, it is a clash of major proportions, as there are no second chances......it is the knock-out FA Cup.

Original_Search_Title: The Making of a Ugandan Cardinal
Headline: Uganda: The Making of a Ugandan Cardinal
Body_Text:
This month, it is 56 years since a young man named Emmanuel was ordained a Catholic priest in Rome. Now Emmanuel Cardinal Wamala, the retired archbishop of Kampala archdiocese, told his life story to Simon Kasyate, who presents Capital radio's Desert Island Discs programme every Sunday evening.
Let us start at the beginning of the man who became Emmanuel Cardinal Wamala...

Original_Search_Title: Food Prices Stable in Kigali
Headline: Rwanda: Food Prices Stable in Kigali
Body_Text:
Prices of most foodstuffs and other commodities are stable in markets and shops in the City of Kigali despite the soaring demand during the festive season.
A kilogramme of beef costs Rwf2,300 a kilo, fish is unchanged at between Rwf2,500 and Rwf3,000 a kilogramme in Nyarugenge and Kimironko markets, respectively, while chicken goes for Rwf10,000 in Remera and Nyamirambo markets.

Original_Search_Title: Lake Kivu's Newest Gate-Away
Headline: Rwanda: Lake Kivu's Newest Gate-Away
Body_Text:
The festive season is on, and to some, it calls for bigger spending and yes ... there is also that peculiar craving for bigger space. After all, it is the time to sit back, throw one's legs up in the air, relax, and generally unwind.
If you subscribe to this school of thought, just tag along with HotSpots this week, as we unveil one of the newest gems on Lake Kivu; the Emeraude Kivu Resort. Located right on the shores of Lake Kivu, in Cyangugu, Rusizi District, the resort is just over five months in business, and is, without a doubt, the only such facility in Cyangugu area.

Original_Search_Title: Pastor Sentenced to Life for Incest
Headline: Kenya: Pastor Sentenced to Life for Incest
Body_Text:
A pastor with the Akorino church has been handed a life sentence by a Gatundu court for incest. Samuel Kiambuthi was found guilty by Gatundu resident magistrate Daniel Ndungi. He defiled his 11-year-old niece at Mutaara village in Gatundu subcounty, Kiambu county.
The court heard that on May 22, 2012, Kiambuthi sexually assaulted the standard two pupil. He faced another charge of touching the girl indecently. Prosecutor Kunga Muli told the court that on May 23, 2012, the pupil's teacher noted that something was wrong with the girl. After interrogation, she narrated to him what had happened the previous day.

Original_Search_Title: 25 Percent of Miscarriages Could Be Prevented
Headline: Ethiopia: 25 Percent of Miscarriages Could Be Prevented
Body_Text:
Having a miscarriage is often a painful experience for couples to overcome, both physically and mentally.
Overall, about 12 to 15 percent of clinically recognized pregnancies end in miscarriage - with the occurrence increasing with maternal age.

Original_Search_Title: 100 Massacred in Fresh Borno Attacks
Headline: Nigeria: 100 Massacred in Fresh Borno Attacks
Body_Text:
No fewer than 100 people were, weekend, killed in separate attacks in Baga and Izge villages of Kukawa and Gwoza local government areas of Borno State, by Boko Haram insurgents, who also reportedly wounded several others and destroyed property worth millions of Naira. This came as President Goodluck Jonathan said in Lagos, yesterday, that the security challenges facing the country would have been worse without prayers from religious groups across the country.
It has also emerged that the Federal Government may be under pressure to open fresh negotiations with the terrorist group.

Original_Search_Title: Wolokoso - What Happened to the Cops' Soda Allowance?
Headline: Uganda: Wolokoso - What Happened to the Cops' Soda Allowance?
Body_Text:
When Ugandan police officers were invited to Parliament to mount a guard of honour for President Museveni at the opening ceremony of the 4th meeting of the 2nd EALA session, they were promised an allowance of Shs 7,000 each.
Upon arrival at Parliament, they were served a bottle of soda each, which raised their morale. They marched with a lot of vigour, as if in anticipation of what was coming ahead.

Original_Search_Title: The Burden of Witchcraft in African Villages
Headline: Kenya: The Burden of Witchcraft in African Villages
Body_Text:
"'I have smelled a rat', Gwako firmly made his announcement in his croaked voice before a gathered crowd of villagers outside the house of Senta's parents.
'Senta will be alright, but the evil and ruthless spirits have half-broken the eye of Mayenga as I speak.'" This truncate is lifted from Jaspher Rori's new novel, The Eye of Mayenga, 2013 (Nsemia Publishers).

Original_Search_Title: Surgeon General - Health Workers Threaten Legal Action
Headline: Nigeria: Surgeon General - Health Workers Threaten Legal Action
Body_Text:
Following the acceptance of the post of a surgeon general in principle by President Goodluck Jonathan, health workers in the nation's tertiary health institutions, have threatened to exploit all options available to them, including legal action to thwart the decision.
The team leaders of the health workers under the aegis of the Assembly of Health Workers and Joint Health Workers Union (JOHESU), disclosed this on Wednesday when they paid a courtesy visit to the corporate headquarters of LEADERSHIP NEWSPAPERS LIMITED in Abuja.

Original_Search_Title: President Museveni's New Year Message
Headline: Uganda: President Museveni's New Year Message
Body_Text:
Fellow Ugandans,
I extend greetings to all of you.
